## lattice-system

A Lean 4 + Mathlib formalization project targeting general lattice models.
This project subsumes and generalizes the earlier
[ising-model](https://github.com/phasetr/ising-model) project, progressively
covering classical spin systems, quantum spin systems, Hubbard, BCS,
CAR algebras, and eventually lattice QCD.

## Design axis: graphs, not lattices

Despite the name, the **primary combinatorial abstraction in this
library is a graph `(╬δ, E_╬δ)`** έΑΦ finite for finite-volume work and
infinite for the thermodynamic-limit / algebraic-formulation work
that is a major long-term goal έΑΦ not "a lattice". Concrete lattices
(the 1D chain, square / cubic grids, infinite chains, έΕν^d, έΑο) appear
only as specific instances such as `SimpleGraph.pathGraph`,
`SimpleGraph.cycleGraph`, products of these, or their infinite
analogues. This convention follows the standard mathematical-physics
literature on many-body systems on graphs (Lieb's theorem on
bipartite lattices, the MarshallέΑΥLiebέΑΥMattis theorem, Miyao 2021
┬π3, έΑο) and aligns the project with mathlib's `SimpleGraph`
foundations.

Finite-volume work uses `╬δ : Type*` together with `[Fintype ╬δ]`
when needed (e.g. for traces, partition functions, finite sums of
local terms); infinite-volume work drops the `Fintype` assumption
and uses graphs over types like `έΕν` or `έΕν^d` instead.

The bridge from a `SimpleGraph` to the pairwise coupling
`J : ╬δ έΗΤ ╬δ έΗΤ έΕΓ` consumed by `heisenbergHamiltonian` (and similar
operators) lives in `LatticeSystem.Lattice.Graph` (`couplingOf`).
Existing chain coupling families (`openChainCoupling`,
`periodicChainCoupling`) are characterised as
`couplingOf (pathGraph _) _` and `couplingOf (cycleGraph _) _`
respectively.

## Scope

| Area | Stage | Typical references |
|---|---|---|
| Classical spin systems | Inherited from ising-model | Friedli-Velenik, Glimm-Jaffe |
| Quantum spin systems | Current focus | Tasaki, Nielsen-Chuang (cross-check) |
| Hubbard / BCS | Medium term | Tasaki 1998, Bru-Pedra |
| CAR-algebraic formulation | Medium-long term | Araki-Moriya, Bru-Pedra |
| Thermodynamic limit (infinite graphs) | Long term, **major project goal** | Simon, Friedli-Velenik, Bratteli-Robinson |
| Lattice QCD | Longest term | Aarts, Davies |

## Refactoring conventions and review criteria

A **single-source-of-truth document** for refactoring conventions
applied as the review checklist on every pull request:
[Refactoring conventions and review criteria](refactoring-conventions.html).
Topics: test methods (decide / bridge / small-exhaustive / shim /
`#guard_msgs`), module-split criteria, generic / dedup conventions,
deprecation window policy, naming / docstring rules, linter
exceptions, public-doc synchronisation.

The companion page [Deprecation window](deprecations.html) tracks
currently-deprecated public declarations, the `since` date,
recommended replacement, and earliest-removal window.

anisotropic sector matrix, structural irreducibility, and CollatzέΑΥWielandt PF/minimum identification now provide the balanced-sector `finrank έΕΓ έΚν 1` input at the spin-1/2 target point and in the general spin-S target sector; the PF-free target wrappers no longer take an explicit `h_balanced_sector_pf` callback. **Strict-gap, SU(2)-uniqueness, and MLM endpoint wrappers** now replace the explicit balanced/full equality input by the standard strict gap over all non-balanced non-empty magnetization sectors, derive that strict gap from SU(2)-endpoint global uniqueness, and then construct the SU(2) uniqueness input from the general Theorem 2.3 MLM/Casimir/PF endpoint. **D-boundary extension**: the spin-1/2 parity-block route has a dedicated `D έΚξ 0` version because single-ion `┬▒2` moves are impossible for `Fin (1+1)`. The general spin-S route now also has a bond-only parity reachability replacement for the single-ion branch, giving general `D έΚξ 0` parity-block irreducibility and the MLM/Casimir target wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_D_nonneg_general`. **╬╗=1 boundary**: at spin `1/2`, `(S^3)^2 = (1/4)I`, so `singleIonAnisotropyS D 1` is a scalar shift and `anisotropicHeisenbergS J 1 D 1` reduces to the Heisenberg Hamiltonian plus `D|╬δ|/4`; the SU(2) Theorem 2.3/MLM uniqueness transfers to target uniqueness and zero magnetization at `╬╗ = 1`. For general spin-S, the SU(2)-point wrappers cover `╬╗ = 1`, `D = 0`, and for `2 έΚν N` the ion-only parity reachability route gives the `╬╗ = 1`, `D > 0` endpoint wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_lambda_one_D_pos_general`. **Case (ii) bridges**: the case-(ii) path-region lemmas show the deformation path stays in `╬╗ έΚξ 1`, `D έΚν 0`; the first case-(ii) target wrappers reuse the balanced-sector PF bridge while leaving balanced-sector/full-ground equality plus full `finrank <= 2` explicit; the strict-gap wrappers replace the direct balanced-sector/full-ground equality input by the strict sector gap; the no-full-finrank wrappers then use sector projection and Hermitian below-min exclusion to remove the full `finrank <= 2` input once strict gap is supplied; the crossing-callback bridge compresses the remaining strict-gap derivation to a single target crossing contradiction callback; the path-callback bridge reduces that target callback to a contradiction for crossings along the case-(ii) path; the crossing-set bridge reduces the path callback to contradiction for non-empty `perMCrossingSet M έΙσ Icc 0 1`; the first-crossing bridge reduces crossing-set non-emptiness to contradiction at `sInf (perMCrossingSet M έΙσ Icc 0 1)`; the argmin-first-crossing bridge reduces the target contradiction to the sector whose first crossing minimises those `sInf` values; the first-crossing finrank bridge proves that argmin callback from a `finrank <= 2` bound at the selected first crossing; the path-global finrank bridge moves this to every `t έΙΙ Icc 0 1`; the block-path finrank bridge reduces it to full-ground-energy parity-block submatrix simplicity; and the block-PF/min bridge reduces that to pathwise bare parity-block PF simplicity plus PF/min identification; and the parity-gauge sign layer introduces the case-(ii) block gauge needed to flip exactly the ┬▒2 parity-bond and single-ion moves; and the local sign layer proves strict shifted-entry positivity for transverse, parity-bond, and single-ion elementary moves; and the block reachability, block non-negativity, step-support, raw support-classification, and boundary move-set bridges reduce parity-block irreducibility to conditional irreducibility under the bipartite support-zero assumption on `J`. | The public general spin-S `2 έΚν N` parameter-region wrappers `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` and `anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf_general` package the general spin-S surface. The spin-`1/2` wrappers `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_finrank_le_one_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` and `spinHalf_anisotropicHeisenbergS_tasaki24_target_zero_magnetization_of_MLM_casimir_ladder_t23_pf` now package the exact `N = 1` surface: `-1 < ╬╗ < 1`, `D έΚξ 0`; `╬╗ = 1`, using the scalar-shift boundary; and strict case (ii) `1 < ╬╗`, `D έΚν 0`. |

**number conservation (`TasakiFlatBandNumberConservation.lean`)**: `[─ν,N╠Γ]=0` (`flatBandHamiltonian_commute_fermionTotalNumber`; `b╠Γ` lowers / `b╠ΓέΑι` raises `N╠Γ` by one), so ground states split into fixed-`N` sectors; and any ground state lies in the Hubbard hard-core subspace (`flatBand_groundState_mem_hardcoreSubspace`); **uniqueness framework subspaces (`TasakiFlatBandSubspaces.lean`)**: `flatBandAlphaFockSubmodule` (span of ╬▒-Slater states, contains `|╬ο╬▒έθσ`) and `flatBandBKernelSubmodule = έρΖ_{u,╧Δ} ker b╠Γ_{u,╧Δ}` (contains every ground state, from 11.3.11) έΑΦ Tasaki's uniqueness is the inclusion `BKernel έΛΗ ╬▒Fock` + symmetric/maximal-spin classification; **rotated-basis Fock-spanning infrastructure toward it (`TasakiFlatBandModeCreation.lean`, `TasakiFlatBandModeMonomial.lean`, `Math/ListProdMulVec.lean`, Issue #4346)**: the single-particle-mode creation/annihilation maps `─ΙέΑι_╧Δ(w)`/`─Ι_╧Δ(w)` (`flatBandModeCreation`/`flatBandModeAnnihilation`, with ├λέΑι/b╠ΓέΑι/├λ/b╠Γ as values at ╬▒/╬▓), the operator-level single-particle change of basis (`flatBandModeCreation_eq_repr_sum`), the generic single-particle CAR `{─Ι_╧Δ(w),─ΙέΑι_╧Ε(w')}=(έΙΣ_x w(x)w'(x))╬┤_╧Δ╧Ε┬╖1` (`flatBandMode_annihilation_creation_anticomm`, giving the ╬▓-Gram and `{b╠Γ,├λέΑι}=0`), and `flatBandModeFockSubmodule_eq_top` έΑΦ the rotated-basis Fock monomials `(έΙΠ ─ΙέΑι_╧Δ(basis i))|vacέθσ` span the whole space (every `basisVec c` is an ordered site-creation product on the vacuum, and the span is invariant under each site creation), reindexed by occupation configs (card `2^(4K+4)=finrank`) into the **rotated occupation basis** `flatBandOccBasis`; **the hard inclusion `BKernel έΛΗ AlphaFock` is now PROVED** (`flatBandBKernelSubmodule_le_alphaFockSubmodule`, `TasakiFlatBandUniqueness.lean`, axiom-clean): the ╬▓-Gram is invertible (PosDef, `flatBandBetaGram`), its inverse gives dual annihilators `d_{u,╧Δ}=έΙΣ_v(GέΒ╗┬╣)_{uv}b╠Γ_{v,╧Δ}` with `{d,b╠ΓέΑι}=╬┤`, `{d,├λέΑι}=0`, `d|vacέθσ=0`; the projector `b╠ΓέΑι_{u,╧Δ}d_{u,╧Δ}` kills a b╠Γ-kernel vector (since `d v=0`), forcing every ╬▓-occupied occupation-basis coordinate to vanish, so the vector is a combination of ╬▓-free occ monomials (= ╬▒-Slater states) έΙΙ ╬▒-Fock; with the easy inclusion, `BKernel = AlphaFock`; **dimension-route reduction of the uniqueness `έΚν` half (`TasakiFlatBandClassification.lean`, Issue #4346)**: `finrank(multiplet)=K+2`, `[┼ε^z_tot,─ν_flat]=0` (`fermionTotalSpinZ_commute_flatBandHamiltonian`, from `[┼ε^┬▒,─ν]=0` + su(2) `┼εέΒ║┼εέΒ╗έΙΤ┼εέΒ╗┼εέΒ║=2┼ε^z`), `┼ε^z` preserves the ground subspace, the finite `┼ε^z`-weight decomposition `G=έρΗ_{a:Fin(K+2)} GέΛΥeigenspace(┼ε^z, aέΙΤ(K+1)/2)` (`flatBandHalfFilledGroundSubmodule_eq_iSup_weight`, off-weight blocks `έΛξ` since `N╠Γ=K+1` fixes the half-integer weights), and the capstone `flatBand_groundSubmodule_eq_multipletSpan_of_blocks` έΑΦ IF each `┼ε^z`-weight block of `G` is `έΚν1`-dimensional THEN `G=multiplet` (via `Math/EigenspaceWeightFinrank.finrank_le_of_weight_blocks` + `Submodule.eq_of_le_of_finrank_le`, using the proven `multiplet έΚν G`); the residual `finrank(block)έΚν1` (symmetric/maximal-spin core) and removal of the axiom are subsequent steps; **easy inclusion (`TasakiFlatBandAlphaFockKernel.lean`)**: `╬▒Fock έΚν BKernel` (`flatBandAlphaFockSubmodule_le_BKernelSubmodule`) έΑΦ every ╬▒-Slater state is annihilated by all `b╠Γ` via the 11.3.7 anticommutation + move-through; the hard reverse `BKernel έΛΗ ╬▒Fock` needs the non-orthogonal-basis Fock factorisation, deferred; **Theorem 11.11 CAPSTONE (`TasakiFlatBandTheorem11_11.lean`)**: `flatBand_theorem_11_11_groundSubmodule_eq_multipletSpan` έΑΦ the half-filled (`N_e=K+1`) zero-energy ground subspace `ker ─ν έΛΥ eigenspace(N╠Γ,K+1)` equals the ferromagnetic multiplet span (`2S_max+1`-dim, maximal spin), + maximal-spin corollary; `έΚξ` (existence) fully proven, `έΚν` (uniqueness) uses a **single documented `axiom`** (Tasaki App. A classification, deferred like Theorem 11.8; existence half is axiom-free); **┬π11.3.2 Mielke's flat-band ferromagnetism (`MielkeHamiltonian.lean`, Issue #4177)**: `mielkeHamiltonian` on a graph (uniform hopping + `2t┬╖N╠Γ` shift + `U`) with Hermiticity, `[─ν,N╠Γ]=0`, SU(2) invariance έΑΦ model setup; (11.3.36)έΑΥ(11.3.39)) έΑΦ the line-graph operator presented as a PSD Gram matrix with diagonal `2t` (edge degree 2) and off-diagonal `t` on shared-vertex edge pairs, kernel `= ker S`; the algebraic core for the rankέΑΥnullity + bipartite zero-mode count (11.3.41) discharging Theorem 11.12, sorry-free; **rank step (PR3)**: `mielke_lineGraph_ker_finrank_eq` έΑΦ for `tέΚξ0`, `dim ker T = |B| έΙΤ rank S` (via mathlib `ker_mulVecLin_conjTranspose_mul_self` giving `ker Sά┤┤S = ker S` + rankέΑΥnullity), the Lemma 11.14 rank form; **zero-mode count + dim (PR4)**: `mielke_conjTranspose_ker_finrank` έΑΦ for `t>0` and a **connected** base, `dim ker(SSά┤┤) = dim ker Sά┤┤ = (Colorable 2 ? 1 : 0)`, proved directly via `ker Sά┤┤` (`(Sά┤┤x)_b = έΙγt(x_u+x_v)`; bipartite έΘΤ 1-dim span of the alternating ┬▒1 colouring vector, non-bipartite έΘΤ trivial since a nonzero kernel vector would 2-colour the graph); and `mielke_lineGraph_ker_finrank_eq_dim` έΑΦ the assembled flat-band dimension `D = |B| έΙΤ (|╬δ╠Δ| έΙΤ bip)` (Theorem 11.12 general-base form; inner subtraction keeps it sound under έΕΧ truncation, = Tasaki's `|B|έΙΤ|╬δ╠Δ|+1` once `|╬δ╠Δ|έΚν|B|`). **`mielke_theorem_11_12` (PR5, capstone)** έΑΦ **now a proved theorem** (formerly the ┬π11.3.2 axiom): transports the flat-band dimension to the `Fin (M+1)` line-graph realisation along the `SimpleGraph.Iso` via `Matrix.rank_submatrix` (rank/kernel-dim invariance under equiv-reindex), with hypothesis `|╬δ╠Δ| έΚν |B|` (base has a cycle έΑΦ exactly where Tasaki's `|B|έΙΤ|╬δ╠Δ|+1` is the true dimension). The Theorem 11.11 occupation-basis machinery, re-developed generically in the basis for instantiation at the spectral eigenbasis of `T`. **Theorem 11.17 PR3 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandEigenbasis.lean`): spectral-eigenbasis annihilation peel** έΑΦ the orthonormal eigenbasis `T.IsHermitian.eigenvectorBasis` transported to a `Module.Basis` of `Fin(M+1)έΗΤέΕΓ` (`eigenbasisAsBasis`), its coordinate orthonormality `╬μ_x conj(e_j(x))e_k(x)=╬┤_{jk}`, the dual CAR `{─Ι_╧Δ(─Υ_j),─ΙέΑι_╧Ε(e_k)}=╬┤_{jk}╬┤_{╧Δ╧Ε}┬╖1`, the smeared-annihilator vacuum kill `─Ι_╧Δ(╧Η)|vacέθσ=0`, and the eigenbasis-annihilation peel `─Ι_╧Δ(─Υ_j)|qsέθσ = ╬μά╡λ (-1)^i┬╖╬┤_{j,(qs[i]).1}╬┤_{╧Δ,(qs[i]).2}┬╖|qsέΙΨiέθσ` (the occupation-detection engine). **Theorem 11.17 PR4 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpanning.lean`): eq. (11.3.46) Fock spanning (hard direction)** έΑΦ the eigenvalue equation for the transported eigenbasis, the ground-state annihilation `─Ι_╧Δ(─Υ_j)╬ο=0` for every nonzero-eigenvalue mode, the eigenmode number operator `n╠Γ_{j,╧Δ}=─ΙέΑι_╧Δ(e_j)─Ι_╧Δ(─Υ_j)` with its creation commutation `[n╠Γ,─ΙέΑι_╧Ε(e_k)]=╬┤_{jk}╬┤_{╧Δ╧Ε}─ΙέΑι_╧Ε(e_k)`, its diagonality in the occupation basis `n╠Γ_{j,╧Δ}occMon(g)=g(j,╧Δ)┬╖occMon(g)` (list induction), the **coefficient vanishing** (ground-state occupation-basis coefficient = 0 on any config occupying a nonzero-eigenvalue mode), and the capstone **`flatBand_groundState_mem_flatFockSpan`**: a flat-band ground state lies in the span of the flat-band-supported (zero-eigenvalue) occupation monomials (`IsFlatSupported`). **Theorem 11.17 PR5 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandFilling.lean`): filling-refined eq. (11.3.46)** έΑΦ the total fermion number operator is diagonal in the occupation basis (`N╠Γ occMon(g)=|occupied(g)|┬╖occMon(g)`, list induction via `[N╠Γ,─ΙέΑι(w)]=─ΙέΑι(w)`), so a `DέΓΑ`-electron ground state has occupation-basis coefficients supported on the `DέΓΑ`-electron configs; combined with the flat-band coefficient vanishing this gives `flatBand_groundState_atFilling_mem_flatFockSpan` (flat-band ground state έΙΙ span of flat-supported occupation monomials with exactly `DέΓΑ` occupied modes). **Theorem 11.17 PR6 (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandMuTransport.lean`): transport of eq. (11.3.46) to the special basis** έΑΦ the coordinate identity `ker T = span{╬╝_z}` (finrank transport via WithLp), the invariance of the `╬╝`-Slater Fock submodule `generalFlatBandFockSubmodule ╬╝` under `─ΙέΑι_╧Δ(w)` for `w έΙΙ span{╬╝_z}`, and the capstone `flatBand_groundState_mem_generalFlatBandFockSubmodule`: a flat-band ground state at filling lies in the `╬╝`-Slater Fock submodule of the special basis (since the flat-supported eigenbasis monomials use only flat eigenvectors, each έΙΙ `ker T = span{╬╝_z}`). **Theorem 11.17 PR7 in progress (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpinConfig.lean`): index-mode number-operator machinery toward eq. (11.3.47)** έΑΦ the site-dual CAR localized on the index set I (`{─Κ_{z,╧Δ},├λέΑι_{╬╝_{z'},╧Ε}}=╬┤_{╧Δ╧Ε}╬┤_{zz'}╬╝_z(z)`), the index-mode number operator `n╠Γ^╬╝_{z,╧Δ}=├λέΑι_{╬╝_z,╧Δ}─Κ_{z,╧Δ}` with its creation commutation, its diagonality in the ╬╝-Slater states (`n╠Γ^╬╝_{z,╧Δ}|qsέθσ=╬╝_z(z)┬╖count┬╖|qsέθσ`, list induction), and the **no double occupancy of index modes** `n╠Γ^╬╝_{z,έΗΣ}n╠Γ^╬╝_{z,έΗΥ}╬ο=0` (operator identity = `├λέΑι├λέΑι┬╖generalCDownUp` + `─Κ_έΗΥ─Κ_έΗΣ╬ο=0`). **Theorem 11.17 PR8 in progress (Issue #4363, `GeneralFlatBandSpinRep.lean`): tight `I`-mode transport toward eq. (11.3.47)** έΑΦ the special basis `{╬╝_z}` extends to a full `Fin(M+1)`-basis `e╬╝` (`exists_extended_special_basis`); an `I`-mode `╬╝`-Slater state equals a mode monomial over `e╬╝` (`generalFlatBandSlaterState_eq_generalModeMonomial`, carrying them into `generalOccBasis e╬╝` for linear independence); the tight `I`-mode `╬╝`-Slater Fock submodule (`generalFlatBandIModeFockSubmodule`) is invariant under index-mode creation, and a flat-band ground state lies in it (`flatBand_groundState_mem_imode`, resolving the `zέΙΚI` subtlety). (11.3.49)): for an edge-swap-invariant D, the D-weighted sum of ─Κ_{xέΗΥ}─Κ_{xέΗΣ}-coordinates at the (a,b)-emptied target vanishes έΑΦ via `cDownUp_canonicalSum_eq_two_terms` collapse + coordinate negation, either a,b connect at x (edge έθ╣ D(╧Δ)=D(╧Δ') cancel) or the ╬╝_a(x)╬╝_b(x) factor vanishes (PR #4446). `cDownUp_canonicalSlaterSum_repr_eq_zero_of_not_twoHoleTarget` (the off-target companion): at a g that is NOT a (DέΓΑ-2)-emptied two-hole config, the D-weighted ─Κ_{xέΗΥ}─Κ_{xέΗΣ}-coordinate sum vanishes έΑΦ each inner rest-Slater coordinate is a Kronecker delta (`generalFlatBandSlaterState_over_I_repr`) at `idxConfigOf` of the twice-erased canonical list (`flatBandSpinConfigList_eraseIdx_eraseIdx`), which g matches for no pair (`hnot`) (PR #4447). `generalCDownUp_mulVec_canonicalSlaterSum_eq_zero_of_edgeSwap_invariant` (interaction-kill main: έΙΑg coordinate dispatch L1/L2 via `generalOccBasis` injectivity, ╧Δ-modified witness for ╧Δa=0/╧Δb=1) + `canonicalSlaterSum_mem_groundSubmodule_of_edgeSwap_invariant`: **an edge-swap-invariant canonical-Slater sum is a ground state** (kinetic per-Slater + interaction kill + N╠Γ=DέΓΑ) έΑΦ the converse of the eq. (11.3.49) characterization, placing per-block weight states into ground (PR #4448). `upCountOn_comp_swap_eq` (in-block transposition preserves a block's up-count) + `generalFlatBand_blockWeightState_mem_groundSubmodule`: the per-block fiber sum W_{p,q} = ╬μ_{upCount A=p, upCount Aά╢ε=q} Slater(extend s) is a ground state, since its indicator coefficient is edge-swap-invariant (basis-graph edges lie within one block, so swaps preserve both block up-counts) (PR #4449). `exists_blockUpCount_config` (a config with prescribed per-block up-counts) + `generalFlatBand_disconnected_finrank_gt`: **┬υconnected έθ╣ finrank ground > DέΓΑ+1** έΑΦ the (|A|+1)(|Aά╢ε|+1) weight states W_{p,q} are linearly independent (at a fiber representative's index config, only its W has a nonzero occupation coordinate, via `idxConfigOf_flatBandSpinConfigList_inj_gen`), so finrank έΚξ (|A|+1)(|Aά╢ε|+1) > DέΓΑ+1; the contradiction with the multiplet's finrank=DέΓΑ+1 (PR #4450). **`generalFlatBand_theorem_11_17` (CAPSTONE, AXIOM DISCHARGED)**: `generalFlatBandFerromagnetic T U έΗΦ generalFlatBandBasisConnected I ╬╝` έΑΦ έΘΡ via `generalFlatBand_connected_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`, έΘΤ via the `┬υconnected έθ╣ finrank > DέΓΑ+1` contrapositive; the ┬π11.3.4 Theorem 11.17 axiom is now a proved theorem depending only on `propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound` (PR #4451). PR3b `TJAllUpGround.lean` then proves `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_allUpState_eq_zero` `─ν_tJ|έΗΣέΑοέΗΣέθσ=0` at half-filling έΑΦ the maximal-spin `|έΗΣέΑοέΗΣέθσ` is a zero-energy eigenstate; PR3c-prep `TJSingletAnnihilation.lean` adds the singlet annihilator `╬Φ_xy=─Κ_{yέΗΥ}─Κ_{xέΗΣ}έΙΤ─Κ_{yέΗΣ}─Κ_{xέΗΥ}` (`╬Φ_xy|έΗΣέΑοέΗΣέθσ=0`), towards the bond identity; PR3c `TJExchangeBondPSD.lean` proves the CAR identity `tJSingletAnnihilation_conjTranspose_mul_self` `╬Φ_xyέΑι╬Φ_xy = n╠Γ_{xέΗΣ}n╠Γ_{yέΗΥ}+n╠Γ_{xέΗΥ}n╠Γ_{yέΗΣ}έΙΤ┼εέΒ║_x┼εέΒ╗_yέΙΤ┼εέΒ╗_x┼εέΒ║_y` (xέΚιy) + cross-site commutes `TJCrossSiteSpinCommute.lean`, the content behind the half-bond identity; PR3d `TJExchangeBondHalf.lean` proves `tJExchangeBond_eq_half_singletNormSq` `n╠Γ_xn╠Γ_y/4έΙΤ┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y=┬╜╬Φ_xyέΑι╬Φ_xy` (xέΚιy) and `tJExchangeBond_posSemidef` (the bond is positive-semidefinite) έΑΦ the per-bond PSD input; PR3e `TJExchangePSD.lean` proves `tJExchange_posSemidef` `(tJExchange N G).PosSemidef` (graph sum of per-bond PSD terms via `Finset.sum_induction`) έΑΦ the operator nonnegativity behind the half-filling ground energy 0; PR3f `TJHalfFillingReduction.lean` extends to the whole sector έΑΦ `tJFillingSector_full` (Ne=N+1 έθ╣ fully occupied), `tJ_kinetic_sandwich_mulVec_eq_zero_of_filling`, and `tJHamiltonian_mulVec_eq_smul_tJExchange_of_filling` (`─ν_tJ v=J┬╖tJExchange v` for hard-core N╠Γ=N+1 v); PR3g `TJAllUpProperties.lean` (`N╠Γ|έΗΣέΑοέΗΣέθσ=(N+1)|έΗΣέΑοέΗΣέθσ`, hardcore, έΚι0) + `TJHalfFillingGroundEnergy.lean` `tJ_groundEnergyAtFilling_eq_zero` (`groundEnergyAtFilling ─ν_tJ (N+1)=0`: all-up at 0, PSD έΘΤ rayleighέΚξ0); axiom-free); PR3h `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyLower.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_ge` (`N+2 έΚν finrank` of the half-filling ground subspace: the all-up state is a highest-weight ground state, so the SU(2) lowering tower yields `N+2` LI ground states `(┼εέΒ╗)^k|έΗΣέΑοέΗΣέθσ`; mirrors #4305; axiom-free); PR3i `TJHalfFillingBondAction.lean` `tJExchangeBond_mulVec_tJConfigOf_full` (the exchange bond is a half spin-swap on the filled sector: `(┬╝ n╠Γ_x n╠Γ_y έΙΤ ┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y)|╬ο_sέθσ=┬╜(|╬ο_sέθσέΙΤ|╬ο_{tJSpinSwap s x y}έθσ)` via the sign-free swap over the 4 spin cases; ground states of `tJExchange` have spin-swap-invariant bond amplitudes έΑΦ toward the degeneracy upper bound; axiom-free); PR3i-2 `TJHalfFillingBondGround.lean` `tJ_ground_bond_mulVec_eq_zero` (a half-filling ground state is annihilated by every adjacent bond: `bond_xy v=0` via the PSD sum `έθρv,tJExchange vέθσ=0` έΘΤ each `έθρv,bond_xy vέθσ=0` έΘΤ `╬Φ_xy v=0`; axiom-free); PR3i-3a `TJHalfFillingAmplitude.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_swap_invariant` (half-filling ground amplitudes are spin-swap invariant: `v(tJConfigOf s)=v(tJConfigOf(tJSpinSwap s x y))` for adjacent bonds, via filling-basis expansion + the bond half-swap action + `bond_xy v=0`; `tJFillingSwap` sector permutation; axiom-free); PR3i-3b `TJHalfFillingUpCount.lean` `tJ_ground_amplitude_eq_of_same_upCount` (half-filling ground amplitudes depend only on the up-count: equal up-counts έθ╣ equal value-counts έθ╣ adjacent-swap reachable [`adjacentSwapReachable_of_same_counts`] έθ╣ amplitudes equal by reachability induction over the per-bond swap invariance; axiom-free); PR3i-3c `TJHalfFillingDegeneracyUpper.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_groundSubmodule_finrank_le` (**upper bound `finrank G έΚν N+2`**: ground states determined by amplitudes constant on the N+2 up-count classes έθ╣ injective `G έΗς (Fin(N+2)έΗΤέΕΓ)`; with the lower bound #4326 έθ╣ `finrank G = N+2`; axiom-free); **PR3-cap `TJHalfFillingMaximalSpin.lean` `tJ_halfFilling_isMaximalSpinMultiplet`** (the half-filling t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin `(N+2)`-fold multiplet: `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule N G (N+1)`, assembling `finrank=N+2` with the all-up SU(2) tower spanning `G`; boundary case of Theorem 11.26's t-J side; axiom-free, no A.17); **PR-unify `TJMaximalSpinUnified.lean` `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`** (the d=1 t-J ground subspace is the maximal-spin multiplet for ALL odd `Ne έΚν K+1`: metallic `Ne<K+1` via `proposition_11_24` + half-filling `Ne=K+1`; the half-filling chain generalized to drop `0<N` so `K=0` is covered; t-J input to Theorem 11.26, rests on A.17 via Prop 11.24); **PR-final: `theorem_11_26` is now a PROVED theorem** (`MetallicFerroModel.lean`, Issue #4314): discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented strong-coupling-equivalence axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`; `#print axioms theorem_11_26` = `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, lemma_11_25]` (A.17's `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` now discharged ┬πA.3.2). Tasaki ┬π11.5.3 metallic-ferromagnetism Theorem 11.26 complete + **`theorem_11_26` (PROVED, Issue #4314)** (d=1, `NeέΚνK+1=|E|` odd (Tasaki's `NέΚνL`) έΘΤ `IsMaximalSpinMultipletSubmodule (3K+2) (groundSubmoduleAtFilling (decHubbardHamiltonian έΑο) Ne) Ne` for large `t,U` έΑΦ ground `S_tot=Ne/2`, `Ne+1`-fold; metallic when `Ne<K+1`); discharged from `lemma_11_25` (documented axiom) + `tJ_isMaximalSpinMultiplet_of_le`, so the model and Theorem 11.26 are proved modulo `lemma_11_25` (A.17 now discharged); Theorem 11.27 still a documented axiom); **generic single-particle-state operators (`SpinfulVectorOperator.lean`)**: `spinfulCreationFromVector`/`spinfulAnnihilationFromVector` (`─Ι_╧Δ(╧Η)=╬μ_x ╧Η(x)─Κ_{x,╧Δ}`), shared helper; axiom-free; **┬π11.5.4 TanakaέΑΥTasaki model + Theorem 11.27 (AXIOMATIZED, `TanakaTasakiModel.lean`, Issue #4198) έΑΦ ┬π11.5/Chapter 11 capstone**: the heavily-decorated lattice `╬δ=E├Ω{1,2,3}έΙςI├Ω{1,2}` (externals triplicated, internals duplicated), d=1 in `Fin(5K+5)` (`ttExtSite`/`ttIntSite`); special states `ttAlpha`/`ttBeta`/`ttDeltaP`/`ttDeltaI` (eqs. (11.5.19)έΑΥ(11.5.22), `├λ` with `1/έΙγ(3+4╬╜┬▓)`) + ops via `spinfulCreationFromVector`; `ttHopping` (`╬μ_{έθρp,qέθσ}(έΙΤs ├λέΑι├λέΙΤt b╠ΓέΑιb╠Γ)+uέΓΒ╬μb╠ΓέΑιb╠Γ+uέΓΓ╬μd╠ΓέΑιd╠Γ`, eq. (11.5.24)) + `ttInteraction` + `ttHamiltonian` (finite); genuine `uέΓΓ,UέΗΣέΙη` limit objects `ttDKernel` (`d╠Γ╬ο=0`, via Theorem A.12) + `ttEffectiveHamiltonian` (`uέΓΓ,U=έΙη` effective hopping); **`axiom theorem_11_27`** (d=1, `uέΓΒ>2(|s|+2|t|)`, `K+1έΚνNeέΚν2(K+1)` (Tasaki `L^dέΚνNέΚν2L^d`) έΘΤ **in the limit** every ground state in `groundSubmoduleAtFilling (ttEffectiveHamiltonian έΑο) Ne έΛΥ ttDKernel` has max spin `S_tot=Ne/2` έΑΦ taken as a genuine limit, not finite thresholds (Tasaki proves it only in the limit, warns finite `uέΓΓ,U` is not expected to work); weaker than the multiplet predicate, Tasaki claims only the spin; metallic when `1<Ne/L<2`); model axiom-free, `theorem_11_27` documented axiom ([63])) |

see the **status & axiomatization policy** note below the Roadmap): **Theorem A.1 (Lie product formula)** `e^{A+B} = lim_N (e^{A/N}e^{B/N})^N` **now proved (axiom-free)** (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/LieProduct.lean`, `lieProductFormula` έΑΦ mathlib has only the commuting case): generic `trotterProductFormula` in a complete normed `έΕζ`-algebra via exponential-series tail bounds (`έΑΨe^XέΙΤ1έΙΤXέΑΨ έΚν έΑΨXέΑΨ┬▓e^{έΑΨXέΑΨ}` etc.) + telescoping power estimate (`έΑΨCέΒ┐έΙΤDέΒ┐έΑΨ έΚν n┬╖M^{nέΙΤ1}┬╖έΑΨCέΙΤDέΑΨ`) + `O(s┬▓)` product comparison + the exact `n`-th-power identity, instantiated for matrices under the scoped operator norm; **Lemmas A.4** (Hermitian `├Γ` is `έΚξ0` iff all eigenvalues `έΚξ0`), **A.5** (`├Γ,B╠ΓέΚξ0 έΘΤ ├Γ+B╠ΓέΚξ0`), **A.6** (`B╠ΓέΑιB╠ΓέΚξ0`, and conversely every `├ΓέΚξ0` is `─Ι┬▓` for a *unique* PSD square root `─Ι=έΙγ├Γ` via `cfc Real.sqrt`, existence+uniqueness) **proved** (axiom-free, `Math/PosSemidef/Basics.lean`). **Theorem A.7** (Weyl monotonicity: Hermitian `├ΓέΚνB╠Γ` έΘΤ `i`-th sorted eigenvalue `├Γ.eigenvaluesέΓΑ i έΚν B╠Γ.eigenvaluesέΓΑ i`) **now proved (axiom-free)** via the CourantέΑΥFischer block/pigeonhole argument (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/CourantFischer.lean`: spectral Rayleigh-sum `reέθρx,Txέθσ=έΙΣ╬╗ά╡λέΑΨέθρbά╡λ,xέθσέΑΨ┬▓` + block bounds + the top-`(i+1)`/bottom-`(cardέΙΤi)` eigenspaces meeting by finrank pigeonhole; `EigenvalueMonotone.lean`). **Corollary A.8** (`├ΓέΚνB╠Γ έΘΤ Tr e^├Γ έΚν Tr e^B╠Γ`, eq A.2.31) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`MatrixAnalysis/TraceExpMonotone.lean`: spectral mapping `Tr e^├Γ = ╬μ e^{a_j}` via `Matrix.exp_conj` + `exp_diagonal` + `trace_mul_cycle`, then A.7 Weyl + `Real.exp` monotone). **Lemmas A.9/A.10** (frustration-free Hamiltonian `─ν=╬μ─ξέ▒╝`, `─ξέ▒╝έΚξ╬╡έ▒╝`: A.9 simultaneous eigenstate έΘΤ ground state at `╬μ╬╡έ▒╝`; A.10 converse) **proved** (axiom-free, `FrustrationFree.lean`). **Lemma A.11** (`├ΓέΚξ0 έΙπ έθρ╬ο|├Γ|╬οέθσ=0 έΘΤ ├Γ╬ο=0`; `├Γ=B╠ΓέΑιB╠Γ έΘΤ B╠Γ╬ο=0`; + angular-momentum corollary `─┤┬▓╬ο=0 έΘΤ ─┤έΒ╜ά╡ΖέΒ╛╬ο=0`) **proved** (axiom-free, `PosSemidef/Kernel.lean`, over the existing `RayleighPosSemidefKernel`). **Theorem A.12** (`─ν_v=─νέΓΑ+vV╠Γ`, `V╠ΓέΚξ0`: `vέΗΣέΙη` finite-energy eigenstate families converging to nonzero `╬ο` satisfy the effective Schr├╢dinger eq `P╠ΓέΓΑ─νέΓΑ╬ο=E╬ο` on `HέΓΑ=ker V╠Γ`) **now proved (axiom-free)** (`EffectiveLimit.lean`, weak-form + Tendsto: the paired eigenvalue relation gives `v┬╖έθρ╬ο_v|V╠Γ|╬ο_vέθσ = E_vέΑΨ╬ο_vέΑΨ┬▓έΙΤέθρ╬ο_v|─νέΓΑ|╬ο_vέθσ`, the converging right side times `vέΒ╗┬╣έΗΤ0` kills the limiting quadratic form, Lemma A.11 kills `V╠Γ╬ο`; pairing with `╧Ι έΙΙ ker V╠Γ` removes the `vV╠Γ` term outright and the limit is the weak equation). **Appendix A.2 complete.** **┬πA.3 angular momentum**: **Lemma A.14** (su(2) ladder) έΑΦ operator identity `─┤έΒ╗─┤έΒ║=─┤┬▓έΙΤ─┤┬│(─┤┬│+1)` (eq A.3.7) + ladder non-vanishing (`έΙΤJέΚνM<J`, `╬οέΚι0` έΘΤ `─┤έΒ║╬οέΚι0`) + raising **membership** (`─┤έΒ║╬οέΙΙH_{J,M+1}`, via `[─┤┬│,─┤έΒ║]=─┤έΒ║` + `[─┤┬▓,─┤έΒ║]=0`) + **lowering** direction (`─┤έΒ║─┤έΒ╗=─┤┬▓έΙΤ─┤┬│(─┤┬│έΙΤ1)` eq A.3.8, `─┤έΒ╗╬οέΚι0` for `έΙΤJ<MέΚνJ`, `─┤έΒ╗╬οέΙΙH_{J,MέΙΤ1}`) **proved** έΑΦ A.14 fully done (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Ladder.lean`). **Lemma A.15 (spin bound, part 1)**: norm identities `έΑΨ─┤┬▒╬οέΑΨ┬▓={J(J+1)έΙΤM(M┬▒1)}έΑΨ╬οέΑΨ┬▓` (eq A.3.9, Hermitian ─┤^╬▒) `angRaise/angLower_normSq` + `angMom_abs_le_J` (`έΙΤJέΚνMέΚνJ`, i.e. `JέΙΤM,J+MέΚξ0`) **proved** (axiom-free). **Lemma A.15 (integrality)** + **Theorem A.13 (`J = n/2`)**: ladder-termination `raiseIter` (iterated `─┤έΒ║`) with `raiseIter_eigenspace`/`raiseIter_ne_zero` gives `JέΙΤM έΙΙ έΕνέΚξ0` (`angMom_sub_mem_nat`); the gauge-reflected `(─┤┬╣,έΙΤ─┤┬▓,έΙΤ─┤┬│)` gives `J+M έΙΙ έΕνέΚξ0`, so `2J=(JέΙΤM)+(J+M)έΙΙέΕνέΚξ0`, i.e. `J=n/2` (`angMom_J_eq_half_nat`) **proved** (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Quantization.lean`). **Theorem A.16 (SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy)**: for SU(2)-invariant `─ν` (`[─ν,─┤ά╡Ζ]=0`), a joint energy eigenstate in `H_{J,M0}` yields nonzero same-energy companions in every `H_{J,JέΙΤk}` (`kέΚν2J`) έΑΦ `ham_su2_multiplet` (energy `E` is έΚξ`(2J+1)`-fold degenerate), proved via raise-to-top + reflected-lowering ladder + `ham_mulVec_raiseIter` (axiom-free, `AngularMomentum/Multiplet.lean`). **Theorem A.17 (spin-0/1-2 sector suffices)**: every energy eigenvalue of an SU(2)-invariant `─ν` has an eigenstate with `─┤┬│=0` or `─┤┬│=1/2` έΑΦ `ham_eigenstate_spin_zero_or_half` (corollary of A.16+A.13; the lone simultaneous-diagonalization step `exists_joint_su2_energy_eigenstate` is **now proved (axiom-free)** via the generic common eigenvector of two commuting Hermitian operators on an invariant subspace (`Math/CommutingHermitianEigenvector.lean`: `exists_common_eigenvector_of_isHermitian_commute`), the Casimir `─┤┬▓` being PSD and commuting with `─┤┬│`/`─ν`, `AngularMomentum/SpinHalfSector.lean`). **Theorem A.18 (PerronέΑΥFrobenius, real symmetric)**: a real symmetric `M` with `(c┬╖1έΙΤM)` irreducible (off-diag `έΚν0` + connectivity) has a nondegenerate lowest eigenvalue with a strictly positive eigenvector έΑΦ `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` (reuses the project's CollatzέΑΥWielandt PF + eigenspace simplicity; the lowest-eigenvalue identification is the variational `|w|`-argument eq A.4.1), proved (axiom-free, `PerronFrobeniusSymmetric.lean`). **Theorems A.19/A.20 (polar + SVD)**: `matrix_polar_decomposition` (`A = W C`, `W` unitary, `C` PSD) and `matrix_singular_value_decomposition` (`A = U D VέΑι`, `U,V` unitary, `D = diagonal d` with `d έΚξ 0`) έΑΦ **now proved (axiom-free)**: SVD built from the spectral theorem of `Aά┤┤A` (`eigenvectorUnitary` `V`, eigenvalues `╬╗_i έΚξ 0`, `d_i = έΙγ╬╗_i`), with the normalised images `u_i = d_iέΒ╗┬╣┬╖A v_i` extended to an orthonormal basis (`exists_orthonormalBasis_extension_of_card_eq`) giving `U`, and `A V = U D` columnwise; polar follows as `W = U Vά┤┤`, `C = V D Vά┤┤` (`MatrixAnalysis/Decomposition.lean`). **Theorems A.21/A.22 (Wigner)**: `wignerAutomorphism_unitary` (linear έΙΩ-automorphism of `M(H)` έθ╣ `╬Υ(├Γ)=├δέΑι├Γ├δ`) + `wignerAutomorphism_antiunitary` (antilinear έθ╣ `├δέΑι├Γ╠Ε├δ`) + `wignerProjection` (rank-1-projection map preserving `Tr[P PέΑ▓]` έθ╣ (anti)unitary `V╠Γ`) έΑΦ documented axioms over `Matrix D D έΕΓ` (`WignerTheorem.lean`, Issue #4224). **Definition A.23 + Theorem A.24 (states / BanachέΑΥAlaoglu)**: `IsState` (weak-έΙΩ-continuous `╧Η : WeakDual έΕΓ A` with `╧Η 1 = 1`, `0 έΚν ╧Η(aέΑιa)`) over an abstract unital C*-algebra + `stateSpace_isCompact` (state space weak-έΙΩ compact, documented axiom) έΑΦ `CStarAlgebra/State.lean`. **Definitions A.25/A.27 + Theorem A.26 (ground states)**: dynamics modelled by a derivation `╬┤=[─ν,┬╖]`; `IsGroundState` (`0 έΚν ╧Κ(aέΑι┬╖╬┤a)`) + `HasNonzeroGap` (`╬│>0`, `╧Κ(aέΑι╬┤a) έΚξ ╬│╧Κ(aέΑιa)` on `╧Κ(a)=0`) + `groundState_variational` (ground state έθ║ `╧Κ(─ν_L)` least over states agreeing outside ╬δ_L, documented axiom) έΑΦ `CStarAlgebra/GroundState.lean`. **Theorem A.28 (GNS construction)**: `gns_construction` έΑΦ every state `╧Β` on a C*-algebra has a GNS triple `(H_╧Β, ╧Α_╧Β, ╬σ_╧Β)` (`έΙΩ`-representation `╧Α : A έΗΤέΜΗέΓΡ[έΕΓ] (HέΗΤL H)` + cyclic `╬σ`) with `╧Β(├Γ)=έθρ╬σ,╧Α(├Γ)╬σέθσ` and `{╧Α(├Γ)╬σ}` dense; documented axiom (mathlib has the GNS machinery), `CStarAlgebra/GNS.lean`. **┬πA.6έΑΥ┬πA.7 (Wigner / states / ground states / GNS) complete έΗΤ Appendix A complete.** Prove-first where mathlib supports, axiomatize-first for the heavy analytic results, strict book order (see the status & axiomatization-policy note below). |

### Appendix A: status and axiomatization policy

Tasaki's **Appendix A is fully formalized in book order (A.1έΑΥA.28)**, and the
entire Tasaki text up to and including Chapter 11 (┬π11.5) plus this appendix is
now covered. The appendix splits into two kinds of items:

- **Proved (axiom-free)** έΑΦ the linear-algebra and angular-momentum core that
  `mathlib` supports directly: **A.1** (the Lie product / Trotter formula, proved
  from scratch in a complete normed `έΕζ`-algebra), **A.12** (the strong-coupling
  effective Hamiltonian limit, by the `vέΒ╗┬╣`-squeeze + kernel-pairing continuity
  argument), **A.4έΑΥA.6**
  (positive-semidefinite basics + unique
  PSD square root), **A.7έΑΥA.8** (Weyl eigenvalue monotonicity via CourantέΑΥFischer,
  and the trace-exp monotonicity through the spectral mapping for `exp`),
  **A.9έΑΥA.11** (frustration-free Hamiltonians, energy-form
  kernels), **A.13έΑΥA.16** (angular-momentum quantization `J = n/2`, the ladder,
  and SU(2)-multiplet degeneracy), **A.17** (spin-0/half sector via the common
  eigenvector of commuting Hermitians), **A.18** (PerronέΑΥFrobenius for a real
  symmetric matrix, via the project's CollatzέΑΥWielandt development and the
  variational `|w|`-argument), and **A.19έΑΥA.20** (polar + singular-value
  decompositions from the spectral theorem).
- **Documented axioms (faithful statements, deferred proofs)** έΑΦ the
  operator-algebraic **A.21έΑΥA.28** (Wigner's
  theorem, states, BanachέΑΥAlaoglu, ground states of infinite systems, the GNS
  construction).

**Value judgment / policy.** The documented axioms above are kept as *faithful,
book-order statements* έΑΦ they record exactly what Tasaki proves έΑΦ but they are
**not active proof targets** of this project. The heavy functional-analytic and
operator-algebraic results (states on the quasi-local C\*-algebra, weak-έΙΩ
compactness, ground states, GNS, Wigner) belong to a dedicated operator-algebra /
functional-analysis development; such a development may well be carried out
**separately** (for instance contributed to `mathlib`), and these axioms simply
**wait for that implementation**. Accordingly the project's policy is to
**axiomatize only the appendix results that Tasaki's formalized main development
actually uses**, to **prove** the remaining ones where `mathlib` provides the
tools, and otherwise to leave a faithful axiom in place rather than invest in a
large bespoke operator-algebra build whose natural home is elsewhere. The
`#print axioms` of every theorem in the repository makes the precise dependency
on these documented axioms auditable.

## Formalized theorems

All items below are formally proved with **zero `sorry`**. Full
mathematical statements and proof sketches are in
[`tex/proof-guide.tex`](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/blob/main/tex/proof-guide.tex).

### Single-site Pauli operators

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬π2.1 eq. (2.1.8), p. 15. Cross-checked with Nielsen-Chuang
┬π2.1.3 Figure 2.2 (pp. 65-66, definitions), Ex. 2.19 (p. 70,
Hermiticity), Ex. 2.41 (p. 78, `(╧Δ^╬▒)┬▓ = I` and anticommutation),
Ex. 2.40 (p. 77, commutator, whence the cyclic products).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `pauliX/Y/Z_isHermitian` | `(╧Δ^╬▒)έΑι = ╧Δ^╬▒` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX/Y/Z_mul_self` | `(╧Δ^╬▒)┬▓ = I` | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_mul_pauliY` etc. | `╧Δ^x ╧Δ^y = i┬╖╧Δ^z` (cyclic) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |
| `pauliX_anticomm_pauliY` etc. | `╧Δ^╬▒ ╧Δ^╬▓ + ╧Δ^╬▓ ╧Δ^╬▒ = 0` (╬▒ έΚι ╬▓) | `Quantum/Pauli.lean` |

|---|---|---|
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3` | `├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕ := cos(╬╕/2) ┬╖ 1 - 2i ┬╖ sin(╬╕/2) ┬╖ ┼ε^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |

| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_unitary` | unitarity `├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕ ┬╖ (├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕)έΑι = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi` | `├δ^(╬▒)_╧Α = -2i ┬╖ ┼ε^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |

| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_conjTranspose` | `(├δ^(╬▒)_╧Α)έΑι = 2i ┬╖ ┼ε^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_pi_mul_spinHalfRot2_pi` (and cyclic) | `├δ^(╬▒)_╧Α ┬╖ ├δ^(╬▓)_╧Α = ├δ^(╬│)_╧Α` (Tasaki (2.1.29), S=1/2) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1/2/3_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | axis invariance and sign flip at ╬╕=╧Α (Tasaki (2.1.15)/(2.1.21)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{2,3,1}` | `(├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕)έΑι ┼ε^(╬▓) ├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕ = cos ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼ε^(╬▓) - sin ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼ε^(╬│)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), even-╬╡ cyclic triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{3,1,2}` | `(├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕)έΑι ┼ε^(╬▓) ├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕ = cos ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼ε^(╬▓) + sin ╬╕ ┬╖ ┼ε^(╬│)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.16), odd-╬╡ triple) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | same-axis invariance `(├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕)έΑι ┼ε^(╬▒) ├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕ = ┼ε^(╬▒)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.15)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot{1,2,3}_half_pi_conj_spinHalfOp_*` | `╧Α/2`-rotation conjugation `(├δ^(╬▒)_{╧Α/2})έΑι ┼ε^(╬▓) ├δ^(╬▒)_{╧Α/2} = -╬╡^{╬▒╬▓╬│} ┼ε^(╬│)` (Tasaki eq. (2.1.22), 6 cases) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_eq_exp` | `├δ^(3)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼ε^(3))` via `Matrix.exp_diagonal` + Euler (Problem 2.1.b, axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | `├δ^(3)_╧Η ├δ^(2)_╬╕ |╧Ι^έΗΣέθσ = e^{-i╧Η/2} cos(╬╕/2) |╧Ι^έΗΣέθσ + e^{i╧Η/2} sin(╬╕/2) |╧Ι^έΗΥέθσ` (coherent state, Problem 2.1.d) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_mul_spinHalfRot2_mulVec_spinHalfDown` | `├δ^(3)_╧Η ├δ^(2)_╬╕ |╧Ι^έΗΥέθσ = -e^{-i╧Η/2} sin(╬╕/2) |╧Ι^έΗΣέθσ + e^{i╧Η/2} cos(╬╕/2) |╧Ι^έΗΥέθσ` (rotation of spin-down, Problem 2.2.c auxiliary) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_half_pi_mul_spinHalfRot2_half_pi_mulVec_spinHalfUp` | specialization at ╬╕ = ╧Η = ╧Α/2 (Problem 2.1.e) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfDotVec` / `spinHalfDotVec_isHermitian` | vector inner product `┼ε ┬╖ v := ╬μ_╬▒ v_╬▒ ┼ε^(╬▒)` and its Hermiticity (cf. (2.1.19)) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot3_commute_spinHalfOp3_smul` | same-axis rotation commutes with `v ┬╖ ┼ε^(3)` (cf. (2.1.20) along axis) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard` / `hadamard_mul_self` | the Hadamard basis-change matrix `W = (1/έΙγ2)┬╖!![1,1;1,-1]` and `W┬╖W = 1` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp1_mul_hadamard` | `W ┬╖ ┼ε^(1) ┬╖ W = ┼ε^(3)` (basis change between ╧Δ^x and ╧Δ^z) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `hadamard_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_hadamard` | `W ┬╖ ┼ε^(3) ┬╖ W = ┼ε^(1)` (inverse basis change) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_hadamard_conj` | `├δ^(1)_╬╕ = W ┬╖ ├δ^(3)_╬╕ ┬╖ W` (axis 1 rotation as Hadamard conjugate of axis 3) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot1_eq_exp` | `├δ^(1)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼ε^(1))` via Hadamard conjugation + `Matrix.exp_conj` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 1) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `yDiag` / `yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_yDiagAdj` / `yDiag_mul_spinHalfOp3_mul_yDiagAdj` | y-axis basis-change unitary `V` with `V┬╖VέΑι = 1` and `V┬╖┼ε^(3)┬╖VέΑι = ┼ε^(2)` | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |
| `spinHalfRot2_eq_yDiag_conj` / `spinHalfRot2_eq_exp` | `├δ^(2)_╬╕ = V┬╖├δ^(3)_╬╕┬╖VέΑι` and `├δ^(2)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼ε^(2))` (Problem 2.1.b, axis 2) | `Quantum/SpinHalfRotation.lean` |

### ZέΓΓ ├Ω ZέΓΓ representation (Tasaki ┬π2.1 eqs. (2.1.27)-(2.1.34))

The ZέΓΓ ├Ω ZέΓΓ structure is proved across files:
- S = 1/2 (projective, eq. (2.1.31)): `spinHalfRot*_pi_sq = -1`, anticommutation, products.
- S = 1 (genuine, eq. (2.1.27)): `spinOnePiRot*_sq = 1`, commutation.

See `Quantum/Z2Z2.lean` for the unified documentation.

### Polynomial-basis decomposition for S = 1 (Tasaki ┬π2.1 Problem 2.1.a, S = 1)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum
Many-Body Systems*, ┬π2.1 Problem 2.1.a, p. 15 + solution S.1, p. 493.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}` | the three diagonal projectors `\|╧Ι^╧Δέθσέθρ╧Ι^╧Δ\|` (╧Δ έΙΙ {+1, 0, -1}) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneProj{Plus,Zero,Minus}_eq_polynomial` | each diagonal projector equals a polynomial in `┼ε^(3)` (Lagrange interpolation) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,02,10,12,20,21}` | the six off-diagonal matrix units `\|╧Ι^╧Εέθσέθρ╧Ι^╧Δ\|` (╧Ε έΚι ╧Δ) | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{01,12}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/έΙγ2) ┼ε^- ┬╖ P_╧Δ` for the two single-step lowering units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit{10,21}_eq_polynomial` | `(1/έΙγ2) ┼ε^+ ┬╖ P_╧Δ` for the two single-step raising units | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit02_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (┼ε^-)┬▓ ┬╖ P_+` for the double-step lowering unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOneUnit20_eq_polynomial` | `(1/2) (┼ε^+)┬▓ ┬╖ P_-` for the double-step raising unit | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |
| `spinOne_decomposition` | every 3├Ω3 complex matrix is a linear combination of the 9 matrix units (entry-wise); combined with the polynomial expressions above this gives Tasaki Problem 2.1.a for `S = 1` | `Quantum/SpinOneDecomp.lean` |

### Spin-`S` operators (general S έΚξ 0, parameterised by `N = 2S : έΕΧ`)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body Systems*, ┬π2.1 Problem 2.1.a (p. 15) and solution S.1 (p. 493).

Generic spin-`S` operators live on `Matrix (Fin (N + 1)) (Fin (N + 1)) έΕΓ`, with `N = 2S : έΕΧ` (so `N = 1` έΘΦ `S = 1/2`, `N = 2` έΘΦ `S = 1`). Tracking issue #458 (Tasaki ┬π2.1 P1d''' general-S generalisation).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinSOp3` | `┼ε^(3) := diag(N/2, N/2 - 1, έΑο, -N/2)` for `N : έΕΧ` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus` / `spinSOpMinus` | raising/lowering operators with matrix entries `έΙγ(k┬╖(N έΙΤ k + 1))` (resp. `έΙγ((N έΙΤ k)(k + 1))`) on the immediate sub/super-diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp1` / `spinSOp2` | `┼ε^(1) := (┼ε^+ + ┼ε^-) / 2`, `┼ε^(2) := (┼ε^+ έΙΤ ┼ε^-) / (2 i)` | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_apply_top` / `_bottom` | `┼ε^+` annihilates `\|Nέθσ` (highest weight); `┼ε^-` annihilates `\|0έθσ` (lowest weight) έΑΦ the ladder boundaries | `Quantum/SpinS/Operators.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp{Plus,Minus}` | **Cartan relations** `[┼ε^{(3)}, ┼ε^+] = ┼ε^+` and `[┼ε^{(3)}, ┼ε^-] = -┼ε^-`: `┼ε^┬▒` shift the magnetic quantum number by `┬▒1`. Proved entry-wise via `Matrix.diagonal_mul` / `mul_diagonal` (╬▓-2 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Algebra.lean` |
| `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_conjTranspose` / `spinSOp{1,2,3}_isHermitian` | adjointness `(┼ε^+)ά┤┤ = ┼ε^-`, `(┼ε^-)ά┤┤ = ┼ε^+`, and Hermiticity of `┼ε^{(╬▒)}` for `╬▒ έΙΙ {1, 2, 3}` (╬▓-3 of Issue #458). The ladder adjointness follows from the matrix-entry symmetry; `┼ε^{(1)}` and `┼ε^{(2)}` use the `1/2` and `1/(2i)` self-conjugacy facts | `Quantum/SpinS/Hermitian.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj` / `spinSOp3_sub_smul_mul_diagProj` / `_self_mul_diagProj` | the **diagonal projector** `P_k := diag(╬┤_{i,k})` and the eigenvalue-action lemma `(┼ε^{(3)} έΙΤ ╬╗_j έΑλ 1) ┬╖ P_k = (╬╗_k έΙΤ ╬╗_j) ┬╖ P_k`, with the annihilation specialisation at `j = k` (╬▓-4 of Issue #458). Foundation for the Lagrange-interpolation polynomial decomposition: each `P_k` will be expressed as `έΙΠ_{j έΚι k} (┼ε^{(3)} έΙΤ ╬╗_j έΑλ 1) / (╬╗_k έΙΤ ╬╗_j)` in a follow-up PR | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProj.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mul_diagProj` / `diagProj_mul_spinSOp3` / `spinSOp3_commute_diagProj` | the **eigenvalue equation** `┼ε^{(3)} ┬╖ P_k = ╬╗_k ┬╖ P_k` (where `╬╗_k = (N : έΕΓ)/2 έΙΤ k`), with the symmetric `P_k ┬╖ ┼ε^{(3)} = ╬╗_k ┬╖ P_k` and the resulting commutativity. Both are diagonal-times-diagonal computations (╬▓-5 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Lagrange.lean` |
| `mul_diagProj_apply` / `diagProj_mul_apply` / `spinSOp{Plus,Minus}_mul_diagProj_apply` | **Column/row selection** by the diagonal projector: `(A ┬╖ P_k)[i, j] = A[i, k]` if `j = k` else `0` (and dually for `P_k ┬╖ A`). Specialised to `┼ε^┬▒` produces off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6 of Issue #458) έΑΦ the building blocks of the polynomial decomposition theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderProj.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_mul_spinSOpMinus` | **Ladder recursion**: `┼ε^+ ┬╖ P_{k+1} ┬╖ ┼ε^- = (k + 1)(N έΙΤ k) ┬╖ P_k`. The triple product collapses to a non-zero scalar multiple of `P_k`. Iterating from the lowest-weight projector `P_N` (itself a polynomial in `┼ε^{(3)}`) yields every diagonal projector as a polynomial in `{1╠Γ, ┼ε^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-7 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderRecursion.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_first` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_last` | **Ladder boundaries**: `┼ε^+ ┬╖ P_0 = 0` (top of ladder) and `┼ε^- ┬╖ P_N = 0` (bottom). The first/last columns of `┼ε^┬▒` vanish, so multiplication by `P_{0/N}` (which selects that column) gives `0`. Terminate the recursion at the highest- and lowest-weight projectors (╬▓-8 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderBoundary.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_isHermitian` / `sum_spinSDiagProj_eq_one` | **Hermiticity** of each `P_k`, and the **resolution of the identity** `╬μ_k P_k = 1`. The latter is the cornerstone for the matrix-unit decomposition: combined with the off-diagonal matrix units (╬▓-6, ╬▓-7), gives every matrix in `M_{N+1}(έΕΓ)` (╬▓-9 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjProperties.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_diagProj_succ_eq_single` / `spinSOpMinus_mul_diagProj_eq_single` | **Off-diagonal matrix-unit decomposition**: `┼ε^+ ┬╖ P_{i+1} = έΙγ((i+1)(NέΙΤi)) ┬╖ E_{i, i+1}` and `┼ε^- ┬╖ P_i = έΙγ((NέΙΤi)(i+1)) ┬╖ E_{i+1, i}`, where `E_{a, b} := Matrix.single a b 1` is the (a, b) matrix unit (╬▓-10 of Issue #458). These are the simplest off-diagonal matrix units expressible via `┼ε^┬▒` ladder action on diagonal projectors | `Quantum/SpinS/OffDiagUnit.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_sq_eq_diagonal` | `(┼ε^{(3)})┬▓ = diag((N/2 έΙΤ i)┬▓)`. Step toward the **Casimir identity** `(┼ε^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼ε^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼ε^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1` for general spin (╬▓-11 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Op3Square.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mul_spinSOpMinus_eq_diagonal` | `┼ε^+ ┬╖ ┼ε^- = diag((i + 1)(N έΙΤ i))`. The product is diagonal because `┼ε^+[i, l] ┬╖ ┼ε^-[l, j]` is non-zero only when `l = i + 1 = j + 1`, forcing `i = j`. Combined with the analogous `┼ε^- ┬╖ ┼ε^+` and `(┼ε^{(3)})┬▓`, this assembles the Casimir identity (╬▓-12 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PlusMinusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOpMinus_mul_spinSOpPlus_eq_diagonal` | `┼ε^- ┬╖ ┼ε^+ = diag(i ┬╖ (N έΙΤ i + 1))`. Symmetric to ╬▓-12 with `┼ε^┬▒` swapped (╬▓-13 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/MinusPlusDiag.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_sq_add_spinSOp2_sq` / `spinSOp_total_squared` | **Casimir identity** for general spin-`S`: `(┼ε^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼ε^{(2)})┬▓ + (┼ε^{(3)})┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1`, equivalent to `S(S+1) ┬╖ 1` for `S = N/2`. Proves the irreducible spin-`S` representation has Casimir eigenvalue `S(S+1)` (Schur's lemma). The intermediate identity `(┼ε^{(1)})┬▓ + (┼ε^{(2)})┬▓ = (1/2)(┼ε^+ ┬╖ ┼ε^- + ┼ε^- ┬╖ ┼ε^+)` is proved using `module` (╬▓-14 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Casimir.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_mulVec_basis` | spin-`S` eigenstate equation `┼ε^{(3)} ┬╖ \|kέθσ = (N/2 έΙΤ k) ┬╖ \|kέθσ`, where `\|kέθσ := Pi.single k 1` is the `k`-th unit basis vector of `(Fin (N + 1) έΗΤ έΕΓ)` (╬▓-15 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Eigenstates.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mulVec_basis` / `spinSOpMinus_mulVec_basis` | **Ladder action on basis vectors**: `┼ε^+ ┬╖ \|kέθσ = έΙγ(k(N έΙΤ k + 1)) ┬╖ \|k έΙΤ 1έθσ` for `k έΚξ 1`, and `┼ε^- ┬╖ \|kέθσ = έΙγ((N έΙΤ k)(k + 1)) ┬╖ \|k + 1έθσ` for `k έΚν N έΙΤ 1`. The standard SU(2) ladder relations on the magnetic-quantum-number basis (╬▓-16 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LadderStates.lean` |
| `spinSOp_total_squared_mulVec_basis` | **Casimir eigenvalue on basis**: `(┼ε)┬▓ ┬╖ \|kέθσ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ \|kέθσ`. Direct consequence of `(┼ε)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1` (╬▓-14) έΑΦ every basis vector has the universal Casimir eigenvalue, reflecting that the spin-`S` representation is a single irreducible (Schur) (╬▓-17 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirEigenvalue.lean` |
| `spinSOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}_commute_total_squared` | **Casimir invariance**: each `┼ε^{(╬▒)}` and `┼ε^┬▒` commutes with the Casimir `(┼ε)┬▓ = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ 1`. Direct consequence of the scalar nature of the Casimir (╬▓-18 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirInvariance.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_commutator_spinSOpMinus` | **Third Cartan relation**: `[┼ε^+, ┼ε^-] = 2 ┬╖ ┼ε^{(3)}`. Combines ╬▓-12 (`┼ε^+ ┬╖ ┼ε^- = diag((i + 1)(N έΙΤ i))`) and ╬▓-13 (`┼ε^- ┬╖ ┼ε^+ = diag(i (N έΙΤ i + 1))`); the difference `(i+1)(N-i) έΙΤ i(N-i+1) = N έΙΤ 2i = 2(N/2 έΙΤ i)` matches `2 ┬╖ ┼ε^{(3)}` (╬▓-19 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/Cartan3.lean` |
| `spinSOp1_commutator_spinSOp2` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼ε^{(1)}, ┼ε^{(2)}] = i ┬╖ ┼ε^{(3)}`. Derived from the Cartan relations by algebraic manipulation through `┼ε^{(1)} = (┼ε^+ + ┼ε^-)/2` and `┼ε^{(2)} = (┼ε^+ έΙΤ ┼ε^-)/(2i)`: `(P+Q)(P-Q) έΙΤ (P-Q)(P+Q) = -2[P, Q] = -4 ┼ε^{(3)}`, then scalar simplification `-1/I = I` (╬▓-20 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator.lean` |
| `spinSOp2_commutator_spinSOp3` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼ε^{(2)}, ┼ε^{(3)}] = i ┬╖ ┼ε^{(1)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 (`[┼ε^{(3)}, ┼ε^┬▒] = ┬▒┼ε^┬▒`) via `┼ε^{(2)} = (┼ε^+ έΙΤ ┼ε^-)/(2i)`: the commutator reduces to `(1/(2i)) (-┼ε^+ έΙΤ ┼ε^-) = (-1/(2i)) ┬╖ 2 ┼ε^{(1)} = i ┬╖ ┼ε^{(1)}` (╬▓-21 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator23.lean` |
| `spinSOp3_commutator_spinSOp1` | **Cyclic SU(2) commutator** `[┼ε^{(3)}, ┼ε^{(1)}] = i ┬╖ ┼ε^{(2)}`. Derived from Cartan ╬▓-2 via `┼ε^{(1)} = (┼ε^+ + ┼ε^-)/2`: the commutator reduces to `(1/2) (┼ε^+ έΙΤ ┼ε^-) = (1/2) ┬╖ (2i) ┬╖ ┼ε^{(2)} = i ┬╖ ┼ε^{(2)}`. Together with ╬▓-20 and ╬▓-21 this completes the standard SU(2) commutator algebra (Tasaki eq. (2.1.1)) for spin-`S` operators (╬▓-22 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/CyclicCommutator31.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mul_self` / `spinSDiagProj_mul_of_ne` | **Idempotence and orthogonality** of the diagonal projectors `P_k = |kέθσέθρk|`: `P_k ┬╖ P_k = P_k` and `P_i ┬╖ P_j = 0` for `i έΚι j`. Combined with ╬▓-9 (`έΙΣ_k P_k = 1`) this gives the spectral decomposition of the identity for `┼ε^{(3)}` (╬▓-23 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/DiagProjOrtho.lean` |
| `aeval_diagonal` | **Polynomial evaluation at a diagonal matrix**: `aeval (Matrix.diagonal v) p = Matrix.diagonal (fun i => p.eval (v i))`. Foundational lemma for the Lagrange-interpolation step (╬▓-25+) έΑΦ lets us pull a polynomial in `┼ε^{(3)}` (a diagonal matrix) through to its scalar action on each diagonal entry. Proof by `Polynomial.induction_on'` on monomials and addition (╬▓-24 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AevalDiagonal.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_eq_lagrange_aeval` | **Lagrange-interpolation formula for `P_k`**: `P_k = aeval (┼ε^{(3)}) (Lagrange.basis Finset.univ (spinSOp3Eigen N) k)`, equivalently `P_k = έΙΠ_{j έΚι k} (┼ε^{(3)} έΙΤ ╬╗_j έΑλ 1)/(╬╗_k έΙΤ ╬╗_j)` with `╬╗_j = (N : έΕΓ)/2 έΙΤ j.val`. Combines ╬▓-24 (`aeval` of diagonal) with mathlib's `Lagrange.eval_basis_self` / `eval_basis_of_ne`. Each diagonal projector is therefore a **polynomial in `┼ε^{(3)}`**, which combined with ╬▓-9 (`έΙΣ P_k = 1`) gives an explicit polynomial decomposition of `1╠Γ` (╬▓-25 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/LagrangeFormula.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_eq_one_add_I_smul_two` / `spinSOpMinus_eq_one_sub_I_smul_two` | **Inversion of the Cartesian definition**: `┼ε^+ = ┼ε^{(1)} + i ┬╖ ┼ε^{(2)}` and `┼ε^- = ┼ε^{(1)} έΙΤ i ┬╖ ┼ε^{(2)}`. The defining identities `┼ε^{(1)} = (1/2)(┼ε^+ + ┼ε^-)`, `┼ε^{(2)} = (1/(2i))(┼ε^+ έΙΤ ┼ε^-)` invert to express the ladder operators as **linear combinations** of the Hermitian Cartesian spin operators (╬▓-26 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMAsOneTwo.lean` |
| `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin_spinSOp3` / `spinSDiagProj_mem_adjoin` | **Diagonal projectors live in the algebra generated by the spin operators**: `P_k έΙΙ Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(3)}}` (and a fortiori `P_k έΙΙ Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(1)}, ┼ε^{(2)}, ┼ε^{(3)}}`). Promotes the Lagrange-interpolation formula (╬▓-25) from "polynomial-evaluation" form to "subalgebra-membership" form via `Algebra.adjoin_singleton_eq_range_aeval` (╬▓-27 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/ProjMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `spinSOpPlus_mem_adjoin` / `spinSOpMinus_mem_adjoin` | **Ladder operators live in `Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(╬▒)}}`**: `┼ε^+, ┼ε^- έΙΙ Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(1)}, ┼ε^{(2)}, ┼ε^{(3)}}`. Direct consequence of ╬▓-26 (`┼ε^┬▒ = ┼ε^{(1)} ┬▒ i ┬╖ ┼ε^{(2)}`) and the fact that subalgebras are closed under `έΕΓ`-linear combinations (╬▓-28 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/PMMemAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Immediate-neighbor matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+1}, E_{i+1, i} έΙΙ Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(1)}, ┼ε^{(2)}, ┼ε^{(3)}}`. Combines ╬▓-10 (`┼ε^+ ┬╖ P_{i+1} = έΙγ((i+1)(N-i)) ┬╖ E_{i, i+1}`) with ╬▓-27 (`P_k έΙΙ adjoin`) and ╬▓-28 (`┼ε^┬▒ έΙΙ adjoin`); the ladder coefficient is non-zero on the valid range and the subalgebra is closed under multiplication and scalar `smul`. Step toward expressing every matrix unit as a polynomial in `{┼ε^{(╬▒)}}` (╬▓-29 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/NeighborUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `single_offset_succ_mem_adjoin` / `single_offset_succ_swap_mem_adjoin` | **Arbitrary stride-(k+1) matrix units live in `Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(╬▒)}}`**: `E_{i, i+(k+1)}, E_{i+(k+1), i} έΙΙ Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(╬▒)}}` for any `k`. Induction on `k`: base case is ╬▓-29; inductive step chains via `Matrix.single_mul_single_same` (`E_{i,j} ┬╖ E_{j,k} = E_{i,k}`) (╬▓-30 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/OffsetUnitAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_single_mem_adjoin` | **Every matrix unit `E_{i,j}` lives in `Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(╬▒)}}`**. Three-case split: `i = j` reduces to ╬▓-27 via `Matrix.diagonal_single` (`E_{i,i} = P_i`); `i.val < j.val` is ╬▓-30 upper; `j.val < i.val` is ╬▓-30 lower. Last building block before the spanning theorem (╬▓-31 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllUnitsAdjoin.lean` |
| `matrix_mem_adjoin` / **`spinS_adjoin_eq_top`** | **ΏθΟψ Tasaki ┬π2.1 Problem 2.1.a (P1d''') general-`S`**: every operator on the `(2S+1)`-dimensional spin-`S` Hilbert space `έΕΓ^{N+1}` is a polynomial in `{1╠Γ, ┼ε^{(1)}, ┼ε^{(2)}, ┼ε^{(3)}}`. Equivalently, `Algebra.adjoin έΕΓ {┼ε^{(1)}, ┼ε^{(2)}, ┼ε^{(3)}} = έΛν`. Proof: every matrix unit `E_{i,j} έΙΙ adjoin` (╬▓-31), so by the entry-wise decomposition `M = έΙΣ_{i,j} M_{i,j} έΑλ E_{i,j}` (`Matrix.matrix_eq_sum_single`) and the linearity of the subalgebra, every matrix is in the adjoin (╬▓-32 of Issue #458) | `Quantum/SpinS/SpanningTheorem.lean` |
| `ManyBodyOpS` / `onSiteS` / `spinSSiteOp{1,2,3,Plus,Minus}` | **Multi-site spin-`S` operator space** indexed by configurations `╧Δ : ╬δ έΗΤ Fin (N + 1)`, with the site-embedded operator `onSiteS i A` acting as `A` on site `i` and as the identity elsewhere; site-specialised `┼ε_i^{(╬▒)}, ┼ε_i^┬▒`. Hermiticity preservation `onSiteS_isHermitian` lifts from single-site to multi-site (Tasaki ┬π2.5 Phase B-╬▓ ╬▓-3a, Issue #412) | `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSiteCore.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/MultiSite.lean` |
