| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfUp` / `_spinHalfDown` | `╬α╠Γ|╧Ι^έΗΣέθσ = |╧Ι^έΗΥέθσ` and `╬α╠Γ|╧Ι^έΗΥέθσ = -|╧Ι^έΗΣέθσ` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_add` | additivity `╬α╠Γ(v + w) = ╬α╠Γ(v) + ╬α╠Γ(w)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_smul` | **antilinearity** in the scalar: `╬α╠Γ(c έΑλ v) = (conj c) έΑλ ╬α╠Γ(v)` (Tasaki ┬π2.3 antilinearity warning, pp. 26έΑΥ27) | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_sq` | **Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki eq. (2.3.8), half-odd-integer): `╬α╠Γ┬▓ = -1╠Γ` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | spin sign flip (Tasaki eq. (2.3.14)): `╬α╠Γ(┼ε^(╬▒) ┬╖ v) = (-┼ε^(╬▒)) ┬╖ (╬α╠Γ v)` for `╬▒ = 1, 2, 3` έΑΦ equivariance form of `╬α╠Γ ┼ε^(╬▒) ╬α╠ΓέΒ╗┬╣ = -┼ε^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf` | the antilinear complex-conjugation map `K╠Γ` of Tasaki ┬π2.3 eq. (2.3.4) at `S = 1/2`: `K╠Γ(v) i := conj(v i)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `complexConjugationSpinHalf_sq` / `_add` / `_smul` | `K╠Γ` is involutive (`K╠Γ┬▓ = id`), additive, and antilinear in the scalar | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalf_eq_spinHalfRot2_pi_mulVec` | the **factorisation identity** of Tasaki ┬π2.3: `╬α╠Γ = ├╗_2 ┬╖ K╠Γ` where `├╗_2 = spinHalfRot2 ╧Α` is the ╧Α rotation about the `2`-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalSpinHalf.lean` |
| `flipConfig` / `flipConfig_apply` / `flipConfig_involutive` | the spin-flip on a many-body configuration `╧Δ : ╬δ έΗΤ Fin 2`: `flipConfig ╧Δ x := 1 - ╧Δ x`; involutive | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign` / `timeReversalSign_zero` / `_one` / `_mul_flip` | per-site sign factor `╬╡(0) = 1`, `╬╡(1) = -1` for the multi-spin time-reversal, with `╬╡(s) ┬╖ ╬╡(1 - s) = -1` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` | multi-spin time-reversal map (Tasaki ┬π2.3 lattice extension, `S = 1/2`): `(╬α╠Γ_tot v) ╧Ε := (έΙΠ_x ╬╡(╧Ε x)) ┬╖ conj(v (flip ╧Ε))` for finite `╬δ` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_conj` / `timeReversalSign_prod_mul_flip` | the product `έΙΠ_x ╬╡(╧Ε x)` is real (conjugation invariant); the cross product equals `(-1)^|╬δ|` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_sq` | **Multi-spin Kramers degeneracy at `S = 1/2`** (Tasaki ┬π2.3 half-odd-integer extension): `╬α╠Γ_tot┬▓ = (-1)^|╬δ| ┬╖ 1╠Γ` έΑΦ `+1╠Γ` when `|╬δ|` is even, `-1╠Γ` when odd | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec` | action of `╬α╠Γ_tot` on a many-body basis state: `╬α╠Γ_tot |╬ρ_╧Δέθσ = (έΙΠ_x ╬╡(flip ╧Δ x)) ┬╖ |╬ρ_{flip ╧Δ}έθσ` έΑΦ natural many-body generalisation of `╬α╠Γ|έΗΣέθσ = |έΗΥέθσ` and `╬α╠Γ|έΗΥέθσ = -|έΗΣέθσ` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliZ_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `╧Δ^z` (Tasaki ┬π2.3 (2.3.14) lifted to many-body): `╬α╠Γ_tot ((onSite x ╧Δ^z) v) = (-(onSite x ╧Δ^z))(╬α╠Γ_tot v)`. Diagonal-action case; `╧Δ^x`, `╧Δ^y` deferred | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `siteFlipAt` / `siteFlipAt_self` / `siteFlipAt_of_ne` / `flipConfig_siteFlipAt_comm` / `siteFlipAt_involutive` | per-site flip helpers: `siteFlipAt ╧Ε x` flips slot `x` only; commutes with `flipConfig`; involutive. The combinatorial primitive underlying off-diagonal `╧Δ^x_x` / `╧Δ^y_x` action (deferred) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_basisVec` | basis-state action of the off-diagonal site Pauli: `(onSite x ╧Δ^x).mulVec |╬ρ_╧Δέθσ = |╬ρ_{siteFlipAt ╧Δ x}έθσ` (the spin at site `x` is swapped) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `pauliX_eq_indicator` / `onSite_pauliX_mulVec_apply` | closed-form `pauliX a b = if b = 1 - a then 1 else 0`, lifted to `((onSite x ╧Δ^x).mulVec v) ╧Ε = v (siteFlipAt ╧Ε x)` for any state `v` (general extension of the basis-state action) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSign_prod_siteFlipAt` | `έΙΠ_y ╬╡((siteFlipAt ╧Ε x) y) = -(έΙΠ_y ╬╡(╧Ε y))` έΑΦ the per-site flip swaps `╬╡(╧Ε x)` with `╬╡(1 - ╧Ε x) = -╬╡(╧Ε x)`, flipping the total sign | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliX_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `╧Δ^x` (Tasaki ┬π2.3 (2.3.14) at ╬▒ = 1): `╬α╠Γ_tot ((onSite x ╧Δ^x) v) = (-(onSite x ╧Δ^x))(╬α╠Γ_tot v)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_pauliY_mulVec` | multi-site sign-flip equivariance for `╧Δ^y` (Tasaki ┬π2.3 (2.3.14) at ╬▒ = 2): `╬α╠Γ_tot ((onSite x ╧Δ^y) v) = (-(onSite x ╧Δ^y))(╬α╠Γ_tot v)`. The proof handles the per-site `┬▒i` factor via `conj(pauliY_sign(1 - s)) = pauliY_sign(s)` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_add` / `_smul` / `_real_smul` | multi-spin `╬α╠Γ_tot` is additive, antilinear in the scalar (`╬α╠Γ_tot(c έΑλ v) = conj(c) έΑλ ╬α╠Γ_tot v`), and real-linear (special case of antilinearity at real `r`) έΑΦ foundational for lifting Pauli-axis equivariance to bilinear / Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian forms | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_onSite_spinHalfOp{1,2,3}_mulVec` | Tasaki ┬π2.3 (2.3.14) for spin-1/2 ops `┼ε^(╬▒) = ╧Δ^(╬▒) / 2`: `╬α╠Γ_tot ((onSite x ┼ε^(╬▒)) v) = (-(onSite x ┼ε^(╬▒)))(╬α╠Γ_tot v)` for ╬▒ = 1, 2, 3 έΑΦ direct corollaries of the Pauli versions by scalar (1/2) multiplication | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_spinHalfDot_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the bilinear `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y`** (Tasaki ┬π2.3): `╬α╠Γ_tot ((┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y) v) = (┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y)(╬α╠Γ_tot v)` έΑΦ two equivariance `-1` factors cancel; sums per-axis | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec` | **Time-reversal invariance of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki ┬π2.3): for real coupling `J` (`conj(J(x,y)) = J(x,y)`), `╬α╠Γ_tot ((H J) v) = (H J)(╬α╠Γ_tot v)`. Combines per-bond invariance + ╬α╠Γ_tot antilinearity (J reality) + additivity (over double-sum) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_conj` / `periodicChainCoupling_conj` | every entry of `openChainCoupling N J` (resp. `periodicChainCoupling N J`) is real (under complex conjugation), since `J : έΕζ` makes `(-(J : έΕΓ))` real-valued | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_openChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_periodicChainHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_squareTorusHeisenberg_mulVec` / `_cubicLatticeHeisenberg_mulVec` | concrete time-reversal invariance: the open / periodic chain, the 2D open square / torus, and the 3D cubic Heisenberg Hamiltonians all commute with `╬α╠Γ_tot` for any real coupling `J : έΕζ`. Backed by `*Coupling_conj` reality lemmas in `HeisenbergChain.lean` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_basisVec_upDown` / `_basisVec_basisSwap_upDown` | `╬α╠Γ_tot |έΗΣέΗΥέθσ = -|έΗΥέΗΣέθσ` and `╬α╠Γ_tot |έΗΥέΗΣέθσ = -|έΗΣέΗΥέθσ` on `Fin 2` | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_singlet` | the two-site spin singlet `|έΗΣέΗΥέθσ - |έΗΥέΗΣέθσ` is **time-reversal invariant** (Tasaki ┬π2.3 / ┬πA.3): being the SU(2) `S = 0` representation, it survives `╬α╠Γ_tot` unchanged | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_triplet_zero` | the triplet `m = 0` state `|έΗΣέΗΥέθσ + |έΗΥέΗΣέθσ` is **anti-invariant** under `╬α╠Γ_tot`: `╬α╠Γ_tot (|έΗΣέΗΥέθσ + |έΗΥέΗΣέθσ) = -(|έΗΣέΗΥέθσ + |έΗΥέΗΣέθσ)` (the symmetric combination picks up a minus sign from the per-basis-vector flip) | `Quantum/TimeReversalMulti.lean` |

### Multi-body operator space (abstract lattice)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬π2.2, pp. 21-26 (tensor-product Hilbert space and site-local
operators). The lattice `╬δ` is an arbitrary finite set with decidable
equality; specializing to `╬δ = Fin N` recovers an `N`-site chain.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `ManyBodyOp ╬δ` | `Matrix (╬δ έΗΤ Fin 2) (╬δ έΗΤ Fin 2) έΕΓ` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite i A` | site-embedded operator at `i : ╬δ` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_isHermitian` | `A.IsHermitian έΗΤ (onSite i A).IsHermitian` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_add`, `onSite_sub`, `onSite_zero`, `onSite_smul`, `onSite_one` | linearity of the site embedding and `onSite i 1 = 1` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_of_ne` | distinct-site commutation (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x έΚι y`, S = 1/2) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `basisVec` / `onSite_mulVec_basisVec` | tensor-product basis states and their action under site operators (Tasaki (2.2.1)/(2.2.4)) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_mul_onSite_same` | same-site product `onSite i A ┬╖ onSite i B = onSite i (A ┬╖ B)` (Tasaki (2.2.6), `x = y`) | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_same` | same-site commutator `[onSite i A, onSite i B] = onSite i [A, B]` | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | commuting Hermitians multiply Hermitian (generic; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4342) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |

### Generic matrix-analysis helpers (`Math/MatrixAnalysis/`)

Topic-organized generic linear-algebra facts extracted from the physics files where they had been
re-proved as private helpers (Issue [#4339](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4339)).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.isHermitian_sum` | a finite sum of Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.mul_of_commute` | the product of two commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianSum.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_isHermitian` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting Hermitian matrices is Hermitian | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_sq_of_sq_one` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting involutions is an involution | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mul_self_of_idempotent` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of pairwise-commuting idempotents is idempotent | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.noncommProd_mulVec_eq_self` | a `Finset.noncommProd` of matrices each fixing `╧Ι` also fixes `╧Ι` | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/NoncommProd.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | the trace of a Hermitian matrix is real | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `Tr(A┬╖B)` of two Hermitian matrices is conjugation-invariant | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |

(The two trace helpers above are also listed in the Gibbs-state section where they are consumed; the
canonical definitions live in `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean`.)

These are consumed by the JordanέΑΥWigner string / Hubbard hard-core projection layers (PR #4342),
replacing the per-file private copies in `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean`,
`Fermion/JordanWigner/Operators.lean`, and `Fermion/JordanWigner/Hubbard/HardcoreProjection.lean`.

### HorschέΑΥvon der Linden low-lying states (Tasaki ┬π3.4, Theorem 3.1)

Start of the Chapters 3έΑΥ10 backfill (Issue
[#4485](https://github.com/phasetr/lattice-system/issues/4485); book order, infinite systems in
scope). Tasaki ┬π3.4, Theorem 3.1, eqs. (3.4.7)έΑΥ(3.4.12), pp. 66έΑΥ67.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `horsch_vonderLinden_lowLying` | for Hermitian `H` with min eigenvalue `EέΓΑ = eigenvalues iέΓΑ`, a unit state `╬Υ` orthogonal to the ground eigenvector with `έθρ╬Υ,H╬Υέθσ έΚν EέΓΑ+╬┤` yields an energy eigenstate `j έΚι iέΓΑ` with `EέΓΑ έΚν E_j έΚν EέΓΑ+╬┤` (a low-lying state; possibly another ground state if degenerate, as Tasaki notes) | `Quantum/HorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `kaplan_horsch_vonderLinden_order_lower_bound` | **Theorem 3.2, finite-volume core** (┬π3.4; the thermodynamic double limit 3.4.22 is not formalized): for the field-perturbed ground state `╬ρ` of `H έΙΤ h┬╖O` (`h>0`) and any trial `╬η`, the order parameter obeys `έθρ╬η,O╬ηέθσ + (EέΓΑέΙΤέθρ╬η,H╬ηέθσ)/h έΚν έθρ╬ρ,O╬ρέθσ` (eq. (3.4.21), the variational core; the double limit `LέΗΣέΙη,hέΗΥ0` gives the έΙγqέΓΑ SSB bound) | `Quantum/KaplanHorschVonderLinden.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS` / `staggeredOrderOpS_isHermitian` | **Theorem 4.1** (┬π4.1, DysonέΑΥLiebέΑΥSimon, toward; eq. (4.1.7)): the staggered (N├σel) order operator `├Φ = ╬μ_x ╬╡_x ┼ε_x^(3)` (`╬╡_x = ┬▒1` sublattice sign) and its Hermiticity (real ┬▒1 combination of Hermitian `┼ε_x^(3)`), so `έθρ╬ο,├Φ┬▓╬οέθσ` is a real order parameter. The N├σel-LRO statement needs the actual hypercubic torus + reflection positivity (false for arbitrary bipartite graphs); deferred pending that infrastructure (infinite-volume in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/DysonLiebSimon.lean` |
| `shastry_no_symmetry_breaking_1d` | **Theorem 4.2** (┬π4.1, Shastry, AXIOM; eqs. (4.1.9)έΑΥ(4.1.10)): absence of SSB in 1D. For the 1D AFM Heisenberg ring under a staggered field `─ν_h = ╬μ_x ┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_{x+1} έΙΤ h┬╖├Φ_L^(3)`, the per-site staggered order parameter of any normalized ground state vanishes in the iterated limit `lim_{hέΗΥ0} lim_{LέΗΣέΙη}` (eq. (4.1.10), ╬╡έΑΥ╬┤ form). Tasaki states it without proof; the deep argument is now being formalized as the **reflection-positivity infrastructure project** (#4777, codex-validated 11-PR plan). Defines `ringCoupling`, `ringStaggeredSublattice`, `staggeredFieldChainHamiltonianS`. **RP infra layer 1 (in progress):** `RingBondReflection.lean` έΑΦ even-ring bond reflection `ringReflect n x = 2nέΙΤ1έΙΤx` (involutive, half-swap, staggered-sign flip); `RingReflectionTheta.lean` έΑΦ reflection map `╬╕(A) ╧Δ ╧Ε = conj(A(╧Β╧Δ)(╧Β╧Ε))`, an antilinear `*`-automorphism, with the single-site bridge `╬╕(onSiteS x A) = onSiteS (ringReflect n x) (conj A)`. **RP infra layer 2** (`RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`): the Hamiltonian ╬╕-decomposition έΑΦ orientation reversal `J(r x)(r y)=J y x`, spin-op conjugation (S┬╣/S┬│ real, S┬▓ imaginary), `╬╕(┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y)=┼ε_{r x}┬╖┼ε_{r y}`, `╬╕(├Φ_L^(3))=έΙΤ├Φ_L^(3)` (staggered-sign flip), `╬╕(─ν)=─ν` (reindex + adjacency + dot-comm), and `╬╕(─ν_h)=─ν_{έΙΤh}` (the reflection symmetry exchanging the two staggered ground states). **RP infra layer 3** (`RingReflectionPositivity.lean`): the left-half subalgebra `SupportedOnLeftS` = `B(H_left) έΛΩ I_right` (two conditions: entries vanish off the right-diagonal **and** are independent of the common right-half value) with closure (zero/one/add/smul, left-site `onSiteS`), `╬╕` maps left-supported to right-supported (`theta_right`), and the reflection-positive functional predicate `ReflectionPositiveFunctionalS` (`0 έΚν Re ╧Η(╬╕(A)┬╖A)` for left-supported `A`). **RP infra layer 4** (`RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`): the `╬▓ = 0` base case έΑΦ `traceFunctional_reflectionPositive` proves the trace functional `X έΗο Tr X` is reflection positive, i.e. `0 έΚν Re Tr(╬╕(A)┬╖A)` for every left-supported `A`. The proof collapses `Tr(╬╕(A)┬╖A) = έΙΣ_{╧Δ,╬╝} ╬╕(A)╧Δ╬╝┬╖A╬╝╧Δ` to its diagonal (both support conditions force `╬╝=╧Δ`), then factorizes over the left/right configuration split (`configSplitEquiv`, from `finSumFinEquiv`): the diagonal value `A ╧Δ ╧Δ` depends only on the left half, giving `Tr(╬╕(A)┬╖A) = conj S ┬╖ S = έΑΨSέΑΨ┬▓ έΚξ 0` with `S = έΙΣ_έΕΥ D έΕΥ`. This is the infinite-temperature trace cone on which a later layer mounts the Gibbs exponential via Trotter. **RP infra layer 5** (`RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`): the algebraic completion of the cone έΑΦ `SupportedOnLeftS.mul` (the left-half subalgebra is closed under products), `SupportedOnLeftS.mul_theta_comm` (a left-supported `A` commutes with `╬╕(B)`, which acts on the right half), and the **weighted trace cone** `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive` (`X έΗο Tr((╬╕(C)┬╖C)┬╖X)` is reflection positive for left-supported `C`, via `Tr((╬╕C┬╖C)┬╖(╬╕A┬╖A)) = Tr(╬╕(C┬╖A)┬╖(C┬╖A)) έΚξ 0`) plus its nonnegative-finite-combination version `weightedTraceFunctional_reflectionPositive_finsetSum` έΑΦ the cone on which the Trotter/Lie-product factors of the Gibbs exponential will be mounted. **RP infra layer 6** (`RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`): the reflection-positive trace-weight cone έΑΦ `RPTraceWeightS M` (`X έΗο Tr(M┬╖X)` is an RP functional), the cone-representability predicate `RPTraceConeRepS` (nonnegative finite combination `έΙΣ cά╡λ ╬╕(Cά╡λ)┬╖Cά╡λ`) with closure under `one`/`zero`/`add`/`smul_nonneg`/`mul`/`pow` (the product closure via `weightGen_mul`: `(╬╕C┬╖C)(╬╕D┬╖D) = ╬╕(CD)┬╖(CD)`), `RPTraceConeRepS.rpTraceWeight` (representable έθ╣ RP trace weight, from the weighted cone), and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` (RP trace weights are closed under limits, by finite-dimensional trace continuity) έΑΦ the cone on which the next layer mounts the interaction Gibbs exponential `exp(t┬╖έΙΣcά╡λ╬╕(Cά╡λ)┬╖Cά╡λ)` as a limit of cone-representable partial sums. **RP infra layer 7** (`RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`): `rpInteractionExp_reflectionPositive` έΑΦ for a nonnegative finite interaction `D = t┬╖έΙΣά╡λ cά╡λ ╬╕(Cά╡λ)┬╖Cά╡λ` (`t, cά╡λ έΚξ 0`, `Cά╡λ` left-supported), the matrix exponential `exp D` is a reflection-positive trace weight. Each partial sum `έΙΣ_{k<m}(k!)έΒ╗┬╣Dά╡Π` of the exponential series is cone-representable (`smul_nonneg`/`add`/`pow`), hence an RP trace weight, and `exp D` is their limit (`expSeries_summable'.hasSum.tendsto_sum_nat`), so `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto` applies. Uses the `LέΙη`-operator-norm Banach structure (`open scoped Matrix.Norms.Operator`), whose topology is the entrywise (Pi) topology έΑΦ the same one underlying the trace continuity, so no topology diamond. **RP infra layer 8** (`RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`): the matrix exponential and the left subalgebra / reflection map έΑΦ `SupportedOnLeftS.exp` (`X` left-supported έθ╣ `exp X` left-supported: the left subalgebra is closed under products/sums/scalars/entrywise-limits via `SupportedOnLeftS.of_tendsto`, and `exp X` is the limit of its partial sums) and `ringReflectionThetaS_exp` (`╬╕(exp X) = exp(╬╕ X)`: `╬╕` is a continuous conjugate-linear `*`-automorphism with real exponential coefficients) έΑΦ the building blocks for the `e^{-╬▓H_L}` Hamiltonian factor of the full Gibbs reflection-positivity decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondReflection.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTheta.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionHamiltonian.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionPositivity.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionWeightedCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCone.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsExp.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionExpSupport.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `falkBruch_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkέΑΥBruch** (`FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the concrete ground-state FalkέΑΥBruch inequality έΑΦ for a ground state `╬ο`, Hermitian `A` and a potential `y` for `A╬ο` (`(HέΙΤEέΓΑ)y = A╬ο`), `2(Reέθρ╬ο,A┬▓╬οέθσ)┬▓ έΚν Reέθρ╬ο,[A,[H,A]]╬οέθσ┬╖Reέθρy,A╬οέθσ`; combines the abstract FalkέΑΥBruch (`K = HέΙΤEέΓΑ` PSD) with the double-commutator = variational-energy identity | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruchDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `hermitianSubMin_posSemidef` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkέΑΥBruch** (`HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Hamiltonian shifted to its ground energy `H έΙΤ EέΓΑ` (`EέΓΑ = hermitianMinEigenvalue`) is positive-semidefinite έΑΦ the quadratic form is real (Hermiticity, `isHermitian_dotProduct_mulVec_im_zero`) with nonnegative real part (the variational lower bound); this is the PSD `K` fed into the FalkέΑΥBruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianSubMinPosSemidef.lean` |
| `falkBruch_of_mulVec_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkέΑΥBruch** (`FalkBruch.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the abstract ground-state FalkέΑΥBruch inequality έΑΦ for PSD `K` and `w` in the range of `K` (`K y = w`), `(Reέθρw,wέθσ)┬▓ έΚν Reέθρw,K wέθσ┬╖Reέθρy,wέθσ`, an immediate instance of the PSD CauchyέΑΥSchwarz (`posSemidef_re_dotProduct_mulVec_sq_le`) with second argument `y`; no spectral theory / pseudo-inverse needed | `Quantum/SpinS/FalkBruch.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_nonneg` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkέΑΥBruch** (`DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state double commutator `Reέθρ╬ο, [A,[H,A]] ╬οέθσ έΚξ 0` for Hermitian `A` and a minimal-eigenvalue eigenvector `╬ο` έΑΦ it equals twice the variational energy of `A╬ο`, nonnegative since the ground energy is the Rayleigh minimum (`hermitianMinEigenvalue_mul_dotProduct_re_le_rayleighOnVec`); the sign of the FalkέΑΥBruch numerator | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorNonneg.lean` |
| `double_commutator_ground_state_eq` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkέΑΥBruch** (`DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): for Hermitian `H`, `A` and a ground state `H╬ο = EέΓΑ╬ο` (`EέΓΑ` real), the f-sum-rule oscillator strength equals twice the variational energy of `A╬ο` έΑΦ `έθρ╬ο, [A,[H,A]] ╬οέθσ = 2έθρA╬ο, H A╬οέθσ έΙΤ 2EέΓΑέθρA╬ο, A╬οέθσ` (Hermitian-shift); the denominator of the ground-state FalkέΑΥBruch inequality | `Quantum/SpinS/DoubleCommutatorVariational.lean` |
| `spinSDot_double_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the per-bond double commutator is the negated transverse bond energy `[┼ε_a^{(3)}, [┼ε_a┬╖┼ε_b, ┼ε_a^{(3)}]] = έΙΤ(┼ε_a^{(1)}┼ε_b^{(1)} + ┼ε_a^{(2)}┼ε_b^{(2)})` (the f-sum-rule integrand / oscillator strength of the staggered mode), bounded by the bond energy | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_commutator_onSiteS` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the Heisenberg Hamiltonian commutator distributes over bonds `[─ν_J, ┼ε_z^{(3)}] = ╬μ_x ╬μ_y J_{xy} [┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]`; with the per-bond case analysis this reduces the spin-current divergence `[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/HeisenbergSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` / `_right` | **Cor 4.3 / IR bound** (`SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[┼ε_a┬╖┼ε_b, ┼ε_a^{(3)}] = i(┼ε_a^{(1)} ┼ε_b^{(2)} έΙΤ ┼ε_a^{(2)} ┼ε_b^{(1)})` for `a έΚι b` (only the transverse bond components fail to commute with `┼ε_a^{(3)}`, via the cyclic SU(2) commutators; `_right` is the symmetric right-endpoint companion `[┼ε_a┬╖┼ε_b, ┼ε_b^{(3)}]`, and `_eq_zero_of_ne` is the off-bond vanishing `[┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y, ┼ε_z^{(3)}] = 0` for `z έΙΚ {x,y}`); the building block of `[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]` and the double commutator in the infrared / f-sum-rule bound | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondSpinSOp3Commutator.lean` |
| `expectation_abs_le_manyBodyOperatorNormS` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`ExpectationNormBound.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the general norm-to-expectation bound έΑΦ for any `M` and a normalized state `╬ο` (`star ╬ο έυζά╡ξ ╬ο = 1`), `|έθρ╬ο, M ╬οέθσ.re| έΚν έΑΨMέΑΨ` (normalized-state form of `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul`); converts operator-norm bounds into expectation bounds in the FalkέΑΥBruch argument | `Quantum/SpinS/ExpectationNormBound.lean` |
| `transverseBondEnergy_expectation_abs_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): in a normalized state `╬ο`, the transverse bond energy expectation `|έθρ╬ο, (┼ε_a^{(1)}┼ε_b^{(1)} + ┼ε_a^{(2)}┼ε_b^{(2)}) ╬οέθσ.re| έΚν 2N┬▓` (the operator-norm bound + the normέΗΤexpectation bridge `abs_re_dotProduct_mulVec_le_norm_mul` + `έΑΨ╬οέΑΨ = 1`); summed over the `O(L)` adjacent bonds bounds the oscillator strength by `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/TransverseBondEnergyExpectation.lean` |
| `spinSDot_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`SingleBondCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the single-bond spin current `[┼ε_a┬╖┼ε_b, ┼ε_a^{(3)}]` has operator norm `έΚν 2N┬▓` (`έΑΨiέΑΨ = 1` + per-site norm bounds + submultiplicativity); the `O(1)` bound per bond, summed over `z`'s two incident bonds, bounds `έΑΨ[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]έΑΨ` by `O(1)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleBondCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `transverseBondEnergy_manyBodyOperatorNormS_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`TransverseBondEnergyNorm.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the transverse bond energy (per-bond oscillator strength `= έΙΤ[┼ε_a^{(3)},[┼ε_a┬╖┼ε_b,┼ε_a^{(3)}]]`) has operator norm `έΑΨ┼ε_a^{(1)}┼ε_b^{(1)} + ┼ε_a^{(2)}┼ε_b^{(2)}έΑΨ έΚν 2N┬▓` (per-site norm bound `έΑΨ┼ε^{(╬▒)}έΑΨ έΚν N` + submultiplicativity); summed over the `O(L)` adjacent bonds this bounds the oscillator strength by `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/TransverseBondEnergyNorm.lean` |
| `pair_double_commutator_eq_zero_of_ne` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`PairCommutatorVanish.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): off-pair vanishing `[┼ε_x^{(3)}, [─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]] = 0` for `x έΙΚ {zέΙΤ1, z, z+1}` έΑΦ since `[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]` is supported on `z`'s two incident bonds, `┼ε_x^{(3)}` commutes with it (Commute lemmas); hence the oscillator-strength double sum has only `O(L)` nonzero terms (three per `z`) | `Quantum/SpinS/PairCommutatorVanish.lean` |
| `no_long_range_order_1d_of_susceptibility` | **Cor 4.3 / conditional reduction** (`NoLongRangeOrderConditional.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the exact `╬╡`έΑΥ`╬┤` statement of Corollary 4.3 *modulo the susceptibility bound* έΑΦ if there is `C έΚξ 0` such that every normalized ground state of the zero-field ring has a potential `y` for `├Φ╬ο` with `Reέθρy,├Φ╬οέθσ έΚν C┬╖L`, then for every `╬╡ > 0` there is `LέΓΑ` beyond which every normalized ground state has `\|έθρ╬ο,├Φ┬▓╬οέθσ.re/L┬▓\| < ╬╡` (assembling the `O(L)` oscillator bound + susceptibility reduction + ground-state bridge + an Archimedean `╬╡`έΑΥ`╬┤`). This isolates the unconditional Cor 4.3 to the reflection-positivity infrared bound `Reέθρy,├Φ╬οέθσ έΚν C┬╖L`; the axiom `no_long_range_order_1d` remains until that bound is proven (PR #4848) | `Quantum/SpinS/NoLongRangeOrderConditional.lean` |
| `groundState_mulVec_eq_hermitianMinEigenvalue` | **Cor 4.3 / ground-state bridge** (`HermitianGroundStateEigenvalue.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): a ground-state eigenvalue equals the minimum eigenvalue έΑΦ for a Hermitian `H` and a normalized eigenvector `╬ο` (`H╬ο = EέΓΑέΑλ╬ο`) whose `EέΓΑ` has minimal real part over all eigenpairs, `H╬ο = (hermitianMinEigenvalue H)έΑλ╬ο` (`EέΓΑ.im = 0` by Hermiticity; `EέΓΑ.re` squeezed between the variational lower bound and the minimizing eigenvector); bridges the "energy-minimizing eigenpair" axiom hypothesis to the FalkέΑΥBruch `hermitianMinEigenvalue` interface (PR #4847) | `Quantum/SpinS/HermitianGroundStateEigenvalue.lean` |
| `staggeredOrder_sq_le_susceptibility` | **Cor 4.3 / FalkέΑΥBruch** (`StaggeredOrderSusceptibility.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the squared staggered order parameter is bounded by `O(L)` times the susceptibility έΑΦ for a ground state `╬ο` of the zero-field ring (eigenvalue `hermitianMinEigenvalue`) and a potential `y` for `├Φ╬ο` (`(─νέΙΤEέΓΑ)y = ├Φ╬ο`), `2(Reέθρ╬ο,├Φ┬▓╬οέθσ)┬▓ έΚν 12N┬│┬╖L┬╖Reέθρy,├Φ╬οέθσ` (FalkέΑΥBruch `2έθρ├Φ┬▓έθσ┬▓έΚνosc┬╖╧Θ` with the `O(L)` oscillator bound `oscέΚν12N┬│L` and `╧Θ=Reέθρy,├Φ╬οέθσέΚξ0` PSD); reduces Cor 4.3 to the susceptibility bound `╧Θ έΚν C┬╖L` (PR #4846) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSusceptibility.lean` |
| `oscillatorStrength_abs_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`OscillatorStrengthBound.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the f-sum-rule oscillator strength is `O(L)` έΑΦ in a normalized state `╬ο`, `|έθρ╬ο, [├Φ, [─ν, ├Φ]] ╬οέθσ.re| έΚν 12N┬│┬╖L` (assembling the double-sum form, the off-pair vanishing restricting to three nonzero `z` per `x`, the uniform per-pair norm `έΚν 4N┬│`, and the normέΗΤexpectation bridge); the `O(L)` numerator of the ground-state FalkέΑΥBruch bound (PR #4845) | `Quantum/SpinS/OscillatorStrengthBound.lean` |
| `pair_double_commutator_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`PairCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): each summand of the oscillator strength is bounded uniformly in `L` έΑΦ `έΑΨ[┼ε_x^{(3)}, [─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]]έΑΨ έΚν 4N┬│` (commutator norm `έΚν 2έΑΨ┼ε^{(3)}έΑΨ┬╖έΑΨ[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]έΑΨ` with `έΑΨ┼ε^{(3)}έΑΨ έΚν N/2` and `έΑΨ[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]έΑΨ έΚν 4N┬▓`); combined with off-pair vanishing gives `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/PairCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_norm_le` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorNorm.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): since `[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]` localizes to `z`'s two incident bonds, its operator norm is `O(1)` έΑΦ `έΑΨ[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]έΑΨ έΚν 4N┬▓` (sum of the two single-bond spin-current norms `έΚν 2N┬▓` each); the length-independent locality making the oscillator strength `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorNorm.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3_closed` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorClosed.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): closed form of the spin-current divergence on a ring `L έΚξ 2` έΑΦ `[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}] = [┼ε_z┬╖┼ε_{z+1}, ┼ε_z^{(3)}] + [┼ε_{zέΙΤ1}┬╖┼ε_z, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]` (the bond sum collapses to `z`'s two incident bonds via `Finset.sum_pair`, every other bond commuting); plus `finRotate_ne_self` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorClosed.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_commutator_onSiteS_spinSOp3` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingHamiltonianCommutatorBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ring Hamiltonian commutator with `┼ε_z^{(3)}` as a bond sum `[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}] = ╬μ_x [┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_{x+1}, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]` (bond-sum form + commutator distribution); since each bond commutator vanishes off `z`, this localizes the spin-current divergence to `z`'s two incident bonds | `Quantum/SpinS/RingHamiltonianCommutatorBondSum.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_eq_bondSum_general` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`RingBondSumGeneral.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian on `Fin L` of any length as a bond sum `─ν = ╬μ_x ┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_{finRotate L x}` (directed coupling collapses the defining double sum); the form used to localize `[─ν, ┼ε_z^{(3)}]` to the two bonds incident to `z` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingBondSumGeneral.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_double_commutator_dotProduct` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`OscillatorStrengthForm.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the oscillator strength as a double sum of two-site expectations `έθρ╬ο,[├Φ,[─ν,├Φ]]╬οέθσ = ╬μ_x ╬μ_z ╬╡_x ╬╡_z έθρ╬ο,[┼ε_x^{(3)},[─ν,┼ε_z^{(3)}]]╬οέθσ` (mulVec/dotProduct linearity over the operator double sum), whose summands vanish off adjacent sites έΑΦ the `O(L)` oscillator strength | `Quantum/SpinS/OscillatorStrengthForm.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_double_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`StaggeredOrderDoubleCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the f-sum-rule double commutator as a double site sum `[├Φ,[─ν,├Φ]] = ╬μ_x ╬μ_z ╬╡_x ╬╡_z [┼ε_x^{(3)},[─ν,┼ε_z^{(3)}]]` (order-operator commutator distribution applied twice; `_commutator'` reversed variant), whose summands vanish off adjacent sites so the apparent `O(L┬▓)` collapses to `O(L)` | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderDoubleCommutator.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_commutator` | **Cor 4.3 / f-sum-rule** (`StaggeredOrderCommutator.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the staggered order operator commutator distributes over sites `[├Φ, C] = ╬μ_x ╬╡_x [┼ε_x^{(3)}, C]`; applied twice it expands the f-sum-rule double commutator `[├Φ,[─ν,├Φ]]` into a double site sum, whose terms vanish off adjacent sites (reducing the naive `O(L┬▓)` count to `O(L)`) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderCommutator.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_dotProduct` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the ground-state quadratic-form expansion `έθρ╬ο, ├Φ┬▓ ╬οέθσ = ╬μ_x ╬μ_y ╬╡_x ╬╡_y έθρ╬ο, ┼ε_x^{(3)} ┼ε_y^{(3)} ╬οέθσ`, rewriting the order parameter in the exact form `star ╬ο έυζά╡ξ (├Φ┬▓).mulVec ╬ο` of the Corollary through the two-point functions the RP machinery bounds | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquareForm.lean` |
| `staggeredOrderOpS_sq_eq_sum` | **Cor 4.3 bridge** (`StaggeredOrderSquare.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Corollary 4.3): the staggered order operator squared expands as the two-point double sum `├Φ┬▓ = ╬μ_x ╬μ_y ╬╡_x ╬╡_y ┼ε_x^{(3)} ┼ε_y^{(3)}` έΑΦ the bridge from the order parameter to the two-point functions bounded by the reflection-positivity / infrared machinery (the squared order parameter per `L┬▓` is controlled by the total two-point sum, only `O(L)` in one dimension) | `Quantum/SpinS/StaggeredOrderSquare.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz_abs` | **RP infra layer 40** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the square-root (usable) form of the RP CauchyέΑΥSchwarz έΑΦ `|Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B)| έΚν έΙγ(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A))┬╖έΙγ(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B))` for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` and left-supported `A,B`, by taking roots in the chessboard inequality (diagonals nonnegative); the form iterated in the DLS spreading argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarzSqrt.lean` |
| `ringTranslateConj` / `chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of` | **RP infra layer 39** (`RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the ring translation `╧Ε(X) = TέΑι┬╖X┬╖T` is a trace-preserving `*`-homomorphism (`ringTranslateConj_mul`/`_trace`), so any chessboard estimate transports unchanged to the translation-conjugated data `(╧ΕM, ╧Ε(╬╕A), ╧ΕB)` (`chessboard_ringTranslateConj_of`) έΑΦ the weight `╧ΕM` is the Marshall gauge relative to the shifted reflection plane; carries the single-axis chessboard to every bond toward Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboardTransport.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_gibbs` / `thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation` | **RP infra layer 38** (`RingTranslationGibbs.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): unlike the half-system gauge, the physical ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_heisenbergHamiltonianS`), so the physical Gibbs operator `exp(έΙΤ╬▓┬╖─ν)` is translation invariant (`chainTranslation_conj_gibbs`) and every physical thermal average is translation covariant `έθρTέΑιATέθσ = έθρAέθσ` (`thermalAverageReS_chainTranslation`); the thermal-state translation symmetry for the momentum-space infrared analysis | `Quantum/SpinS/RingTranslationGibbs.lean` |
| `chainTranslation_conj_ringReflectionThetaS_onSiteS` / `_spinSDot` | **RP infra layer 37** (`RingReflectionTranslation.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): translation covariance of the fixed-axis reflection map `╬╕` έΑΦ conjugating `╬╕(onSiteS x A)` (resp. `╬╕(┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y)`) by the ring translation `chainTranslationOp` shifts the reflection axis, sending the sites to `finRotate (ringReflect x)` (resp. both endpoints); the foundation for reflections across every bond, needed to spread the single-axis chessboard toward DLS Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTranslation.lean` |
| `ringDLSGibbs_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 36** (`RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete chessboard inequality for the gauged ring Gibbs weight έΑΦ combining its reflection positivity (Gibbs capstone) and reflection invariance (`╬╕M=M`), for `╬▓ έΚξ 0` and left-supported `A,B`, `(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B))┬▓ έΚν Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B)` with `M = exp(έΙΤ╬▓┬╖toHamiltonian)`; the DLS factorization estimate for the thermal state of the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_toHamiltonian` / `_ringDLSGibbs` | **RP infra layer 35** (`RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauged DLS Hamiltonian is reflection-invariant `╬╕(toHamiltonian) = toHamiltonian` (the `H_L + ╬╕(H_L)` part is swapped to itself; each crossing bond `╬╕(crossBondInteractionS x) = crossBondInteractionS x` since `┼ε_x^╬▒` commutes with its right-half reflection `╬╕(┼ε_x^╬▒)`), hence the gauged ring Gibbs weight `exp(έΙΤ╬▓┬╖toHamiltonian)` is reflection-invariant έΑΦ supplying the `╬╕M = M` hypothesis for the chessboard/real-diagonal lemmas | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThetaInvariance.lean` |
| `rpForm_re_symm` / `rpTraceWeight_reflection_step` | **RP infra layer 34** (`RingReflectionChessboard.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): for a reflection-invariant RP trace weight `M` (`╬╕M=M`) and left-supported `A,B`, the off-diagonal form value is symmetric `Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B) = Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖A)` (since `A` commutes with the right-half `╬╕B`); feeding this into the CauchyέΑΥSchwarz discriminant collapses the cross term, giving the chessboard bound `(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B))┬▓ έΚν Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B)` έΑΦ the DLS factorization step underlying Gaussian domination | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionChessboard.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_trace` / `rpForm_diag_im_zero` | **RP infra layer 33** (`RingReflectionTraceReality.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection map conjugates the trace `Tr(╬╕X) = conj(Tr X)` (reindex the diagonal by the involutive site reflection), so for a reflection-invariant weight `M` (`╬╕M = M`) and left-supported `A` the RP-form diagonal `Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)` is real (`╬╕┬▓=id`, `*`-homomorphism, `A` commutes with `╬╕A`); together with the CauchyέΑΥSchwarz this makes `(A,B)έΗοTr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B)` a genuine positive-semidefinite form with real diagonal | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionTraceReality.lean` |
| `rpTraceWeight_cauchySchwarz` | **RP infra layer 32** (`RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): a reflection-positive trace weight `M` makes `(A,B) έΗο Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B)` a positive-semidefinite sesquilinear form on the left subalgebra; expanding `0 έΚν Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕(AέΙΤtB)┬╖(AέΙΤtB))` as a nonnegative real quadratic in `t` and taking its discriminant (`discrim_le_zero`) gives `(Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖B) + Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖A))┬▓ έΚν 4┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕A┬╖A)┬╖Re Tr(M┬╖╬╕B┬╖B)` έΑΦ the workhorse for the DLS infrared bound | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionCauchySchwarz.lean` |
| `thermalAverageReS_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 31** (`RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge is a similarity transform, so it leaves the partition function invariant (`thermalPartitionFnS_toHamiltonian_eq`) and transports thermal averages έΑΦ the physical ring Heisenberg canonical average of `A` equals the reflection-positive DLS canonical average of `rightGauge ┬╖ A ┬╖ rightGaugeInv`; the bridge carrying RP bounds back to the physical ring antiferromagnet | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionThermalTransfer.lean` |
| `exp_neg_smul_toHamiltonian_eq` | **RP infra layer 30** (`RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation by the right-half gauge commutes with the matrix exponential (`Matrix.exp_units_conj`), so the physical ring Heisenberg Gibbs operator `exp(έΙΤ╬▓ ─ν)` and the reflection-positive DLS Gibbs operator `exp(έΙΤ╬▓ ┬╖ ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian)` are unitarily equivalent έΑΦ physical thermal averages become DLS thermal averages of gauge-transformed observables | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsGauge.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 29** (`RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge conjugates the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian into the reflection-positive DLS Hamiltonian έΑΦ `rightGauge ┬╖ heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) ┬╖ rightGaugeInv = ringDLSDecomposition.toHamiltonian` (bond Hamiltonians fixed, the two crossing bonds become the negated gauged crossing interactions); the unitary equivalence bridging the physical ring antiferromagnet to its reflection-positive form | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGaugeAssembly.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian` / `_theta_*` | **RP infra layer 28** (`RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): summing the bond-invariance lemmas, the right-half gauge fixes both the left bond Hamiltonian (`rightGauge_conj_ringLeftHamiltonian`) and the reflected right bond Hamiltonian `╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian)` (`rightGauge_conj_theta_ringLeftHamiltonian`) έΑΦ only the two crossing bonds are affected | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionNonCrossConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_crossBond` | **RP infra layer 27** (`RingReflectionCrossConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the gauge image of a crossing bond is the negated gauged crossing interaction. For a left site `x < n` (reflection `r x` a right site), `rightGauge ┬╖ (┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_{r x}) ┬╖ rightGaugeInv = έΙΤcrossBondInteractionS x` έΑΦ the left endpoint untouched, the right endpoint gauged (flipping `┼ε^1`/`┼ε^3` signs), matching `crossBondInteractionS_eq`. | `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left`/`_right` | **RP infra layer 26** (`RingReflectionBondConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half gauge fixes every non-crossing bond. `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_left` (left bond, both sites `< n`: untouched) and `rightGauge_conj_spinSDot_right` (right bond, both sites gauged: the `┼ε^1`/`┼ε^3` sign flips cancel in the product, `┼ε^2` unchanged). Only the two crossing bonds acquire the DLS sign flip. Helpers `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS_left`/`_right` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondConj.lean` |
| `rightGauge_conj_mul` (gauge homomorphism) | **RP infra layer 25** (`RingReflectionGaugeConj.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): conjugation `X έΗο rightGauge┬╖X┬╖rightGaugeInv` by the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge is an algebra homomorphism έΑΦ `rightGauge_conj_mul` (over products, via `rightGaugeInv_mul_rightGauge`), `rightGauge_conj_add`, `rightGauge_conj_smul`, `rightGauge_conj_sum`. With `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` these compute the gauge image of any operator built from single-site operators (the ring Hamiltonian in particular) | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGaugeConj.lean` |
| `AxisTwoPiRotS` / `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` | **RP infra layer 24** (`RingReflectionGauge.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right-half DLS/Marshall gauge. `manyBodyTensorS_conj_onSiteS_dep` έΑΦ site-dependent tensor conjugation `(έΛΩ_i W_i)(onSiteS z A)(έΛΩ_i Winv_i) = onSiteS z (W_z┬╖A┬╖Winv_z)` (for `W_i┬╖Winv_i = 1`). `AxisTwoPiRotS` έΑΦ the single-site ╧Α-rotation around axis 2 as an interface (following `AxisSwapUnitaryS`; fields `U ┼ε^1 UέΒ╗┬╣ = έΙΤ┼ε^1`, `U ┼ε^2 UέΒ╗┬╣ = ┼ε^2`, `U ┼ε^3 UέΒ╗┬╣ = έΙΤ┼ε^3`, non-vacuous at spin-1/2). `rightGauge`/`rightGaugeInv` έΑΦ the gauge unitary applying `U` on the right sites only; `rightGaugeInv_mul_rightGauge` (invertible) and `rightGauge_conj_onSiteS` (conjugation acts as `U┬╖A┬╖UέΒ╗┬╣` on right sites, trivially on left). | `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_ungauged_dls` | **RP infra layer 23** (`RingReflectionUngaugedDLS.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ungauged DLS split of the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian. | `ringRightBondSum_eq_theta` | **RP infra layer 22** (`RingReflectionRightEqTheta.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the right bond Hamiltonian equals the reflection of the left bond Hamiltonian. | `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_bond_split` | **RP infra layer 21** (`RingReflectionBondSplit.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian split into left, right, and crossing bonds. The bonds `(x, x+1)` of `Fin (2n)` fall into four disjoint classes by `x.val` (left `x έΚν nέΙΤ2`, crossing `x = nέΙΤ1`, right `n έΚν x έΚν 2nέΙΤ2`, crossing `x = 2nέΙΤ1`); summing the bond form over this partition (`bondTerm_four_way` per-bond decomposition + `Finset.sum_add_distrib` + `sum_ite_val_eq`) gives `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) = ringLeftHamiltonian + ringRightBondSum + ┼ε_{nέΙΤ1}┬╖┼ε_n + ┼ε_{2nέΙΤ1}┬╖┼ε_0`. Identifying `ringRightBondSum` with `╬╕(ringLeftHamiltonian)` and the crossing bonds with the gauged interaction is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionBondSplit.lean` |
| `ringDLSDecomposition_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` | **RP infra layer 20** (`RingReflectionConcreteGibbs.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the concrete gauged ring antiferromagnet has a reflection-positive Gibbs weight. Identification of `H` with the physical ungauged Hamiltonian (bond-split assembly + gauge unitary) is the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionConcreteGibbs.lean` |
| `ringReflectionThetaS_ringLeftHamiltonian` | **RP infra layer 19** (`RingReflectionRightBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflected left bond Hamiltonian is the right bond Hamiltonian. This is the right part `╬╕(H_L)` of the ring DLS decomposition | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRightBondSum.lean` |
| `ringLeftHamiltonian_eq_leftBondSum` | **RP infra layer 18** (`RingReflectionLeftBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the left-half bond Hamiltonian as a sum over left bonds. Collapsing the left coupling's double sum, `ringLeftHamiltonian n N = έΙΣ_x [if x+1 < n then ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_{x+1} else 0]` έΑΦ only the bonds `(x, x+1)` entirely inside `{0,έΑο,nέΙΤ1}` survive (helpers `ringLeftCoupling_succ_of_lt`, `ringLeftCoupling_eq_zero_of_ne`, `ringLeftCoupling_eq_zero_of_not_lt`). | `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCoupling_eq_bondSum` | **RP infra layer 17** (`RingReflectionBondSum.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the ring Heisenberg Hamiltonian as a sum over nearest-neighbor bonds. The directed coupling `ringCoupling (2n)` is `1` exactly on successor pairs `(x, x+1 mod 2n)`, so the double sum collapses (`Finset.sum_eq_single`) to `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n)) N = έΙΣ_x ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_{ringBondSucc x}` (`ringBondSucc x = x+1 mod 2n`). | `ringLeftHamiltonian_supportedOnLeft` | **RP infra layer 16** (`RingReflectionLeftHamiltonian.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the left part `H_L` of the ring DLS decomposition is left-supported. `SupportedOnLeftS.sum` (left-supportedness closed under finite sums); `heisenbergHamiltonianS_supportedOnLeft` (a Heisenberg Hamiltonian whose coupling `J x y` vanishes unless both sites are in the left half is left-supported, being `έΙΣ_{x,y} J x y ┬╖ ┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y` with each surviving term left-supported); `ringLeftCoupling` (the ring nearest-neighbor coupling restricted to left-half sites); and `ringLeftHamiltonian = heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringLeftCoupling n) N`, the concrete `H_L` that feeds `ringCrossingRPDecomposition`. | `ring_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (ring instance) | **RP infra layer 15** (`RingReflectionRingInstance.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition of the gauged ring antiferromagnet. The two crossing bonds of `Fin (2n)` are `(nέΙΤ1,n)` and `(2nέΙΤ1,0)`, reflected from left sites `nέΙΤ1` and `0`; `ringCrossingRPDecomposition` packages (for an arbitrary left-supported `H_L`) the `RPDecomposition` whose crossing bonds are the single-site spin operators at sites `0` and `nέΙΤ1`, with `ringCrossingRPDecomposition_interaction` identifying its interaction as `crossBondInteractionS 0 + crossBondInteractionS (nέΙΤ1)` and `ringCrossingRPDecomposition_toHamiltonian` giving `toHamiltonian = H_L + ╬╕(H_L) έΙΤ (crossing)`. Then `ring_gibbs_rpTraceWeight` concludes (via the Gibbs capstone) that the gauged ring Gibbs weight `exp(έΙΤ╬▓┬╖(H_L+╬╕(H_L)έΙΤcrossing))` is a reflection-positive trace weight (`╬▓ έΚξ 0`). Also `spinSDot_supportedOnLeft` (a left-half bond is left-supported). The concrete left-bond `H_L` and the unitary equivalence to `heisenbergHamiltonianS (ringCoupling (2n))` are the next step | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRingInstance.lean` |
| `RPDecomposition.gibbs_rpTraceWeight` (**Gibbs RP capstone**) | **RP infra layer 14** (`RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): **full Gibbs reflection positivity** έΑΦ for a reflection-positive decomposition `H = H_L + ╬╕(H_L) έΙΤ D` (`RPDecomposition`) and `╬▓ έΚξ 0`, the Gibbs weight `exp(έΙΤ╬▓┬╖H)` is a reflection-positive trace weight. The DysonέΑΥLiebέΑΥSimon Trotter assembly: `e^{έΙΤ╬▓H} = e^{A+B}` (`A = έΙΤ╬▓┬╖(H_L+╬╕H_L)`, `B = ╬▓┬╖D`), via the Lie product formula `e^{A+B} = lim_m (e^{A/m}┬╖e^{B/m})^m` (`LieProduct.lieProductFormula`); each factor is consumed by an accumulating RP trace weight έΑΦ the kinetic factor `e^{A/m} = exp(Y+╬╕Y)` is cone-representable (`coneRep_exp_add` + `mul_coneRep_right`) and the interaction factor `e^{B/m}` is the exp of a cone-representable operator (`mul_exp_coneRep_right`) έΑΦ then induction over the power and `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`. Helpers: `realSmul_add_theta`, `RPDecomposition.interaction_coneRep`. This is the reflection positivity at the heart of the DLS / Shastry argument | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsCapstone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_exp_coneRep_right` | **RP infra layer 13** (`RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): an RP trace weight absorbs the exponential of a cone-representable operator. If `M` is RP and `P` is `RPTraceConeRepS`, then `M ┬╖ exp P` is RP έΑΦ the partial sums `έΙΣ_{k<m}(k!)έΒ╗┬╣Pά╡Π` are cone-representable (`pow`/`smul_nonneg`/`add`), so each `M┬╖(partial sum)` is RP (`mul_coneRep_right`), and `M┬╖exp P` is their limit (continuity of left multiplication + `RPTraceWeightS.tendsto`). This consumes the interaction Gibbs factor `e^{╬▓D/m}` in the Trotter expansion | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionMulExpConeRep.lean` |
| `coneRep_exp_add` (kinetic factor) | **RP infra layer 12** (`RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the kinetic Gibbs factor is cone-representable. For left-supported `X`, `exp(X + ╬╕ X) = exp X ┬╖ ╬╕(exp X) = ╬╕(exp X) ┬╖ exp X` (`ringReflectionThetaS_exp_add_eq`, via `exp_add_of_commute` since `X` and `╬╕(X)` commute, `╬╕(exp X) = exp(╬╕ X)`, and `mul_theta_comm`); the right-hand side is a single cone generator `╬╕(L)┬╖L` (`L = exp X` left-supported), so `coneRep_exp_add` gives `RPTraceConeRepS (exp(X + ╬╕ X))`. This is the kinetic building block (`e^{έΙΤ╬▓HέΓΑ/m}`) the Trotter expansion of `e^{έΙΤ╬▓H}` consumes | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionKineticConeRep.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right` (cone closure) | **RP infra layer 11** (`RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the reflection-positive trace weights form a convex cone έΑΦ `RPTraceWeightS.zero`/`one` (the identity is RP, the `╬▓=0` trace cone), `add`, `smul_nonneg`, `sum` (closed under finite sums) έΑΦ and `RPTraceWeightS.mul_coneRep_right`: an RP trace weight times a cone-representable operator (`RPTraceConeRepS`) is again RP (since `M┬╖έΙΣcά╡λ╬╕(Cά╡λ)Cά╡λ = έΙΣcά╡λ(M┬╖╬╕(Cά╡λ)Cά╡λ)`, each RP by the cone-generator closure, summed). This is the algebraic engine that accumulates reflection positivity factor-by-factor in the Trotter expansion of `e^{έΙΤ╬▓H}` | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPWeightCone.lean` |
| `RPTraceWeightS.mul_weightGen_right` / `RPTraceWeightS.weightGen_mul_left` | **RP infra layer 10** (`RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): closure of reflection-positive trace weights under cone generators `╬╕(L)┬╖L` (`L` left-supported). The naive "conjugation" closure `M έΗο L┬╖M┬╖╬╕(L)` is **false** (the homomorphism order `╬╕(LA)=╬╕(L)╬╕(A)` clashes with the test operator `B=AL`, and left-supported operators do not commute); the correct closure multiplies by a cone generator: `M┬╖(╬╕(L)┬╖L)` is RP (substitute the test operator `A έΗο L┬╖A`, using `L┬╖╬╕(A)=╬╕(A)┬╖L` and `╬╕(L)┬╖╬╕(A)=╬╕(L┬╖A)`) and `(╬╕(L)┬╖L)┬╖M` is RP (substitute `A έΗο A┬╖L`, via trace cyclicity). These are the kinetic-factor building blocks of the full DysonέΑΥLiebέΑΥSimon Gibbs reflection positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionGibbsRP.lean` |
| `crossBondInteractionS` / `RPDecomposition` | **RP infra layer 9** (`RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean`, Tasaki ┬π4.1, toward Theorem 4.2): the DLS decomposition data and the crossing-bond interaction. `crossBondInteractionS x = ╬μ_╬▒ ┼ε_x^╬▒┬╖╬╕(┼ε_x^╬▒)` (left site `x`), with the **gauge identity** `crossBondInteractionS x = ┼ε_x^1 ┼ε_{r x}^1 έΙΤ ┼ε_x^2 ┼ε_{r x}^2 + ┼ε_x^3 ┼ε_{r x}^3` (the lone `έΙΤ` on the `┼ε^2` term is the sign flipped by the DLS/Marshall gauge έΑΦ a ╧Α-rotation around axis 2 on the right half έΑΦ making all three components contribute the same negative reflection-positive sign). Each `onSiteS x ┼ε^╬▒` (`x<n`) is left-supported; `crossBondInteractionS_exp_rpTraceWeight` shows `exp(t┬╖crossBondInteractionS x)` (`tέΚξ0`) is a reflection-positive trace weight (via `mul_theta_comm` + the interaction-exponential cone). The abstract `RPDecomposition` structure (`H_L + ╬╕(H_L) έΙΤ ╬μ_b C_b┬╖╬╕(C_b)`, all `C_b` left-supported) packages the DLS form, with `interaction_exp_rpTraceWeight` giving the Gibbs interaction factor's reflection positivity | `Quantum/SpinS/RingReflectionRPDecomposition.lean` |
| `no_long_range_order_1d` | **Corollary 4.3** (┬π4.1, AXIOM; eq. (4.1.11)): absence of LRO in 1D. For the zero-field 1D AFM Heisenberg ring, the squared staggered order parameter per site vanishes in the thermodynamic limit `lim_{LέΗΣέΙη} έθρ╬ο_GS\|(├Φ_L^(3)/L)┬▓\|╬ο_GSέθσ = 0` (╬╡έΑΥ╬┤ form). In the book a corollary of Thm 4.2 + Thm 3.2 + MLM; runs through the deferred double-limit form of Thm 3.2, so recorded as a documented axiom (in scope) | `Quantum/SpinS/ShastryNoSSB.lean` |
| `staggeredCasimirOpS` / `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` | **Theorem 4.4** (┬π4.1, ShenέΑΥQiuέΑΥTian, PROVED axiom-free; eqs. (4.1.12)έΑΥ(4.1.13)): ferrimagnetic LRO on an asymmetric bipartite lattice. The SU(2)-invariant squared staggered operator `(├Φ_╬δ)┬▓ = ╬μ_{x,y} ╬╡_x ╬╡_y ┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y`, and the bound `S┬▓(\|A\|έΙΤ\|B\|)┬▓ έΚν έθρ╬ο_GS\|(├Φ_╬δ)┬▓\|╬ο_GSέθσ` for any normalized ground state of the connected bipartite AFM model (same hypotheses as Thm 2.3). **PROVED axiom-free** (Issues #4604/#4617 CLOSED; book proof = chain (4.1.16): (4.1.15) cross-term + LiebέΑΥMattis Thm 2.3 total spin + Casimir). PR1 (#4605) formalizes the **operator-algebra core** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROComponentAlgebra.lean`: `staggeredTransverseCasimirOpS` (transverse part of `(├Φ_╬δ)┬▓`), `(├Φ_╬δ)┬▓ = transverse + (├Φ_╬δ^(3))┬▓`, the PSD drop `έθρtransverseέθσ έΚν έθρ(├Φ_╬δ)┬▓έθσ`, `(┼ε_tot^(╬▒))┬▓ = ╬μ_{x,y} ┼ε_x^(╬▒)┼ε_y^(╬▒)`, the Casimir identity `╬μ_{x,y}(┼ε_x^(1)┼ε_y^(1)+┼ε_x^(2)┼ε_y^(2)) = (┼ε_tot)┬▓ έΙΤ (┼ε_tot^(3))┬▓`, and the M=0 reduction `έθρ(┼ε_tot)┬▓έθσ = έθρtransverseέθσ`. PR2 (#4606) adds the **cross-term inequality (4.1.15)** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTermCore.lean` (transverse correlation + ladder positivity + per-pair sign) + `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCrossTerm.lean` (the summed cross-term inequality, split for build speed): the transverse correlation `έθρ╬ο,T_xy ╬οέθσ` (`T_xy = ┬╜(┼ε_x^+┼ε_y^- + ┼ε_x^-┼ε_y^+)`), its non-positivity for cross-sublattice pairs on a Marshall-positive sector ground state (`marshallSignS┬╖c`, `c>0`), and the summed dominance `έθρ╬ο,(╬μ_{x,y}T_xy)╬οέθσ.re έΚν έθρ╬ο,staggeredTransverseCasimirOpS ╬οέθσ.re`. PR3 (#4607) adds the **total-spin value** in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROTotalSpin.lean` (coupling-agnostic via a `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` hypothesis): `exists_centered_groundState_predictedCasimir_of_tasaki23` produces a centered (`┼ε┬│_tot ╬οέΒ░=0`) Marshall ground state with `(┼ε_tot)┬▓ ╬οέΒ░ = S_tot(S_tot+1)┬╖╬οέΒ░` (`S_tot=(\|A\|έΙΤ\|B\|)N/2`), reusing `tasaki23_pf_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_sector` + the extremal-Casimir machinery. PR4 (#4608) proves the **capstone** `ferrimagnetic_lro_completeBipartite_centered` in `Quantum/SpinS/FerrimagneticLROCapstone.lean`: assembling chain (4.1.16) on the centered ground state, `(N/2)┬▓(\|A\|έΙΤ\|B\|)┬▓┬╖έθρ╬οέΒ░,╬οέΒ░έθσ.re έΚν έθρ╬οέΒ░,(├Φ_╬δ)┬▓╬οέΒ░έθσ.re` (existence form, coupling-agnostic via `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3`; sorry-free, axiom-clean, independent of the `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom; that axiom is now itself proved in UniversalFinal). | `raiseLowerReachableS_of_connected` | **Connected-graph spin-config reachability** (┬π2.5, PROVED; Issue #4609, prereq for the connected MarshallέΑΥLiebέΑΥMattis extension / Thm 4.4 axiom): on a connected graph `G`, any two equal-magnetization configs are raise/lower reachable έΑΦ `G.Connected έΗΤ magSumS ╧Δ = magSumS ╧Δ' έΗΤ RaiseLowerReachableS G ╧Δ ╧Δ'`. This is the ladder step that makes `(├Φ_╬δ)┬▓`'s expectation constant across the spin-`S_tot` multiplet; **PR7 (#4616)** iterates it to `(┼εέΒ╗_tot)^k` (`su2_expectationRatioRe_ladder_iterate_invariant`, `Quantum/SpinS/SU2ExpectationLadderIterated.lean`), so every member of the lowering tower shares the centered ratio. The **stated** `shenQiuTian_ferrimagnetic_lro` axiom (universal over *all* ground states) now remains pending only the final assembly of ingredient (b): the ground-state weight decomposition + the **ground-eigenspace classification** (any global-min eigenvector's nonzero `┼ε┬│`-weight components lie in the admissible sectors έΑΦ needs strict energy separation, the hard remaining gate) (#4604). **PR8 (#4618)** adds step 1 of that dimension bound: `heisenbergHamiltonianSMatrixOnMagSector_finrank_le_one_of_marshall_positive_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/ConnectedSectorFinrankLeOne.lean`) έΑΦ connected-coupling per-sector ground-state uniqueness (`finrank έΚν 1`), the connected-irreducibility mirror of the complete-graph lemma (Issue #4617). **PR9 (#4619)** adds step 3: `weightPreserving_expectation_eq_sum_sector` (`Quantum/SpinS/WeightPreservingExpectationSum.lean`) έΑΦ for any operator commuting with `┼ε┬│_tot`, `έθρ╬ο,O╬οέθσ = ╬μ_M έθρ╬ο_M,O╬ο_Mέθσ` over the `┼ε┬│`-weight sector components (cross terms vanish by `┼ε┬│_tot`-eigenvalue orthogonality; no Hermiticity of `O` needed), the block-diagonal decomposition for `(├Φ_╬δ)┬▓`'s expectation. **PR10 (#4620)** clears the hard gate (step 2): `tasaki23_strict_hOutside_of_connected` (`Quantum/SpinS/StrictHOutsideFerrimagnetic.lean`; (5.1.1)έΑΥ(5.2.5)): **off-diagonal long-range order (ODLRO) of hard-core bosons at half filling**. Hard-core bosons (`uέΗΣέΙη` limit of the bosonic Hubbard model, eq. 5.1.4) are **equivalent to the S=1/2 XY model** (eq. 5.1.5) via the identification `├λ_xέΑιέΗΦ(έΙΤ1)^x ┼ε_x^+`, `n╠Γ_xέΗΦ┼ε_x^(3)+┬╜`, `N╠ΓέΗΦ┼ε_tot^(3)+L^d/2` (eqs. 5.1.6έΑΥ5.1.7); half filling `╧Β=┬╜` έΗΦ the `┼ε_tot^(3)=0` sector. `xyHamiltonianS d L` = the XY Hamiltonian (XXZ at ╬╗=D=0, N=1). The bosonic order operators `├Φ_L^(1,2)` (eqs. 5.2.2/5.2.4) are the staggered XY-plane spin order operators. For `d έΚξ 2` (`hd : 2 έΚν d`) and half filling, ODLRO holds with `qέΓΑ > 0` (depending only on `d`): every ground state `╬ο_GS` (eigenvector + min-energy, `╬οέΚι0`, `┼ε_tot^(3) ╬ο=0`) of `xyHamiltonianS` satisfies `έθρ(├Φ_L^(╬▒))┬▓έθσ/έθρ╬ο,╬οέθσ/(L^d)┬▓ έΚξ qέΓΑ` for ╬▒=1,2 (`╬▒:Fin 3, ╬▒έΚι2`) and all large even `L` (eq. 5.2.5; the squared operator `├Φ_L^(╬▒)/L^d` gives the `(L^d)┬▓` denominator, the intensive ODLRO density). Half filling is essential; the ground state shows LRO **without** SSB (eq. 5.2.8). Proof: reflection positivity (KennedyέΑΥLiebέΑΥShastry, KuboέΑΥKishi) | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |

The phase-`╬╕` **coherent state** `╬η_╬╕ = (2M_max+1)^{έΙΤ1/2} ╬μ_{M=έΙΤM_max}^{M_max} e^{έΙΤiM╬╕} ╬Υ_M` (eq. 5.3.5; `╬Υ_M` = normalized tower state), with a slow window `M_max(L)` (`IsSlowBECWindow`: monotone, έΗΤ έΙη, `έΚν CέΓΒL^{d/2}` eventually). Double limit in eventual-`╬╡'` form (outer `╬╡έΗΥ0`, inner `LέΗΣέΙη`); GS family given (unique by MLM). The two condensates are coherently coupled (entangled). Proof: KomaέΑΥTasaki. **Completes Tasaki Chapter 5** | `Quantum/SpinS/BoseEinsteinCondensate.lean` |

(6.2.1)έΑΥ(6.2.19)): for a **half-odd-integer** spin chain the gap is `O(1/L)`. **PROVED**, axiom-free, in `LiebSchultzMattisProof.lean`): `έθρ╬ο_LSM,─ν╬ο_LSMέθσ/έθρ╬ο_LSM,╬ο_LSMέθσ έΙΤ E_GS έΚν 8╧Α┬▓S┬▓/L` for any `S` έΑΦ via the per-bond symmetrised twist identity `├δέΑι(┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y)├δ + ├δ(┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y)├δέΑι έΙΤ 2(┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y) = (2cos(╬╕_xέΙΤ╬╕_y)έΙΤ2)┬╖XY_{xy}` (the longitudinal `┼ε┬│┼ε┬│` part and the imaginary current cancel in the `┬▒╬╕` average), `cos(╬╕_xέΙΤ╬╕_y)=cos(2╧Α/L)` on every bond, `|έθρXY_bέθσ.re| έΚν 2S┬▓έΑΨ╬οέΑΨ┬▓`, and `1έΙΤcos(2╧Α/L) έΚν (2╧Α/L)┬▓/2` summed over the `L` bonds; **Lemma 6.2** (`lsm_ground_twist_orthogonal`, eq. 6.2.18; **PROVED**, axiom-free, in `LiebSchultzMattisOrthogonality.lean`): for `N` odd (half-odd-integer `S`) and the unique GS (`┼ε_tot^(3)=0`, `huniq` uniqueness), `έθρ╬ο_GS,╬ο_LSMέθσ=0` έΑΦ via the cyclic **translation operator** `T╠Γ` (config-shift permutation): `T╠Γ` commutes with `─ν` so the unique GS is a translation eigenvector `T╠Γ╬ο_GS=c╬ο_GS` (`|c|=1`), and the diagonal transformation law `T╠ΓέΑι├δ_LSM T╠Γ = (έΙΤ1)^{2S} ├δ_LSM e^{i(2╧Α/L)┼ε_tot^(3)}` (eq. 6.2.17, reduced to a per-config `lsmPhase` identity with a `2╧Α` boundary wrap giving `(έΙΤ1)^N`) forces `έθρ╬ο_GS,╬ο_LSMέθσ=(έΙΤ1)^Nέθρ╬ο_GS,╬ο_LSMέθσ`, vanishing for `N` odd; the generic CourantέΑΥFischer second-eigenvalue bound `hermitian_second_eigenvalue_variational` (`HermitianSecondEigenvalue.lean`) with the existence of an eigenvalue above the ground line (`hermitian_exists_eigenvalue_gt`, `HermitianGapExists.lean`) defines `EέΓΒ`, and Lemma 6.1 (trial energy) + Lemma 6.2 (orthogonality) bound `EέΓΒ έΚν EέΓΑ+8╧Α┬▓S┬▓/L` | `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattis.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisRingGap.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisProof.lean`, `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisOrthogonality.lean` |
