(6.3.7)έΑΥ(6.3.9)): the **S=1 chain on the hidden-AFM subspace `H_HAF`**. **`tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` is now a proved theorem** (formerly a documented axiom; `#print axioms` έΗΤ `propext`/`Classical.choice`/`Quot.sound`): the final assembly `tasaki_prop_6_5_hhaf_spin_one` bundles, for even `L>0`, a ground state `╬ο = hhafSubspaceEmbedding` of the balanced PerronέΑΥFrobenius eigenvector with (i) projection-fixedness + nonzero + eigenvector at `E = hhafMinEnergy`, (ii) minimality over `hhafRealSpectrum`, (iii) **uniqueness** (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_ground_finrank_le_one`: the restricted ground eigenspace is one-dimensional έΑΦ at `E < έΙΤ2` the single-`┬▒` block is vacated since its eigenvalues are `έΚξ έΙΤ2`, and the balanced block contributes the 1-D PF ground state via the injective restriction `R` into the balanced block eigenspace), lifted to the operator via `hhafSubspaceEmbedding_of_projFixed` + `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_of_projFixed_eig` + `finrank_le_one_iff`, (iv) the positive gap (`exists_hhaf_positive_gap`), and (v) exponential decay (`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`). For the spin-1 chain (`N=2`, configs `Fin L έΗΤ Fin 3`, `╧Δ_x=0/1/2 έΗο +1/0/έΙΤ1`), a config has **complete hidden AFM order** (`IsHiddenAFMConfig`, via `IsPM`/`InCyclicOpen`/`IsNextPM`) when the nonzero (`┬▒`) spins strictly alternate around the ring with arbitrary `0`-spins between (eq. 6.3.9). `H_HAF` = span of those basis states; `hhafProjection` (diagonal projection), `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS = P_HAF┬╖─ν┬╖P_HAF`, `hhafRealSpectrum` (eigenvalues with `H_HAF` eigenvectors). For even `L>0`, the restricted chain has a **unique ground state**, a **finite gap** (`gap>0` in the `H_HAF`-restricted spectrum), and **exponentially decaying** correlation `\|chainCorrelation\| έΚν C e^{έΙΤd(x,y)/╬╛}` (`ringDist`) έΑΦ so the Haldane conjecture for `S=1` holds rigorously within this artificial subspace. Proof: path-integral / 2D classical stat-mech. **Spectral foundations now proved** (infrastructure for the now-proved theorem, Issue #4718): `hhafProjection_isHermitian` (P_HAF Hermitian), `hhafProjection_mul_self` (P_HAF idempotent), `afmHeisenbergChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian`, `hhafRestrictedChainHamiltonianS_isHermitian` (compressed `P_HAF┬╖─ν┬╖P_HAF` Hermitian). **Ground-energy existence now proved**: `hhafConfig` (hidden-AFM config subtype), `hhafRestrictedMatrix` (H_HAF├ΩH_HAF submatrix, Hermitian), `hhafSubspaceEmbedding` (zero-extension) with the compressionέΑΥembedding intertwining `P_HAF┬╖─ν┬╖P_HAF έΙα emb = emb έΙα restMat`, and `hhafMinEnergy`/`hhafMinEnergy_mem_realSpectrum` (the minimal restricted eigenvalue is a genuine `hhafRealSpectrum` member with a nonzero H_HAF ground state), and `exists_hhaf_min_real_eigenvalue` (it is `έΚν` every restricted eigenvalue, so a genuine restricted **ground energy** with minimality). **Per-L exp-decay** proved (`exp_decay_envelope_of_finite`/`hhaf_correlation_exp_decay_exists`): on the finite ring any state's correlation admits `|έθρ┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_yέθσ| έΚν C e^{έΙΤd/╬╛}` (╬╛=1, C=╬μ|corr|e^{d}) έΑΦ the decay clause of Prop 6.5 (now all clauses proved). **Max-energy infra** added: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eigenvalue_mem_realSpectrum` (every restricted-matrix eigenvalue embeds into `hhafRealSpectrum`), `hhafMaxEnergy` + `hhafMaxEnergy_mem_realSpectrum`; **spectral completeness/finiteness** (`hhafRealSpectrum_restrict` reverse bridge, `hhafRealSpectrum_subset_eigenvalue_range` via mathlib `spectrum_real_eq_range_eigenvalues`, `hhafRealSpectrum_finite`) έΑΦ the restricted spectrum IS the finite restricted-matrix eigenvalue set. **Diagonal bounds**: new general `HermitianVariationalUpperBound` (max-Rayleigh) + `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag`/`diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` give `hhafMinEnergy έΚν (restMat ╧Δ ╧Δ).re έΚν hhafMaxEnergy` via Rayleigh at the basis vector (toward non-scalarity for the gap). **Non-scalarity PROVED**: `hhafMinEnergy_lt_hhafMaxEnergy` (even LέΚξ2) via all-zero config (diag=0) and domain-wall config ╧Δ=[0,2,1,έΑο,1] (HAF, diag<0); general formula `hhaf_diag_eq_succ_sum` (diag = ╬μ_x (1-╧Δx)(1-╧Δ_{x+1})). **Positive gap PROVED**: `exists_hhaf_positive_gap` (even L>0) έΑΦ first-excited eigenvalue EέΓΒ = (eigenvalues image filtered above min).min with EέΓΒ έΙΙ spectrum, EέΓΒ > minEig (gap>0), and minimal among spectrum elements above minEig (via completeness). These foundations feed the now-discharged uniqueness clause. **PF real-form foundation** started: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_im_zero` (entries real), `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal` (real symmetric form) + `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal_ofReal`/`_isSymm` έΑΦ the entry point for the Perron-Frobenius uniqueness route (Marshall gauge (now built: `ringSublattice`, `hhafDressedMatrix`, `hhafDressedMatrix_eq` = ╬╡(╧Δ)╬╡(╧Ε)┬╖M_real via the dressed-Heisenberg machinery) + **symmetrized-coupling infra** for the off-diagonal sign step (`heisenbergHamiltonianS_coupling_swap`: the Heisenberg Hamiltonian is invariant under transposing the coupling, via `spinSDot_comm`; `ringCouplingSym` = `ringCoupling + ringCouplingά╡Α` with `_symm`/`_im_zero`/`_re_nonneg`; `heisenbergHamiltonianS_ringCouplingSym`: `H(ringCouplingSym) = 2┬╖H(ringCoupling)`, so the directed ring Hamiltonian equals ┬╜ the symmetric-coupling one έΑΦ letting the symmetric-coupling off-diagonal nonpositivity lemma apply) έΑΦ **off-diagonal nonpositivity now PROVED**: `ringCouplingSym_bipartite` (even ring `L` is bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice`: for even `L`, `(a+1)%L` flips parity, so equal-parity sites are never adjacent έΑΦ via `Nat.mod_mod_of_dvd`), `dressedHeisenbergS_ringCoupling_re_eq_half` (dressed directed = ┬╜ dressed symmetric), and `hhafDressedMatrix_offdiag_nonpos` (even `L`: `hhafDressedMatrix ╧Δ ╧Ε έΚν 0` for `╧Δ έΚι ╧Ε`, via the symmetric-coupling nonpositivity lemma) έΑΦ the diagonal `┬▒1` Marshall gauge makes the AFM off-diagonals sign-definite. **Symmetry + nonneg shift now PROVED**: `hhafDressedMatrix_isSymm` (the dressed matrix is symmetric), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_eq` (diagonal = real restricted diagonal, since the Marshall sign squares to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix_diag_le_hhafMaxEnergy` (diagonal έΚν max energy), and `hhafShifted_entry_nonneg` (for `c έΚξ hhafMaxEnergy` and even `L`, the shifted matrix `c┬╖I έΙΤ M` has nonnegative entries έΑΦ the nonnegativity hypothesis of `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric`). **Irreducibility started** (Issue #4732, G├│mez-Santos kink ergodicity): `hhafShifted_pos_of_ladderStep` έΑΦ two hidden-AFM configs differing by one adjacent raise/lower (ladder) move on a bipartite bond have a strictly positive shifted entry `(c┬╖I έΙΤ M) ╧Ε ╧Δ > 0` (reusing `dressedHeisenbergS_apply_re_neg_of_raiseLowerStepS_witness` on `ringCouplingSym` + the ┬╜ scaling), the single-edge positivity for the PerronέΑΥFrobenius reachability argument. **Ring bond graph** added: `hhafRingGraph` (cyclic nearest-neighbour graph on `Fin L`), with `hhafRingGraph_adj_sublattice_ne` (bipartite w.r.t. `ringSublattice` for even `L`) and `ringCouplingSym_re_pos_of_ringGraph_adj` (the symmetrized coupling is strictly positive on every edge) έΑΦ the bond-graph data feeding the per-step positivity. **HAF reachability + walkέΗΤpower** added: `RaiseLowerStepSHhaf` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf` (single HAF-preserving ladder move / its reflexive-transitive closure), `hhafShifted_pos_of_stepHhaf` (each step gives a strictly positive shifted entry), and `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable` (HAF-reachability έΘΤ some power `(c┬╖I έΙΤ M)^k` is strictly positive έΑΦ the walkέΗΤpower bridge, mirroring the magnetization-sector version). **Kink-reduction setup** added: `hhafCanonical` (the all-`0`-spin configuration, base case), `pmCount` (number of `┬▒` spins, the induction measure), `pmCount_eq_zero_iff` (no `┬▒` spins έθ║ canonical). **Slide move** added: `slidePM` (move the spin at `a` onto a cyclic neighbour `b`, leaving a `0` at `a`), with `slidePM_apply` (its pointwise values) and `slidePM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single raise/lower ladder step on the ring-graph bond `{a, b}` when `a` carries a `┬▒` spin and `b` carries a `0`). **Slide preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity core): `slidePM_isHiddenAFM` έΑΦ sliding a `┬▒` spin onto its `0`-carrying cyclic successor keeps the strict `+,έΙΤ,έΑο` alternation, proved by transferring each `IsNextPM` pair of the moved configuration back to one of `╧Δ` (relabelling `b έΗο a`) via the open-arc decomposition lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_left_imp` / `inCyclicOpen_succ_right_imp` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ` (each an `omega` calculation after splitting the cyclic wrap). **Slide lifted to a subtype step** (`slidePM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf`) and the **annihilation move** added: `annihPM` (set adjacent `a, b` both to `0`), `annihPM_apply`, and `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStep` (it is a single ladder step when `a, b` carry opposite `┬▒` spins). **Annihilation preserves hidden-AFM order PROVEN**: `annihPM_isHiddenAFM` έΑΦ removing an adjacent opposite-sign `+,έΙΤ` pair keeps the alternation (each `IsNextPM` pair of the annihilated config either avoids the removed pair, giving an `IsNextPM` pair of `╧Δ`, or straddles it, where the chain `x, a, b, y` forces opposite endpoint signs), via the sub-arc lemmas `inCyclicOpen_succ_iff_mem` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_left` / `inCyclicOpen_sub_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_succ_right` / `notInCyclicOpen_pred_left`. **Move-count behaviour** added: `annihPM_isRaiseLowerStepSHhaf` (annihilation as a subtype step), `slidePM_pmCount_card` (sliding preserves the `┬▒` count), `annihPM_pmCount_card_lt` (annihilation strictly decreases it). **CONNECTIVITY PROVEN** (the kink-ergodicity theorem): the reduction measure `hhafS = ╬μ_{┬▒ spins}(L έΙΤ x)` strictly decreases under a slide (`slidePM_hhafS_lt`, non-wrapping successor) and an annihilation (`annihPM_hhafS_lt`); `hhaf_single_step` produces, for any balanced (even-`pmCount`) non-canonical config, a single HAF ladder move to another balanced config of smaller measure (first `┬▒` spin at `p έΚν LέΙΤ2`, sliding past a `0` or annihilating an adjacent opposite `┬▒`); `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical` / `hhaf_reachable_canonical` (strong induction on `hhafS`) then show **every balanced hidden-AFM configuration is HAF-reachable from the canonical all-`0` configuration** (with `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf_symm` for the reverse). **Design note**: the *full* HAF matrix is reducible έΑΦ a single-`┬▒` config (`pmCount = 1`, magnetization `┬▒1`) is vacuously hidden-AFM but unreachable from `canonical` (the moves conserve magnetization), so irreducibility holds only within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector; this is the sector containing the antiferromagnetic ground state. **Balanced-sector block irreducibility PROVEN**: `hhafConfig0` (the even-`pmCount` subtype) with `hhafShiftedMatrix0` (the `c┬╖I έΙΤ M` block), `hhaf_reachable_to_canonical0` / `RaiseLowerReachableSHhaf0_symm` (balanced-sector connectivity lifted to the subtype), `exists_matrixPow_apply_pos_of_hhafReachable0` (subtype walkέΗΤpower), and `hhafShiftedMatrix0_isIrreducible` έΑΦ for even `L` and shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`, the balanced-sector shifted matrix is PerronέΑΥFrobenius irreducible (`isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos`: nonnegative + entrywise-positive power, with the diagonal handled by the strict shift `c > hhafMaxEnergy`). **Balanced-sector unique ground state PROVEN**: `hhafDressedMatrix0` (the dressed block on `hhafConfig0`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_isSymm` (submatrix of a symmetric matrix), `hhafShiftedMatrix0_eq` (`hhafShiftedMatrix0 = c┬╖I έΙΤ MέΓΑ`, the identity restricting since `Subtype.val` is injective), and `hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_finrank_le_one` έΑΦ applying `perronFrobenius_real_symmetric` to the balanced block yields a strictly positive lowest eigenvector with a **one-dimensional** ground eigenspace: the unique ground state of the AFM chain *within the balanced (charge-`0`) sector* of `H_HAF`. **Marshall-gauge transfer DONE**: `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0` (undressed real balanced block), `hhafMarshallDiag0` (the `┬▒1` Marshall sign diagonal, squaring to `1`), `hhafDressedMatrix0_eq_conj` (`MέΓΑ = ╬α┬╖M_realέΓΑ┬╖╬α`), and `hhafRestrictedMatrixReal0_ground_finrank_le_one` έΑΦ transferring the **full PerronέΑΥFrobenius data** (nonzero lowest eigenvector `╬αv`, ground minimality, and `finrank έΚν 1`) through the Marshall similarity (the generic `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq`, now generalised from `έΕΓ` to any field), so the **undressed** real balanced block also has a one-dimensional ground eigenspace with an explicit minimal eigenvector. Remaining for the *global* ground-state uniqueness: the inter-sector energy ordering (balanced sector strictly lowest, a MarshallέΑΥLiebέΑΥMattis statement within `H_HAF`). **N├σel witness** added (toward that ordering): `hhafNeel` (the maximally-AFM configuration `+1` on even sites, `έΙΤ1` on odd), `hhafNeel_isHiddenAFM` (it is hidden-AFM on an even ring έΑΦ all sites are `┬▒`, every `IsNextPM` pair is cyclically adjacent hence opposite parity, via `inCyclicOpen_succ_mem_of_ne`), `hhafNeel_pmCount` (`┬▒`-count `= L`), and `hhafNeelConfig0` (the N├σel configuration as a balanced charge-`0` configuration). **Ground energy έΚν έΙΤL PROVEN**: `hhafNeel_diag` (the N├σel diagonal energy is `έΙΤL`, every nearest-neighbour bond antiferromagnetic `(+1)(έΙΤ1) = έΙΤ1`) and `hhafMinEnergy_le_neg_L` (the global restricted ground energy `hhafMinEnergy έΚν έΙΤL`, via the Rayleigh quotient `hhafMinEnergy_le_diag` at the N├σel state) έΑΦ the first half of the inter-sector ordering (the balanced sector reaches energy `έΙΤL`; the remaining half bounds the magnetization-`┬▒1` single-`┬▒` sectors above this). Toward that bound: `hhaf_diag_eq_zero_of_pmCount_one` έΑΦ a single-`┬▒` configuration has **vanishing diagonal energy** (every nearest-neighbour bond has a `0`-spin endpoint since only one site is `┬▒`), the Gershgorin-disc centre for the magnetization-`┬▒1` sectors. `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_raising_lowering` / `spinSDot_re_abs_le_one_lowering_raising` έΑΦ the spin-`1` ladder off-diagonal amplitude `|έθρ╧Δ'|┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y|╧Δέθσ| έΚν 1` (each `έΙγ` factor `έΚν έΙγ2` for `N = 2`), the Gershgorin-disc radius ingredient. `hhafDressedMatrix_abs_le_ringCouplingSym` έΑΦ for two hidden-AFM configs related by a single ladder move on a bond `{x, y}`, the dressed off-diagonal entry satisfies `|M ╧Δ ╧Ε| έΚν (ringCouplingSym x y).re` (the Marshall signs are `┬▒1`, the Heisenberg element is `ringCouplingSym ┬╖ spinSDot`, and the ladder amplitude is `έΚν 1`). **Magnetization-`┬▒1` sector min `έΚξ έΙΤ2` PROVEN** (codex insight: the cyclic `IsHiddenAFMConfig` forbids odd `pmCount > 1`, so the only HAF sectors are balanced `Sz=0` (even `pmCount`) and single-`┬▒` `Sz=┬▒1` (`pmCount = 1`) έΑΦ there are **no higher-`Sz` sectors**, dissolving the inter-sector LiebέΑΥMattis wall): `hhafConfigPM1` (the `pmCount = 1` subtype) with `hhafDressedMatrixPM1` (dressed block), `hhafPM1Site` (the unique `┬▒` site, computed via `Finset.min'` so `Finset.image` reductions stay light), `hhafPM1_coupling_imp` (a nonzero off-diagonal forces `╧Ε` to be the slide `slidePM` of `╧Δ`'s `┬▒` spin to its new site, sharing the `┬▒` value by magnetization conservation `magEigenvalueS_eq_iff`), `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_offdiag_le` (per-entry bound `έΚν ringCouplingSym`), `hhafPM1Site_injOn_support` (the `┬▒`-site map is injective on the coupling support), `ringCouplingSym_re_row_sum` (`έΙΣ_t (ringCouplingSym s t).re = 2`, the two incident directed ring bonds), the generic `sum_comp_le_sum_univ_of_injOn` (injective-on-support reindex avoiding a heavy concrete `Finset.univ`), and `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_rowSum_le_two` έθ╣ `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` (Gershgorin `eigenvalue_mem_ball`, diagonal `0` + row sum `έΚν 2`: every single-`┬▒` eigenvalue `έΚξ έΙΤ2`) έΑΦ the single-kink tight-binding lower bound placing the `Sz = ┬▒1` sectors above the balanced ground energy (`έΚν έΙΤL έΚν έΙΤ2`). **Balanced ground energy `< έΙΤL` STRICT PROVEN** (`hhafDressedMatrix0_ground_lt_neg_L`): the PerronέΑΥFrobenius lowest eigenvector `v > 0` paired with the N├σel row gives `╬╝┬╖v(N├σel) = έΙΤL┬╖v(N├σel) + ╬μ_{╧ΕέΚιN├σel} MέΓΑ(N├σel,╧Ε) v(╧Ε)`; every off-diagonal term is `έΚν 0` (`hhafDressedMatrix0_offdiag_nonpos`) and the annihilation neighbour (`hhafNeel_step_neighbor`, annihilating the adjacent opposite-`┬▒` pair at sites `0,1`) is strictly `< 0`, so via the generic row inequality `row_sum_mul_lt_diag_mul_of_offdiag_nonpos_exists_neg` and `hhafDressedMatrix0_Neel_diag` (`MέΓΑ(N├σel,N├σel) = έΙΤL`) we get `╬╝┬╖v(N├σel) < έΙΤL┬╖v(N├σel)`, hence `╬╝ < έΙΤL`. Since `έΙΤL έΚν έΙΤ2`, the balanced sector lies **strictly** below the magnetization-`┬▒1` sectors with **no `L=2` tie** έΑΦ the inter-sector ordering is complete. **Sector classification PROVEN** (`hhaf_pmCount_eq_one_or_even`): every hidden-AFM configuration has `pmCount = 1` or even `pmCount` έΑΦ an odd count `έΚξ 3` is impossible. Proof: the `┬▒` sites read in increasing index order are pairwise `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_consecutive` έΑΦ no `┬▒` spin lies strictly between index-adjacent `┬▒` sites, via `orderEmbOfFin`), so their signs strictly alternate (`hhaf_pm_alternates`: the `i`-th `┬▒` value is the `0`-th when `i` is even and its complement `2 - ┬╖` when odd, `pm_flip`); and the maximal/minimal `┬▒` sites are also `IsNextPM` (`hhaf_isNextPM_wrap` έΑΦ the cyclic wrap arc holds no `┬▒` spin), which would force the first and last `┬▒` signs to *differ* while the linear alternation makes them *equal* at odd count έΑΦ contradiction. The magnetization corollaries `hhaf_magSumS_eq_L_of_even` (even `pmCount έθ╣ magSumS = L`, via `magSumS`-invariance under HAF ladder moves `magSumS_eq_of_hhafReachable` + reachability from canonical) and `hhaf_magSumS_ne_L_of_pmCount_one` (single-`┬▒` έθ╣ `magSumS = L ┬▒ 1 έΚι L`) identify the sectors: balanced `= Sz 0`, single-`┬▒` `= Sz ┬▒1`, with no higher sectors έΑΦ the cross-sector vanishing (magnetization conservation) needed to split a ground eigenvector into balanced + single-`┬▒` blocks. **Magnetization-block structure of the restricted matrix PROVEN**: `hhafRestrictedMatrix_eq_zero_of_magSumS_ne` (entries between different-`magSumS` configurations vanish έΑΦ magnetization conservation); the generic slice `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice P` (submatrix on `{╧Δ // P ╧Δ}`) with `hhafRestrictedMatrixSlice_mulVec_of_full_eigen` (a full eigenvector restricted to a block-closed `P`-slice is a `P`-slice eigenvector with the same eigenvalue); and the two block-closures `hhafRestrictedMatrix_pmCount_one_block_closed` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_even_block_closed` (via the classification: a single-`┬▒` config couples only to single-`┬▒`, an even config only to even) έΑΦ the block-diagonal decomposition into the balanced and single-`┬▒` blocks, the structural input for the ground-eigenspace `finrank έΚν 1`. **Per-block spectral bounds PROVEN**: the complex blocks are the real blocks cast to `έΕΓ` (`hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eq_map` / `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eq_map`, entries real); `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrix0_eigenspace_finrank_le_one` έΑΦ the **complex balanced block ground eigenspace is one-dimensional** (realέΗΤcomplex bridge `matrix_complex_eigenspace_finrank_le_one_of_real` from the real balanced PerronέΑΥFrobenius `finrank έΚν 1`); and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_submatrixPM1_eigenvalue_ge` έΑΦ the **complex single-`┬▒` block has all eigenvalues `έΚξ έΙΤ2`** (an eigenvalue is real, its re/im part is a real-block eigenvector, transferred to a dressed-block eigenvector via the Marshall conjugation `hhafDressedMatrixPM1_eq_conj` where the Gershgorin bound `έΚξ έΙΤ2` applies). These are the per-block spectral inputs: at the ground energy `E < έΙΤ2` the single-`┬▒` block is vacated and the balanced block contributes the unique ground state. **Reverse block restriction PROVEN**: `hhafSliceExtend P` (zero-extension of a `P`-slice vector to all of `hhafConfig`) with `hhafSliceExtend_ne_zero` and `hhafRestrictedMatrix_mulVec_hhafSliceExtend` (if there is no coupling *into* a `P`-slice from its complement, the zero-extension of a `P`-slice eigenvector is a full eigenvector at the same eigenvalue) έΑΦ the reverse counterpart of the forward slice restriction, used to lift the balanced PerronέΑΥFrobenius eigenvector to a full restricted-matrix eigenvector (so the balanced ground energy is in the restricted spectrum). (The PerronέΑΥFrobenius uniqueness development έΑΦ kink moves through the Marshall transfer, the magnetization-`┬▒1` Gershgorin bound, and the strict balanced ground energy έΑΦ lives in the companion `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean`, with its combinatorial kink-move/balanced-sector core split off into `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` for build speed.) | `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrder.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniqueness.lean` + `Quantum/SpinS/HiddenAntiferromagneticOrderUniquenessCore.lean` |

(8.2.1)έΑΥ(8.2.4)): the two-parameter Haldane-phase model. `lambdaDChainHamiltonianS L ╬╗ D = ╬μ_{x,y} [openAnisotropicChainCoupling] ┬╖ spinSDotXXZ x y ╬╗ 2 + D ╬μ_x (┼ε_x^{(3)})┬▓` (eq. 8.2.1, `─ν_{╬╗,D}` = open XXZ chain with Ising anisotropy `╬╗` + crystal field `D`; `╬╗=1` έΘΤ open-boundary version of the anisotropic model 8.1.1, agreeing in the bulk). `neelOrderParameterS`/`stringOrderParameterS` (markers, `έΕζέΗΤέΕζέΗΤFin 3έΗΤέΕζ`) are the thermodynamic double-limit N├σel (8.2.2) and den NijsέΑΥRommelse string (8.2.3) order parameters of the GS. `tasaki_theorem_8_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.2.4): for `╬╗έΚξ0` and any `D`, `O_N├σel^{(╬▒)} έΚν O_string^{(╬▒)}` for all `╬▒` έΑΦ nonzero N├σel order (Ising AF phase) forces nonzero hidden order. `tasaki_neel_transverse_eq_nonneg` (`╬╗έΚξ0`): `O_N├σel^{(1)} = O_N├σel^{(2)} έΚξ 0`. Proof: path-integral representation (┬π6.3) | `Quantum/SpinS/LambdaDModel.lean` |
| `ktUnitaryS` / `piRotationS` / `IsZ2Z2Invariant` / `tasaki_prop_8_4` | **┬π8.2.2έΑΥ┬π8.2.3 KennedyέΑΥTasaki transformation + Proposition 8.4** (PollmannέΑΥTurnerέΑΥBergέΑΥOshikawa; eqs. (8.2.5)έΑΥ(8.2.7)): the nonlocal unitary realizing hidden ZέΓΓ├ΩZέΓΓ symmetry breaking. `ktUnitaryS L` (marker) is the KennedyέΑΥTasaki unitary `├δ_KT = έΙΠ_{u<v} exp(i╧Α ┼ε_u^{(3)} ┼ε_v^{(1)})` (eq. 8.2.5), with `ktUnitaryS_sq` (`├δ_KT┬▓=1`) and `ktUnitaryS_selfAdjoint` (`├δ_KT=├δ_KTέΑι`) έΑΦ a self-adjoint involution. `piRotationS L ╬▒ = έΙΠ_x exp(i╧Α ┼ε_x^{(╬▒)})` (**concrete**, on-site matrix exponentials) is the ╧Α-rotation about axis `╬▒`; `IsZ2Z2Invariant H` = `(├δ_╧Α^{(╬▒)})έΑι H ├δ_╧Α^{(╬▒)} = H` for all `╬▒` (commutes with all three ╧Α-rotations). `HasShortRangeInteraction`/`HasSomeShortRangeInteraction` (markers) capture range-`r` locality. `tasaki_prop_8_4` (**AXIOM**): for a short-range open-chain `─ν`, `├δ_KT ─ν ├δ_KT` is again short-range **iff** `─ν` is ZέΓΓ├ΩZέΓΓ invariant έΑΦ the hidden-symmetry-breaking picture is effective exactly when `─ν` has ZέΓΓ├ΩZέΓΓ symmetry | `Quantum/SpinS/KennedyTasakiTransformation.lean` |
| `guWenHamiltonianS` / `IsSPTPhase` / `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` | **┬π8.3.1έΑΥ┬π8.3.2 Symmetry protected topological (SPT) phase** (GuέΑΥWen / PollmannέΑΥTurnerέΑΥBergέΑΥOshikawa; eqs. (8.3.1)έΑΥ(8.3.4)): the Haldane phase as an SPT phase. `guWenHamiltonianS L D B = anisotropicChainHamiltonianS L D + B ╬μ_x ┼ε_x^{(1)}` (eq. 8.3.4, S=1 chain + magnetic field B in 1-dir, breaks ZέΓΓ├ΩZέΓΓέΗΤZέΓΓ; B=0 έΘΤ anisotropic 8.1.1). `tasaki_oshikawa_8_3_3` (**AXIOM**, eq. 8.3.3): the spin-`S` VBS string order `O_string^{(╬▒)}` is `>0` for odd `S`, `=0` for even `S` (even/odd-`S` distinction); `tasaki_vbs_edge_degeneracy` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` AKLT open chain has `(S+1)┬▓`-fold edge degeneracy. **SPT definition** (`def : Prop`, NOT axiom): `HamiltonianPath` (continuous `έΕζέΗΤ` Hamiltonian, each short-range gapped-unique via `IsShortRangeGappedUniqueGS` marker); `ContinuouslyConnected`/`SymmetryConnected sym` (path with all `─ν_s` having the symmetry `sym`); `IsTrivialPhase sym H` = symmetry-connected to a product state (`IsProductStateHamiltonian`); `IsSPTPhase sym H` = short-range gapped-unique + `sym H` + ┬υtrivial. The Haldane phase is the prototypical SPT phase protected by ZέΓΓ├ΩZέΓΓ (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTPhase.lean` |
| `IsTimeReversalInvariant` / `IsBondInversionInvariant` / `vbsInversionParityS` / `tasaki_spt_classification` | **┬π8.3.2έΑΥ┬π8.3.3 Protecting symmetries + topological indices for SPT** (PollmannέΑΥTurnerέΑΥBergέΑΥOshikawa; eqs. (8.3.6)έΑΥ(8.3.10)): the Haldane phase is protected by any of three symmetries έΑΦ (S1) ZέΓΓ├ΩZέΓΓ (`IsZ2Z2Invariant`), (S2) time-reversal (`IsTimeReversalInvariant` marker), (S3) bond-centered inversion (`IsBondInversionInvariant` marker). `vbsInversionParityS L S` (marker, έΕν) + `tasaki_vbs_inversion_parity` (**AXIOM**): `├δ_inv|╬ο_VBS^Sέθσ = (έΙΤ1)^{L┬╖S}|╬ο_VBS^Sέθσ` έΑΦ odd `L┬╖S` έθ╣ odd parity έθ╣ ZέΓΓ obstruction to connecting to the trivial state. `IsSpinSVBSNontrivialSPT S` (marker) + `tasaki_spt_classification` (**AXIOM**): the spin-`S` VBS is a nontrivial SPT phase **iff `S` is odd** (even `S` έΘΤ trivial). `entanglementEntropyS` (marker, eqs. 8.3.7έΑΥ8.3.8): the bipartite entanglement entropy `έΙΤ╬μ p_j log p_j` from the Schmidt decomposition. ┬π8.3.3 is heuristic; precise indices come in ┬π8.3.4 (MPS) / ┬π8.3.6 (Ogata) | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTTopologicalIndex.lean` |
| `IsTrivialProjectiveRep` / `tasaki_theorem_8_7` / `tasaki_corollary_8_5` | **┬π8.3.4 Matrix-product SPT index** (Theorem 8.7 Tachikawa + Corollary 8.5; eqs. (8.3.42)έΑΥ(8.3.47)): the precise MPS invariant. A protecting symmetry `G` acts on the bond space by a projective representation with phase function (2-cocycle) `╧Η : GέΗΤGέΗΤέΕζ` (`IsProjectiveRep` marker); it is **trivial** (`IsTrivialProjectiveRep` marker) iff `╧Η` is a coboundary (eq. 8.3.43) έΑΦ the cohomology class is the SPT index. `SymmetricInjectiveMPSExists G ╧Η` (marker): an injective MPS invariant up to phase under `V╠Γ(g)`. `tasaki_theorem_8_7` (**AXIOM**): symmetric injective MPS έθ╣ trivial projective rep. For half-odd-integer spin (`N` odd), `z2z2Spin_nontrivial_of_odd` (**AXIOM**, eq. 2.1.31): the ZέΓΓ├ΩZέΓΓ rep is nontrivial. `tasaki_corollary_8_5` (**PROVED**, contrapositive of Thm 8.7): for `N` odd there is **no** ZέΓΓ├ΩZέΓΓ-invariant injective MPS έΑΦ the matrix-product LiebέΑΥSchultzέΑΥMattis no-go | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTMatrixProductIndex.lean` |
| `tasaki_theorem_8_6` | **┬π8.3.5 Discrete-symmetry LiebέΑΥSchultzέΑΥMattis theorem** (Theorem 8.6, OgataέΑΥTasaki): the general no-go without continuous symmetry. For **half-odd-integer** spin (`S=N/2`, `N` odd) with a short-ranged translation-invariant Hamiltonian (`HasShortRangeHamiltonianS`/`IsTranslationInvariantS` markers) having **either** ZέΓΓ├ΩZέΓΓ **or** time-reversal symmetry (`IsZ2Z2SymmetricS έΙρ IsTimeReversalSymmetricS`), the ground state can **never** be both unique and gapped: `tasaki_theorem_8_6` (**AXIOM**) `έθ╣ ┬υHasUniqueGappedGroundStateS H`. Unlike the original LSM (┬π6.2, continuous U(1)), this needs only a discrete symmetry; proved rigorously by OgataέΑΥTasaki via the Cuntz algebra / split property | `Quantum/SpinS/LiebSchultzMattisDiscrete.lean` |
| `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_8` | **┬π8.3.6 Rigorous SPT phase transition** (Theorem 8.8, Tasaki 2018 index theorem; eq. (8.3.55)): the existence of a topological phase transition. `sptInterpolatingHamiltonianS L s = s┬╖akltHamiltonianS L + (1έΙΤs)┬╖╬μ_x (┼ε_x^{(3)})┬▓` (eq. 8.3.55) linearly connects the trivial Hamiltonian (8.1.2, `s=0`, large-`D` phase) to the AKLT/VBS Hamiltonian (7.1.1, `s=1`, Haldane phase), keeping all three protecting symmetries. `tasaki_theorem_8_8` (**AXIOM**): the model's infinite-volume limit undergoes a phase transition έΑΦ έΙΔ intermediate `sέΙΙ(0,1)` at which the family `LέΗο─ν_s` is **either** gapless (`IsGaplessInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has multiple ground states (`HasMultipleGroundStatesInInfiniteVolume`), **or** has a local-expectation discontinuity (`HasLocalExpectationDiscontinuityInInfiniteVolume`) έΑΦ markers on the `LέΗΣέΙη` family. Proving topological phase transitions resists standard methods; Tasaki settled it via an index theorem | `Quantum/SpinS/SPTPhaseTransition.lean` |
| `toricCodeHamiltonianS` / `tasaki_theorem_8_9` | **┬π8.4 Topological order: Kitaev's toric code** (Theorem 8.9, BravyiέΑΥHastingsέΑΥMichalakis; eqs. (8.4.1), (8.4.19)): a gapped phase whose degeneracy is protected by topology, not symmetry. Qubits live on the edges `ToricEdge L = (ZMod L ├Ω ZMod L) ├Ω Fin 2` of an `L├ΩL` torus (`2L┬▓` edges); `starEdges`/`plaquetteEdges` are the 4-edge stars `A_v`/plaquettes `B_p`; `sigmaZS`/`sigmaXS` = `╧Δ^z`/`╧Δ^x`. `toricCodeHamiltonianS L = έΙΤ╬μ_v έΙΠ_{A_v} ╧Δ^z έΙΤ ╬μ_p έΙΠ_{B_p} ╧Δ^x` (eq. 8.4.1, commuting stabilizers, gap έΚξ 2, 4-fold degenerate on the torus). `perturbedToricHamiltonianS L ╬╡ V = ─ν_tc + ╬╡ ╬μ_x V_x` (eq. 8.4.19). `tasaki_theorem_8_9` (**AXIOM**): for range-`r` perturbations there are **L-independent** `╬╡έΓΑ,╬Φ>0` such that for `|╬╡|έΚν╬╡έΓΑ`, all `LέΚξ2` (nontrivial torus), and any local perturbation (`IsToricPerturbation`), the perturbed Hamiltonian has **four near-degenerate ground states** separated by `╬Φ` (`HasFourNearlyDegenerateGroundStates`) έΑΦ topological 4-fold degeneracy stable under arbitrary perturbation, no symmetry needed. Lattice + stabilizers + both Hamiltonians concrete | `Quantum/SpinS/ToricCode.lean` |

| `heisenbergHamiltonian_openChainCoupling_one_mulVec_neelChainState_one` | `K = 1` instance: `H_open(N=1, J) ┬╖ |╬ο_N├σelέθσ = -J ┬╖ |έΗΥέΗΣέθσ + (J/2) ┬╖ |╬ο_N├σelέθσ`. | `neelChainConfig_one_eq_upDown` / `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelChainState_one` | bridges the `K = 1` N├σel chain configuration to the existing `upDown` config and computes `╬α╠Γ_tot (neelChainState 1) = -basisVec (basisSwap upDown 0 1)` (the per-down sign convention of `╬α╠Γ` flips the antiparallel pair) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareConfig` / `neelSquareState` | 2D checkerboard N├σel state on `Fin (2K) ├Ω Fin (2L)` (Tasaki ┬π2.5 (2.5.2) bipartite case): `╧Δ(i, j) = έΗΣ` if `(i.val + j.val) % 2 = 0`, `έΗΥ` otherwise | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelSquareConfig_magnetization_zero` / `neelSquareState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | the 2D N├σel configuration has total magnetisation `0` and the corresponding state lies in the `┼ε_tot^(3) = 0` eigenspace `H_0`. Proof: row-by-row column-sum vanishes (helper `sum_alternating_sign_offset` for the 1D parity sum with offset) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelSquareState_horizontal_adjacent` / `_vertical_adjacent` | Tasaki ┬π2.5 (2.5.3) per-bond action on the 2D N├σel state for the horizontal (`(i,j)~(i+1,j)`) and vertical (`(i,j)~(i,j+1)`) nearest-neighbour bonds: same `(1/2) ┬╖ |swapέθσ - (1/4) ┬╖ |╬ο_N├σelέθσ` decomposition as the 1D chain | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelSquareState_horizontal_wrap` / `_vertical_wrap` | wrap-around bond actions on the 2D torus N├σel state: horizontal `((2K+1, j), (0, j))` on `Fin (2(K+1)) ├Ω Fin (2L)` and vertical `((i, 2L+1), (i, 0))` on `Fin (2K) ├Ω Fin (2(L+1))` are antiparallel (parities differ by an odd shift); both inherit the same `(1/2)┬╖|swapέθσ - (1/4)┬╖|╬ο_N├σelέθσ` decomposition. With `_horizontal_adjacent` / `_vertical_adjacent`, covers every nearest-neighbour bond of the 2D torus N├σel state | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelCubicConfig` / `neelCubicState` / `neelCubicConfig_magnetization_zero` / `neelCubicState_mem_magnetizationSubspace_zero` | 3D cubic checkerboard N├σel state on `(Fin (2K) ├Ω Fin (2L)) ├Ω Fin (2M)`: `╧Δ((i,j),k) = έΗΣ` if `(i+j+k) % 2 = 0`, magnetisation = 0, lies in `H_0` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelCubicState_x_adjacent` / `_y_adjacent` / `_z_adjacent` | Tasaki ┬π2.5 (2.5.3) per-bond actions on the 3D cubic N├σel state for the three nearest-neighbour bond axes (x, y, z): same `(1/2)┬╖|swapέθσ - (1/4)┬╖|╬ο_N├σelέθσ` decomposition | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelCubicState_x_wrap` / `_y_wrap` / `_z_wrap` | wrap-around bond actions on the 3D cubic-torus N├σel state: each axis-wrap (`((2K+1, j), k) ~ ((0, j), k)`, `((i, 2L+1), k) ~ ((i, 0), k)`, `((i, j), 2M+1) ~ ((i, j), 0)`) is antiparallel (one coordinate shifts by an odd amount). With `_x_adjacent` / `_y_adjacent` / `_z_adjacent`, covers every nearest-neighbour bond of the 3D cubic torus N├σel state | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelSquareState_one_one` | concrete `K = L = 1` (2├Ω2 = 4-site) instance: `╬α╠Γ_tot (neelSquareState 1 1) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelSquareConfig 1 1))` (the equal up/down counts make `(-1)^|A| = 1`, so no overall sign) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelCubicState_one_one_one` | concrete `K = L = M = 1` (2├Ω2├Ω2 = 8-site) instance: `╬α╠Γ_tot (neelCubicState 1 1 1) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelCubicConfig 1 1 1))` (4 down spins after flipping έΗΤ `(-1)^4 = 1`, so no overall sign) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelChainState` | general-`K` 1D chain: `╬α╠Γ_tot (neelChainState K) = (-1)^K ┬╖ basisVec (flipConfig (neelChainConfig K))` (helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the per-site sign product). | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelSquareState` | general-`K, L` 2D checkerboard: `╬α╠Γ_tot (neelSquareState K L) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelSquareConfig K L))` (no sign because `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`). | `timeReversalSpinHalfMulti_neelCubicState` | general-`K, L, M` 3D cubic checkerboard: `╬α╠Γ_tot (neelCubicState K L M) = basisVec (flipConfig (neelCubicConfig K L M))` (no sign because `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`). | `sum_mul_basisVec` / `basisVec_sum_mul` | selector-sum identities `έΙΣ ╧Ε, f ╧Ε ┬╖ basisVec ╧Δ ╧Ε = f ╧Δ` (and the symmetric form), the workhorses for inner-product computations on the spin Hilbert space | `Quantum/ManyBody.lean` |

| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_antiparallel` | generic lemma: for any antiparallel `(x, y)` configuration `╧Δ`, `έθρbasisVec ╧Δ, ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y ┬╖ basisVec ╧Δέθσ = -1/4`. The 1-line foundation for every N├σel-bond expectation | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_basisVec_parallel` | parallel companion: for `╧Δ x = ╧Δ y` (and `x έΚι y`), `έθρbasisVec ╧Δ, ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y ┬╖ basisVec ╧Δέθσ = +1/4`. Both basis vectors at the parallel pair are eigenvectors of `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y` (eigenvalue `+1/4`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_spinHalfDot_parallel_eq_one_quarter` | N├σel chain same-sublattice (parallel) bond expectation `+1/4`: for any `x έΚι y` with `x.val % 2 = y.val % 2` (e.g., `(0, 2)`, `(1, 3)`), `έθρ╬ο_N├σel, ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y ┬╖ ╬ο_N├σelέθσ = +1/4` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `onSite_spinHalfOp3_mul_onSite_spinHalfOp3_mulVec_basisVec` | `(┼ε^(3)_x ┬╖ ┼ε^(3)_y) ┬╖ basisVec ╧Δ = (spinHalfSign ╧Δ x ┬╖ spinHalfSign ╧Δ y) ┬╖ basisVec ╧Δ`: every basis vector is an eigenvector of the diagonal `┼ε^z┬╖┼ε^z` correlator. | `spinHalfSign_mul_antiparallel` | for antiparallel `s έΚι t : Fin 2`, `spinHalfSign s ┬╖ spinHalfSign t = -(1/4)`. Made public in PR #332 to power the generic `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel = -(1/4)` N├σel correlator | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `inner_basisVec_spinHalfDot_sub_szsz_basisVec_antiparallel` | generic off-diagonal correlator: for any antiparallel `(x, y)` configuration `╧Δ`, `έθρbasisVec ╧Δ, (┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y - ┼ε^(3)_x ┬╖ ┼ε^(3)_y) ┬╖ basisVec ╧Δέθσ = 0`. | `neelChainState_inner_off_diagonal_correlator_adjacent_eq_zero` | the per-adjacent-bond off-diagonal correlator on the N├σel chain vanishes: `έθρ╬ο_N├σel, (┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y - ┼ε^(3)_x ┬╖ ┼ε^(3)_y) ┬╖ ╬ο_N├σelέθσ = 0`. Direct application of the generic helper | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `neelChainState_inner_szsz_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` | per-adjacent-bond `┼ε^(3)_x ┬╖ ┼ε^(3)_y` correlation on the N├σel chain: `-1/4`. | `neelCubicState_inner_spinHalfDot_{x,y,z}_adjacent_eq_neg_one_quarter` / `_{x,y,z}_wrap_` | 3D N├σel: per-bond expectation `-1/4` for every x / y / z adjacent and wrap bond. | `neelChainState_energy_expectation_K1` | `K = 1` (2-site) open-chain Heisenberg energy expectation `έθρ╬ο_N├σel, H_open ┬╖ ╬ο_N├σelέθσ = J/2`. | `neelConfigOf` / `neelStateOf` | generic graph-centric N├σel state from a sublattice indicator `A : V έΗΤ Bool`: `neelConfigOf A x := if A x then έΗΣ else έΗΥ` and `neelStateOf A := basisVec (neelConfigOf A)`. The chain / 2D / 3D `neelXyzConfig` / `neelXyzState` definitions are bridged via `_eq_neelConfigOf` / `_eq_neelStateOf`. Tasaki ┬π2.5 eq. (2.5.2) graph-centric form | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y` action on the canonical N├σel state: for any `x έΚι y` with `A x έΚι A y`, `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y ┬╖ ╬ο_N├σel(A) = (1/2) ┬╖ |swap_{x, y} ╬ο_N├σel(A)έθσ - (1/4) ┬╖ ╬ο_N├σel(A)`. Tasaki ┬π2.5 eq. (2.5.3) graph-centric form. The chain / 2D / 3D `_adjacent` / `_wrap` bond actions are 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_spinHalfDot_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y` expectation on the canonical N├σel state: for any `x έΚι y` with `A x έΚι A y`, `έθρ╬ο_N├σel(A), ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y ┬╖ ╬ο_N├σel(A)έθσ = -(1/4)`. Tasaki ┬π2.5 (2.5.4) ingredient (graph-centric form). The chain / 2D / 3D `_eq_neg_one_quarter` companions reduce to this via the `_eq_neelStateOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `inner_neelStateOf_szsz_neelStateOf_antiparallel` | generic per-bond `┼ε^z_x ┬╖ ┼ε^z_y` correlation on the canonical N├σel state: for any `A x έΚι A y`, `έθρ╬ο_N├σel(A), ┼ε^z_x ┬╖ ┼ε^z_y ┬╖ ╬ο_N├σel(A)έθσ = -(1/4)`. Diagonal half of Tasaki ┬π2.5 (2.5.4) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf` | generic graph-centric Marshall sign `έΙΠ_{x έΙΙ A} (-1)^(╧Δ x)` for any finite vertex type `V`, sublattice indicator `A : V έΗΤ Bool`, and configuration `╧Δ : V έΗΤ Fin 2`. Aligns with the project-wide graph-centric design (CLAUDE.local.md) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_const_zero` | for any sublattice indicator `A`, the all-up Marshall sign is `marshallSignOf A (const 0) = 1`. Generic counterpart of `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` etc.; those are now 1-line corollaries via the `_eq_marshallSignOf` bridges | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig` / `marshallSignChainConfig_neelChainConfig` | the Marshall sign `(-1)^(N_A^έΗΥ)` for spin-1/2 configurations on the parity-coloured chain `Fin (2K)`, encoded as `έΙΠ_{x even} (-1)^(╧Δ x)`; specialisation to the N├σel configuration gives sign `+1` (no down spins on sublattice `A`). Foundational definition for the Marshall basis change underpinning the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬π2.5). **Deprecated** as of 2026-04-22 in favour of the generic `marshallSignOf` (the chain / 2D / 3D Marshall sign defs are kept for backward compatibility but new code should prefer the generic form) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_eq_marshallSignOf` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the chain / 2D / 3D parity-coloured Marshall signs are precisely `marshallSignOf` instantiated at the corresponding parity colouring | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig` / `marshallSignSquareConfig_neelSquareConfig` | 2D analogue: Marshall sign `έΙΠ_{(i,j) with i+j even} (-1)^(╧Δ (i,j))` on `Fin (2K) ├Ω Fin (2L)`; equals `+1` on the 2D checkerboard N├σel configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_neelCubicConfig` | 3D analogue: Marshall sign `έΙΠ_{((i,j),k) with i+j+k even} (-1)^(╧Δ ((i,j),k))` on `(Fin (2K) ├Ω Fin (2L)) ├Ω Fin (2M)`; equals `+1` on the 3D cubic checkerboard N├σel configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down chain configurations: `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 0) = 1` and `marshallSignChainConfig K (const 1) = (-1)^K` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 2D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down checkerboard: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `2KL` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(2KL) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignCubicConfig_const_zero` / `_const_one` | 3D Marshall sign on the all-up / all-down cubic: both equal `+1` (the all-down case has `4KLM` down spins on `A`, so `(-1)^(4KLM) = 1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig` | Marshall sign under the global spin-flip on the chain: `marshallSignChainConfig K (flipConfig ╧Δ) = (-1)^K ┬╖ marshallSignChainConfig K ╧Δ`. Each of the K even-indexed sites contributes `-1`. Proof: `Finset.prod_mul_distrib` + helper `prod_alternating_neg_one` collapses the contributing factor product to `(-1)^K`, with the per-site identity `(-1)^((1-s).val) = (-1)┬╖(-1)^(s.val)` closed by `fin_cases` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignSquareConfig_flipConfig` / `marshallSignCubicConfig_flipConfig` | 2D / 3D Marshall sign invariant under the global spin-flip (the contributing factor product `(-1)^(2KL)` resp. `(-1)^(4KLM)` equals `+1` for all K, L, M) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState` / `_neelChainConfig` | Marshall-rotated chain basis state `marshallSignChainConfig K ╧Δ έΑλ basisVec ╧Δ`; specialisation at the N├σel configuration coincides with `neelChainState K` (since the Marshall sign of the N├σel state is `+1`) | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSquareState` / `_neelSquareConfig` | 2D Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelSquareState K L` at the N├σel configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallCubicState` / `_neelCubicConfig` | 3D cubic Marshall-rotated checkerboard state; coincides with `neelCubicState K L M` at the N├σel configuration | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallSignChainConfig_flipConfig_neelChainConfig` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | Marshall sign on the *flipped* N├σel configuration: `(-1)^K` (1D), `+1` (2D), `+1` (3D) έΑΦ direct compositions of `_flipConfig` and `_neelChainConfig` | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallChainState_flipConfig_eq_timeReversalSpinHalfMulti` / `_square_` / `_cubic_` | the Marshall-rotated *flipped* N├σel state coincides with the time-reversed N├σel state in 1D, 2D, 3D έΑΦ both sides equal the same explicit `(-1)^K` (1D) or `+1` (2D, 3D) scaled basis vector. Establishes a direct bridge between the Marshall basis change (Tasaki ┬π2.5 / Marshall-Lieb-Mattis) and the time-reversal operator (Tasaki ┬π2.3) on the N├σel ground-state ansatz | `Quantum/NeelState.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis A ╧Δ` | **Marshall-dressed standard basis state** `:= marshallSignOf A ╧Δ έΑλ basisVec ╧Δ` on a generic finite vertex type `V` with sublattice indicator `A : V έΗΤ Bool` (Tasaki ┬π2.5 eq. (2.5.8), p. 41). The dressing produces a basis in which the spin-1/2 antiferromagnetic Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a connected bipartite graph has all off-diagonal matrix elements `έΚν 0` (Marshall sign trick), the input to the PerronέΑΥFrobenius proof of the MLM theorem | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_self` / `_of_ne` / `_apply` | pointwise rules: `╬ρ╠Δ^╧Δ ╧Δ = marshallSignOf A ╧Δ`; `╬ρ╠Δ^╧Δ ╧Ε = 0` for `╧Ε έΚι ╧Δ`; explicit `╬ρ╠Δ^╧Δ ╧Ε = marshallSignOf A ╧Δ ┬╖ basisVec ╧Δ ╧Ε` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one` | each factor of `marshallSignOf` is `┬▒1`, so the sign squares to `1`: `(marshallSignOf A ╧Δ)┬▓ = 1` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_inner` | orthonormality of the Marshall-dressed basis under the real bilinear pairing: `╬μ_╧Ε ╬ρ╠Δ^╧Δ ╧Ε ┬╖ ╬ρ╠Δ^╧Β ╧Ε = if ╧Β = ╧Δ then 1 else 0` (combines `basisVec_inner` with `marshallSignOf_sq_eq_one`) | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `marshallDressedBasis_mem_magnetizationSubspace` / `_zero` | the dressed basis state lies in the same magnetisation-`M` subspace `H_M = H_{╧Δ╠Ε/2}` as the underlying `basisVec ╧Δ` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.10)); the `_zero` specialisation places it in `H_0` when `╬μ_x ╧Δ_x = 0` | `Quantum/MarshallDressedBasis.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | every matrix entry of the two-site spin product `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y` is **real**: `((spinHalfDot x y) ╧Δ ╧Δ').im = 0` for all `x, y, ╧Δ, ╧Δ'`. Case analysis on `x = y` / parallel / antiparallel via the existing `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_{parallel,antiparallel}` action lemmas. **Property (i) ingredient** for the MarshallέΑΥLiebέΑΥMattis theorem (Tasaki ┬π2.5, p. 41) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_im_eq_zero` | for **real** coupling `J : ╬δ έΗΤ ╬δ έΗΤ έΕΓ` (`(J x y).im = 0` for all `x, y`), every matrix entry of the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ╬μ_{x,y} J(x,y) ┬╖ spinHalfDot x y` is real: `((heisenbergHamiltonian J) ╧Δ ╧Δ').im = 0`. έΕζ-linearity + `spinHalfDot_apply_im_eq_zero` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_im_eq_zero` | the Marshall sign `marshallSignOf A ╧Δ` is **real**: `(marshallSignOf A ╧Δ).im = 0`. Each factor of the product is `┬▒1 έΙΙ έΕζ` (either `1` or `(-1 : έΕΓ)^(╧Δ x : έΕΧ)` with `(╧Δ x : έΕΧ) έΙΙ {0, 1}`); products of reals are real | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_im_eq_zero` | **MLM Property (i)**: for real coupling `J`, the dressed Heisenberg bilinear pairing `╬μ_╧Ε \|╬ρ╠Δ^╧Δέθσ ╧Ε ┬╖ (H ┬╖ \|╬ρ╠Δ^{╧Δ'}έθσ) ╧Ε` is real (Tasaki ┬π2.5, p. 41 in the proof of Theorem 2.2). Reduces to `marshallSignOf A ╧Δ ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧Δ' ┬╖ H ╧Δ ╧Δ'` (each factor real) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Realness.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | for any operator `M`, the dressed bilinear pairing factorises: `╬μ_╧Ε \|╬ρ╠Δ^╧Δέθσ ╧Ε ┬╖ (M ┬╖ \|╬ρ╠Δ^{╧Δ'}έθσ) ╧Ε = marshallSignOf A ╧Δ ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧Δ' ┬╖ M ╧Δ ╧Δ'`. Generalises the inner-product computation used in Property (i) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `marshallSignOf_mul_marshallSignOf_basisSwap_of_bipartite_antiparallel` | **Marshall sign relation**: for a bond `{x, y}` crossing the bipartition (`A x έΚι A y`) with `╧Δ` antiparallel at `{x, y}` (`╧Δ x έΚι ╧Δ y`), `marshallSignOf A ╧Δ * marshallSignOf A (basisSwap ╧Δ x y) = -1`. The combined product over `╬δ` of pairwise factors collapses: outside `{x, y}` each pairwise factor is `(┬▒1)┬▓ = 1`; at the unique site in `A έΙσ {x, y}` the pair contributes `(-1)^(╧Δ x + ╧Δ y) = -1` since `╧Δ x έΚι ╧Δ y`; the other site of `{x, y}` lies outside `A` and contributes `1` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos` | per-bond non-positivity: for `╧Δ έΚι ╧Δ'` and any bond `(x, y)` with real non-negative `J(x, y)` supported on bipartite bonds, the contribution `marshallSignOf A ╧Δ ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧Δ' ┬╖ J(x,y) ┬╖ (spinHalfDot x y) ╧Δ ╧Δ'` to the dressed off-diagonal element has non-positive real part. Case analysis on `(spinHalfDot x y) ╧Δ ╧Δ'` (zero off-diagonal except at `╧Δ = basisSwap ╧Δ' x y`, antiparallel ╧Δ', `x έΚι y`) combined with the Marshall sign relation | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `dot_marshallDressed_heisenbergHamiltonian_marshallDressed_re_nonpos_of_ne` | **MLM Property (ii)** (Tasaki ┬π2.5, p. 41): for real non-negative `J` supported on bipartite bonds and `╧Δ έΚι ╧Δ'`, the dressed off-diagonal Heisenberg pairing `╬μ_╧Ε \|╬ρ╠Δ^╧Δέθσ ╧Ε ┬╖ (H ┬╖ \|╬ρ╠Δ^{╧Δ'}έθσ) ╧Ε` has non-positive real part. Sum bond-by-bond using `bond_dressed_contribution_re_nonpos`. The **Marshall sign trick** at the heart of the MarshallέΑΥLiebέΑΥMattis Theorem 2.2 proof | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MarshallSignTrick.lean` |
| `SwapStep`, `SwapReachable` | one-step swap relation `╧Δ έΗο basisSwap ╧Δ x y` along a graph edge `(x, y)` with `╧Δ x έΚι ╧Δ y`; reflexive transitive closure for multi-step reachability | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_walk_of_ne` | for any `G`-walk from `x` to `y` and `╧Δ x έΚι ╧Δ y`, `SwapReachable G ╧Δ (basisSwap ╧Δ x y)`. Walk induction with case analysis on `╧Δ z` at intermediate vertex (Tasaki p. 41 "Proof of Property (iii)" Lemma) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_reachable_of_ne` / `_preconnected_` | for any `x, y` reachable in `G` (or any `x, y` if `G` preconnected) with `╧Δ x έΚι ╧Δ y`, the swap is reachable. **MLM Property (iii) ingredient** (Tasaki ┬π2.5 p. 41) έΑΦ combined with iteration over the magnetisation-difference, gives PerronέΑΥFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/Connectivity.lean` |
| `HέΓΑIndex ╬δ` | index type `{╧Δ : ╬δ έΗΤ Fin 2 // magnetization ╬δ ╧Δ = 0}` for the zero-magnetisation subspace `H_0`; `Fintype` and `DecidableEq` instances | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0` | real-valued matrix on `HέΓΑIndex ╬δ` with entries `Re (marshallSignOf A ╧Δ ┬╖ marshallSignOf A ╧Ε ┬╖ (H_J)_{╧Δ,╧Ε})` έΑΦ the matrix to which Tasaki's PerronέΑΥFrobenius proof of MLM applies | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_isSymm` | the matrix is **symmetric** for real symmetric `J` (Hermiticity of Heisenberg + realness of entries) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_offdiag_nonpos` | **off-diagonal entries are non-positive** for real non-negative bipartite `J` and distinct `╧Δ έΚι ╧Ε`, packaged from PR ╬▒-3's Property (ii) via `dot_marshallDressed_mulVec_marshallDressed_eq` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Matrix.lean` |
| `magnetization_basisSwap` | `basisSwap ╧Δ x y` preserves total magnetisation. Proof uses the identification `basisSwap ╧Δ x y = ╧Δ έΙα Equiv.swap x y` (the swap is a permutation of `╬δ`); the magnetisation `έΙΣ_z spinSign(╧Δ z)` is invariant under such reindexing (`Equiv.sum_comp`). Key ingredient for Tasaki ┬π2.5 p. 42 Proposition (equal-magnetisation reachability) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetization.lean` |
| `disagreementSet` / `configDist` | the set / count of sites where `╧Δ` and `╧Δ'` disagree; `configDist_eq_zero_iff` characterises configuration equality | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization` | for `╧Δ έΚι ╧Δ'` with equal magnetisation, there exist sites `x` (with `╧Δ x = 0, ╧Δ' x = 1`) and `y` (with `╧Δ y = 1, ╧Δ' y = 0`). Pigeonhole/cardinality argument: the `(0, 1)`-disagreement and `(1, 0)`-disagreement sets have equal cardinality from magnetisation equality, and the disagreement set is non-empty for `╧Δ έΚι ╧Δ'` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `configDist_basisSwap_lt` | swapping at sites `x έΙΙ D01`, `y έΙΙ D10` strictly decreases the configuration distance to `╧Δ'`. The disagreement set strictly shrinks (`x` newly agrees with `╧Δ'` after swap) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `swapReachable_of_eq_magnetization` | **Tasaki ┬π2.5 p. 42 Proposition**: any two configurations `╧Δ`, `╧Δ'` with the same total magnetisation are connected by a chain of single-edge bond swaps, on a connected graph. Strong induction on `configDist`, reducing by `έΚξ 2` per step via the swap pair from `exists_swap_pair_of_eq_magnetization`. **Final ingredient** for PerronέΑΥFrobenius irreducibility on `H_M` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/EqMagnetizationReachable.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted` | the shifted matrix `B := c┬╖I έΙΤ M` on `HέΓΑIndex ╬δ`. Used as input to PerronέΑΥFrobenius: `B` is symmetric, has non-negative off-diagonal (sign flip of `M`'s non-positive off-diagonal), and non-negative diagonal when `c έΚξ M ╧Δ ╧Δ` for all `╧Δ`. The maximum eigenvalue of `B` corresponds to the minimum eigenvalue of `M` (the H_0 ground state of the AFM Heisenberg) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` / `_nonneg` (`_offdiag_nonneg`, `_diag_nonneg`) | symmetry and (off-diagonal / full) non-negativity of `B` under the appropriate hypotheses on `J` and `c` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0Shifted.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap` | the off-diagonal matrix entry `(spinHalfDot x y) ╧Δ (basisSwap ╧Δ x y) = 1/2` for `x έΚι y` and antiparallel `╧Δ_x έΚι ╧Δ_y`. Building block for the explicit Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations needed for PerronέΑΥFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotSwapEntry.lean` |
| `basisSwap_basisSwap_ne_self_of_ne_bond` | combinatorial helper: for `x έΚι y`, `╧Δ_x έΚι ╧Δ_y`, and `(u, v) έΙΚ {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the configuration `basisSwap (basisSwap ╧Δ x y) u v έΚι ╧Δ`. Site analysis: `╧Δ` and `╧Δ' = basisSwap ╧Δ x y` differ at exactly `{x, y}`, so for the iterated swap to return to `╧Δ`, the swap sites `{u, v}` must coincide with `{x, y}`. | `spinHalfDot_apply_basisSwap_off_bond_eq_zero` | for `╧Δ' = basisSwap ╧Δ x y` (with `x έΚι y`, `╧Δ_x έΚι ╧Δ_y`) and any `(u, v) έΙΚ {(x, y), (y, x)}`, the matrix entry `(spinHalfDot u v) ╧Δ ╧Δ' = 0`. Three cases: `u = v` (diagonal), `u έΚι v` parallel ╧Δ' (constant action), `u έΚι v` antiparallel + off-bond (combinatorial helper) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SpinDotOffBond.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_apply_basisSwap` | the Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related configurations: `(heisenbergHamiltonian J) ╧Δ (basisSwap ╧Δ x y) = (J x y + J y x) / 2`. Decomposes the double sum and uses ╬▒-5e (active bond = 1/2) + ╬▒-5g (off-bond = 0). For symmetric `J`, simplifies to `J x y` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/HeisenbergSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressed_pairing_basisSwap_eq` / `dressedHeisenbergMatrixH0_apply_basisSwap` | the dressed Heisenberg matrix entry on swap-related H_0 configurations: complex-level value `-J(x, y)` (Marshall sign trick ├Ω Heisenberg formula ├Ω symmetric `J`), real-part value `-(J x y).re`. Combined with `J(x, y).re > 0` on graph edges gives strict negativity of M off-diagonal at swap pairs, hence strict positivity of `B = c┬╖I έΙΤ M` έΑΦ the input for PerronέΑΥFrobenius irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/DressedSwapValue.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pos_of_basisSwap` | strict positivity `0 < B ╧Δ ╧Ε` on swap-related H_0 configurations with positive symmetric bipartite `J`. | `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_path` | generic matrix-power positivity from a positive path: for non-negative matrix `B` and a path `p_0, ..., p_{k+1}` with `B(p_i, p_{i+1}) > 0` on every consecutive pair, `(B^(k+1))(p_0)(p_{k+1}) > 0`. Induction on `k` using `pow_succ` + `Finset.sum_pos'`. Used to lift single-step swap positivity (╬▒-5j) to multi-step matrix-power positivity for PF irreducibility | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowPath.lean` |
| `matrix_pow_succ_pos_of_pow_pos_step` | one-step extension: if `(B^m) ╧Δ ╧Ε > 0` and `B ╧Ε ╧Β > 0` for non-negative `B`, then `(B^(m+1)) ╧Δ ╧Β > 0`. Inductive building block for ReflTransGen-style matrix-power lifting | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/MatrixPowExtend.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_pow_pos_of_swapReachable` | for `╧Δ : HέΓΑIndex ╬δ` and any `╬╛` with `Relation.ReflTransGen (SwapStep G) ╧Δ.val ╬╛`, there exists `m` with `(B^m) ╧Δ έθρ╬╛, h_magέθσ > 0`. Induction on `ReflTransGen`: refl gives `m = 0`, tail extends by one swap using ╬▒-5j (single-step swap positivity) and ╬▒-5l (one-step matrix-power extension). | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isIrreducible` | **`B = c ┬╖ I έΙΤ M` is irreducible** on `H_0` for connected bipartite `G` with positive symmetric real coupling supported on `G`-edges and shift constant `c > M ╧Δ ╧Δ` (strict). Cases on `╧Δ = ╧Ε` (use diagonal positivity) vs `╧Δ έΚι ╧Ε` (use ╬▒-5c reachability + ╬▒-5m matrix-power lift). Final input for PerronέΑΥFrobenius application | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0ShiftedIrreducible.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isHermitian` | the shifted matrix is Hermitian (= symmetric for real matrices). Wraps `dressedHeisenbergShifted_isSymm` (PR ╬▒-5d) into the IsHermitian form needed by PerronέΑΥFrobenius | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/H0PFApplication.lean` |
| `dressedHeisenbergShifted_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **PerronέΑΥFrobenius applied to `B = c ┬╖ I έΙΤ M` on `H_0`**: existence of a strictly positive eigenvector `v` for some real eigenvalue `╬╝`, and uniqueness up to positive scalar. | `bipartiteGraphFromA` | the complete bipartite graph on `╬δ` from sublattice indicator `A : ╬δ έΗΤ Bool`: vertices `x, y` are adjacent iff `A x έΚι A y`. | `bipartiteGraphFromA_preconnected` | `bipartiteGraphFromA A` is preconnected when both sublattices are non-empty. | `dressedHeisenbergShifted_toy_exists_pos_eigenvec_max` / `_pos_eigenvec_unique` | **Matrix-level Tasaki (2.5.4) for the toy Hamiltonian**: the shifted toy matrix `B_toy = c ┬╖ I έΙΤ M_toy` (under both-sublattices-nonempty + diagonal-shift hypothesis) has a unique-up-to-positive-scalar strictly positive eigenvector. | `sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOp{2,3,1}` | spin-`S` sublattice SU(2) algebra: `[┼ε_A^╬▒, ┼ε_A^╬▓] = i ╬╡^╬▒╬▓╬│ ┼ε_A^╬│`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commutator_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir self-invariance: `[(┼ε_A)┬▓, ┼ε_A^(╬▒)] = 0`, equivalently `Commute (┼ε_A)┬▓ ┼ε_A^(╬▒)`. | `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_sublatticeSpinSOp{1,2,3}_complement` | spin-`S` sublattice Casimir vs complement axis-╬▒: `Commute (┼ε_A)┬▓ ┼ε_┬υA^(╬▒)` for ╬▒ έΙΙ {1, 2, 3}. | `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_jointCasimirEigenvector` / `neelStateOfS_heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_expectation_via_joint` | **spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian eigenvalue on simultaneous Casimir eigenvectors**: for `v` a joint eigenvector of `(┼ε_tot)┬▓`, `(┼ε_A)┬▓`, `(┼ε_┬υA)┬▓` at eigenvalues `╬▒, ╬▓_A, ╬▓_B`, `─ν_toy_S ┬╖ v = (╬▒ έΙΤ ╬▓_A έΙΤ ╬▓_B) έΑλ v` and `έθρv, ─ν_toy_S vέθσ = (╬▒ έΙΤ ╬▓_A έΙΤ ╬▓_B) ┬╖ έθρv, vέθσ`. The predicted Theorem 2.3 spin magnitude is bounded above by the maximum total spin `m_max = \|╬δ\|┬╖S`. Direct from `\|\|A\| έΙΤ \|┬υA\|\| έΚν \|A\| + \|┬υA\| = \|╬δ\|` (PR #2870) (PR #2874, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightNormLeMMax.lean`) |

| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_lt_mMax_quad_of_non_saturated` | **Unified strict max bound: `max(canonical, complement) < m_max┬╖(m_max+1)`** at non-saturated (`\|A\| έΚξ 1`, `\|┬υA\| έΚξ 1`, `N έΚξ 1`): contrapositive of PR #2970 combined with PR #2972 upper bound. The physical predicted (┼ε_tot)┬▓ eigenvalue is strictly below the fully-polarised ferromagnetic value at non-saturated. Unifies PR #2945 / PR #2946 (orientation-specialised strict bounds) via the physical max quantity (PR #2975, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxLtMMaxQuad.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_add_min_eq_two_norm_sq` | **`max + min` of canonical/complement predicted (┼ε_tot)┬▓ eigenvalues = `2┬╖έΑΨbiwέΑΨ┬▓`** unconditionally: companion of PR #2968 (max έΙΤ min = `2┬╖έΑΨbiwέΑΨ`). Direct from PR #2966 + PR #2967 via `ring`. Equivalent to PR #2963 (canonical + complement sum) since `max + min = canonical + complement` (PR #2976, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxAddMin.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_sub_min_pos_iff_unbalanced` | **Iff: `max έΙΤ min > 0 έΗΦ unbalanced`** at `N έΚξ 1`: the canonical-complement eigenvalue gap (proportional to `έΑΨbiwέΑΨ`) vanishes iff balanced. Direct from PR #2968 (gap = `2┬╖έΑΨbiwέΑΨ`) + PR #2855 (`έΑΨbiwέΑΨ = 0 έΗΦ balanced`). Companion of PR #2971 (`max > 0 έΗΦ unbalanced`) (PR #2977, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxSubMinPosIffUnbalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_abs_canonical_sub_complement_eq_two_norm` | **`\|canonical έΙΤ complement\| = 2┬╖έΑΨbiwέΑΨ`** unconditionally: unsigned version of PR #2960. Equivalent to PR #2968 (`max έΙΤ min = 2┬╖έΑΨbiwέΑΨ`) since `max έΙΤ min = \|canonical έΙΤ complement\|` (PR #2978, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueAbsDiff.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_norm_sq_add` | **`max(canonical, complement) = έΑΨbiwέΑΨ┬▓ + έΑΨbiwέΑΨ`** unconditionally: expanded polynomial form of PR #2966. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_eq_norm_sq_sub` | **`min(canonical, complement) = έΑΨbiwέΑΨ┬▓ έΙΤ έΑΨbiwέΑΨ`** unconditionally: expanded polynomial form of PR #2967. Companion of PR #2979 έΑΦ together makes the affine parameterisation `max = έΑΨbiwέΑΨ┬▓ + έΑΨbiwέΑΨ`, `min = έΑΨbiwέΑΨ┬▓ έΙΤ έΑΨbiwέΑΨ` explicit (PR #2980, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinEqNormSqSub.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_canonical_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **`max(canonical, complement) = canonical`** at proper orientation `\|┬υA\| έΚν \|A\|`: the canonical orientation gives the physical predicted eigenvalue exactly at proper orientation. Direct from PR #2964 + PR #2965 + `biw.re έΚξ 0` at proper orientation (PR #2981, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMaxEqCanonical.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_lt_mMax_quad_iff_non_saturated` | **Iff: `max(canonical, complement) < m_max┬╖(m_max+1) έΗΦ non-saturated`** at `N έΚξ 1`: the iff form of PR #2975. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_eq_complement_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **`min(canonical, complement) = complement`** at proper orientation `\|┬υA\| έΚν \|A\|`: complement is the "wrong" (min) eigenvalue at proper orientation. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_max_canonical_complement_eq_complement_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`max(canonical, complement) = complement`** at complement orientation `\|A\| έΚν \|┬υA\|`: complement is the physical (max) eigenvalue at complement orientation. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_canonical_complement_eq_canonical_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`min(canonical, complement) = canonical`** at complement orientation `\|A\| έΚν \|┬υA\|`: canonical is the "wrong" (min) eigenvalue at complement orientation. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_le_max` | **`min(canonical, complement) έΚν max(canonical, complement)`** unconditionally: trivial corollary of `min_le_max` for the canonical/complement eigenvalue pair. | `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_norm_pos_iff_card_ne` | **Iff: `έΑΨbiwέΑΨ > 0 έΗΦ unbalanced`** at `N έΚξ 1`: iff form combining `έΑΨbiwέΑΨ έΚξ 0` and PR #2855 (`έΑΨbiwέΑΨ = 0 έΗΦ balanced`). | `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_nonpos_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **`biw.re έΚν 0` at complement orientation `\|A\| έΚν \|┬υA\|`**: mirror of the existing `_re_nonneg_of_cardA_ge_cardNotA`. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_canonical_le_max` | **`canonical έΚν max(canonical, complement)`** unconditionally: trivial corollary of `le_max_left`. | `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_complement_le_max` | **`complement έΚν max(canonical, complement)`** unconditionally: companion of PR #2990. Trivial corollary of `le_max_right` (PR #2991, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueComplementLeMax.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_le_canonical` | **`min(canonical, complement) έΚν canonical`** unconditionally: trivial corollary of `min_le_left` (PR #2992, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinLeCanonical.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyGroundStateSubspacePredicted_totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_min_le_complement` | **`min(canonical, complement) έΚν complement`** unconditionally: companion of PR #2992. Trivial corollary of `min_le_right` (PR #2993, file `Quantum/SpinS/PredictedEigenvalueMinLeComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_canonical_eigenvalue_re_sub_sublattice_casimir` | **Predicted min energy decomposition**: `(bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N).re = canonical_eigenvalue.re έΙΤ Re(s_A┬╖(s_A+1)) έΙΤ Re(s_B┬╖(s_B+1))`. Direct from the definition of `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted` via `Complex.sub_re`. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_max_eigenvalue_re_sub_sublattice_casimir_of_cardNotA_le_cardA` | **Predicted min energy via max (physical) eigenvalue at proper orientation**: at `\|┬υA\| έΚν \|A\|`, `(predicted_min_energy).re = max(canonical, complement) έΙΤ Re(s_A┬╖(s_A+1)) έΙΤ Re(s_B┬╖(s_B+1))`. Combines PR #2994 + PR #2981 έΑΦ uses the physical predicted `(┼ε_tot)┬▓` eigenvalue (PR #2995, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReAtProperOrientation.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_eq_min_eigenvalue_re_sub_sublattice_casimir_of_cardA_le_cardNotA` | **Predicted min energy via min (wrong-orientation) eigenvalue at complement orientation**: at `\|A\| έΚν \|┬υA\|`, `(predicted_min_energy).re = min(canonical, complement) έΙΤ Re(s_A┬╖(s_A+1)) έΙΤ Re(s_B┬╖(s_B+1))`. Companion of PR #2995 έΑΦ at complement orientation, the canonical-formula uses the smaller `min` eigenvalue, underestimating the physical min energy. Combines PR #2994 + PR #2986 (PR #2996, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReAtComplementOrientation.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_add_complement` | **Complement-orientation sum identity**: `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted A N + bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted (┬υA) N = έΙΤ\|A\|┬╖\|┬υA\|┬╖N┬▓ έΙΤ \|╬δ\|┬╖N`. Companion of the existing `_sub_complement` (gap identity). Together they give the affine sandwich: `predicted_min A = έΙΤ\|A\|┬╖\|┬υA\|┬╖N┬▓/2 έΙΤ \|┬υA\|┬╖N` and `predicted_min (┬υA) = έΙΤ\|A\|┬╖\|┬υA\|┬╖N┬▓/2 έΙΤ \|A\|┬╖N` (PR #2997, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAddComplement.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_eq_complement_iff_card_eq` | **Iff: `predicted_min_energy A = predicted_min_energy (┬υA) έΗΦ balanced`** at `N έΚξ 1`: the two orientations give the same predicted min energy **iff** balanced sublattices. Direct from the existing `_sub_complement` gap identity `= (\|A\| έΙΤ \|┬υA\|)┬╖N` + `N έΚξ 1` cancellation (PR #2998, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedEqComplementIffBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_le_predictedSymm_re` | **`(predicted_min A).re έΚν (predictedSymm A).re`** unconditionally: canonical-orientation predicted min energy is bounded above by the symmetric (min-aware) form. Difference: `(predictedSymm έΙΤ predicted_min A).re = (\|┬υA\| έΙΤ min(\|A\|, \|┬υA\|))┬╖N έΚξ 0`. Equality at `\|┬υA\| έΚν \|A\|` (where `min = \|┬υA\|`); strict at `\|A\| < \|┬υA\|` and `N έΚξ 1` (PR #2999, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedReLeSymmRe.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_complement_re_le_predictedSymm_re` | **`(predicted_min ┬υA).re έΚν (predictedSymm A).re`** unconditionally: mirror of PR #2999 έΑΦ complement-orientation predicted min energy bounded above by symmetric form. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_eq_predictedSymm_re_iff_balanced` | **`min(...) = (predictedSymm A).re έΗΦ \|A\| = \|┬υA\|`** at `N έΚξ 1`: at balanced, both orientation-specific predicted min energies coincide with `predictedSymm`; at unbalanced (and `N έΚξ 1`), the min sits strictly below. | `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_sub_predicted_re_eq` | **Explicit difference formula: `(predictedSymm A).re έΙΤ (predicted_min A).re = (\|┬υA\| έΙΤ min(\|A\|, \|┬υA\|))┬╖N`** unconditionally. At proper orientation (`\|┬υA\| έΚν \|A\|`) the difference vanishes; at complement orientation (`\|A\| έΚν \|┬υA\|`) the difference is `(\|┬υA\| έΙΤ \|A\|)┬╖N έΚξ 0` (PR #3019, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmSubPredictedReEq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymm_re_sub_complement_predicted_re_eq` | **Explicit difference formula (complement): `(predictedSymm A).re έΙΤ (predicted_min ┬υA).re = (\|A\| έΙΤ min(\|A\|, \|┬υA\|))┬╖N`** unconditionally. Mirror of PR #3019. At complement orientation (`\|A\| έΚν \|┬υA\|`) the difference vanishes; at proper orientation the difference is `(\|A\| έΙΤ \|┬υA\|)┬╖N έΚξ 0` (PR #3020, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSymmSubComplementPredictedReEq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_sub_complement_re_eq` | **Signed difference: `(predicted_min A).re έΙΤ (predicted_min ┬υA).re = (\|A\| έΙΤ \|┬υA\|)┬╖N`** unconditionally. The quadratic terms `έΙΤ\|A\|┬╖\|┬υA\|┬╖N┬▓/2` cancel, leaving only the linear orientation gap. Matches PR #3012's spread formula `max έΙΤ min = \|\|A\| έΙΤ \|┬υA\|\|┬╖N` έΑΦ gives alternative spread characterisation via absolute signed difference (PR #3022, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedAbsSubComplementEq.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_max_sub_min_complement_re_eq_zero_of_balanced` | **Spread vanishes at balanced (unconditional in `N`)**: `\|A\| = \|┬υA\| έΗΤ spread = 0`. Companion of PR #3013 covering the `N = 0` edge case where PR #3013's iff (which requires `N έΚξ 1`) does not apply. Direct from PR #3012 spread formula (PR #3024, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadAtBalanced.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_re_sub_complement_re_eq_zero_of_balanced` | **Signed difference vanishes at balanced (unconditional in `N`)**: `\|A\| = \|┬υA\| έΗΤ (pmA).re έΙΤ (pm┬υA).re = 0`. Since `\|┬υA\| = \|╬δ\|` at this edge, gives `spread = \|╬δ\|┬╖N` έΑΦ the maximum spread (PR #3027, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadAtComplementSaturated.lean`) |

Equality attained at saturated edges (PRs #3026/#3027) έΑΦ gives the maximum possible spread (PR #3028, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteToyMinEnergyPredictedSpreadLeCardMulN.lean`) |
| `bipartiteToyMinEnergyPredicted_min_complement_re_le_zero` | **Min of orientation-specific predicted min energies is non-positive: `min(...) έΚν 0`** unconditionally. | `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_pos_iff` | **Strict positivity iff for the imbalance-weight real part**: `0 < biw.re έΗΦ \|┬υA\| < \|A\| έΙπ 0 < N`. | `bipartiteImbalanceWeight_re_eq_zero_iff` | **Vanishing iff for the imbalance-weight real part**: `biw.re = 0 έΗΦ \|A\| = \|┬υA\| έΙρ N = 0`. Together with PRs #3151 + #3152, classifies the sign of `biw.re` completely: positive iff `|┬υA| < |A| έΙπ N > 0`; negative iff `|A| < |┬υA| έΙπ N > 0`; zero iff balanced or `N = 0` (PR #3153, file `Quantum/SpinS/BipartiteImbalanceWeightReEqZeroIff.lean`) |

(In the `\|A\| έΚξ \|┬υA\|` orientation, this signed weight equals the predicted Theorem 2.3 total spin `\|\|A\| έΙΤ \|┬υA\|\|┬╖S`; in the opposite orientation it is its negative.) Packages: bridge `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N) = bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N`; membership `neelStateOfS A N έΙΙ magSubspaceS ╬δ N (bipartiteImbalanceWeight A N)`; two edge cases (balanced bipartite έΗΤ weight 0; saturated bipartite έΗΤ weight m_max); non-negativity of the real part when `\|A\| έΚξ \|┬υA\|`. Direct specialisation of the spin-`S` Heisenberg Hamiltonian ladder commutators to `J = bipartiteCoupling A`. Completes the full SU(2) algebra commutator set (╬│-4 step 34) | `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianCasimir.lean` (PR #1076) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS` | spin-`S` toy Hamiltonian preserves each magnetization subspace: `v έΙΙ magSubspaceS ╬δ N M έΘΤ (─ν_toy_S ┬╖ v) έΙΙ magSubspaceS ╬δ N M`. | `totalSpinSSquared_commute_totalSpinSOp3` / `totalSpinSSquared_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS` / `_complement_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS` | spin-`S` Casimir operators preserve each magnetization subspace `magSubspaceS ╬δ N M`: `(┼ε_tot)┬▓`, `(┼ε_A)┬▓`, `(┼ε_┬υA)┬▓` each commute with `┼ε_tot^(3)` and so map `magSubspaceS` into itself. Spin-`S` mirror of the spin-`1/2` versions. Uses Cartan relations `[┼ε_tot^(3), ┼ε^έΙΥ_tot] = έΙΥ ┼ε^έΙΥ_tot` (╬│-4 step 37) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedState.lean` (PR #1079) |
