Direct corollary of ╬│-4 step 216 + ╬│-4 step 204 (z-axis cancels). Expresses the per-state transverse contribution as a pure combinatorial quantity (╬│-4 step 219) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1266) |
| `basisVecS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_expectation_general` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼ε_tot^(1))┬▓ + (┼ε_tot^(2))┬▓` on arbitrary `basisVecS ╧Δ` = `\|V\|┬╖N(N+2)/4 έΙΤ ╬μ_x (N/2 έΙΤ ╧Δx.val)┬▓`**. Direct corollary of ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 205 (`magEigenvalueS┬▓` cancels). Reduces to `\|V\|┬╖N/2` for N├σel on balanced bipartite graphs (matches ╬│-4 step 156) and to `\|╬δ\|/2` for spin-`1/2` (╬│-4 step 220) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1267) |
| `neelConfigOfS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **N├σel per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `╬μ_x (N/2 έΙΤ (neelConfigOfS A N x).val)┬▓ = \|V\|┬╖(N/2)┬▓`. Both extremes `╧Δx έΙΙ {0, N}` give `(N/2)┬▓`. Bridges ╬│-4 step 218 (general formula) to existing closed-form N├σel Casimir (╬│-4 step 221) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1268) |
| `allAlignedConfigS_z_eigenvalue_sq_sum` | **AllAligned per-site z-eigenvalue squared sum**: `╬μ_x (N/2 έΙΤ (allAlignedConfigS V N c x).val)┬▓ = \|V\|┬╖(N/2 έΙΤ c.val)┬▓`. Trivial direct computation since `╧Δx = c` constant. Bridges ╬│-4 step 218 to existing `allAlignedStateS_zero_/_last_expectation_totalSpinSSquared` (╬│-4 step 222) | `Quantum/SpinS/AllAlignedStateExpectations.lean` (PR #1269) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS` | **N├σel `┼ε_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N) = (\|A\| έΙΤ \|┬υA\|)┬╖N/2`. Direct from `magSumS_neelConfigOfS` (= `\|┬υA\|┬╖N`) and the filter-card decomposition `\|V\| = \|A\| + \|┬υA\|`. Named lemma extracted from inlined computation in `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_card_Lambda` (╬│-4 step 224) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelBasisVecS.lean` (PR #1271) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`S` N├σel Casimir re-derived via the general formula**: `<╬ο_N├σel \| (┼ε_tot)┬▓ \| ╬ο_N├σel> = ((\|A\|έΙΤ\|┬υA\|)┬╖N/2)┬▓ + \|V\|┬╖N/2` derived from ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 221 + ╬│-4 step 224 + `ring`. Unification check against the existing `_card_Lambda` form (╬│-4 step 127). Validates that the general formula correctly specializes to the N├σel-specific result (╬│-4 step 225) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeelBasisVecS.lean` (PR #1272) |
| `allAlignedStateS_zero/_last_totalSpinSSquared_via_general` | **Spin-`S` allAligned-up/down Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<╬ο_έΛν \| (┼ε_tot)┬▓ \| ╬ο_έΛν> = m_max┬╖(m_max+1)` (and same for `╬ο_έΛξ`) where `m_max = \|V\|┬╖N/2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 218 + ╬│-4 step 222 + `magEigenvalueS_allAlignedConfigS` + `ring`. Recovers existing `allAlignedStateS_zero_expectation_totalSpinSSquared` (╬│-4 step 226) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1273) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` N├σel Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<╬ο_N├σel \| (┼ε_tot)┬▓ \| ╬ο_N├σel> = ((\|A\|έΙΤ\|┬υA\|)/2)┬▓ + \|╬δ\|/2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 216 + `magnetization_neelConfigOf` + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 225 (╬│-4 step 227) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1274) |
| `basisVec_const_totalSpinHalfSquared_expectation_via_general` | **Spin-`1/2` const-state Casimir re-derived via general formula**: `<basisVec(const s) \| (┼ε_tot)┬▓ \| basisVec(const s)> = \|╬δ\|┬╖(\|╬δ\|+2)/4` for any `s : Fin 2`. Composition of ╬│-4 step 216 + `magnetization (const s) = ┬▒\|╬δ\|` + `(spinSign s)┬▓ = 1` + `ring`. Recovers existing ╬│-4 step 201 (╬│-4 step 228) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1275) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement` | **Complement N├σel `┼ε_tot^(3)` eigenvalue**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬υA) N) = (\|┬υA\|έΙΤ\|A\|)┬╖N/2`. Sublattice-swap symmetry; complement of ╬│-4 step 224. Direct corollary by applying ╬│-4 step 224 to `(┬υA)` (╬│-4 step 229) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1276) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_simplified` | **Cleaner form** of ╬│-4 step 229 with double negation `!!A` simplified to `A` via `Finset.filter_congr` + `Bool.not_not`: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬υA) N) = (\|┬υA\|έΙΤ\|A\|)┬╖N/2` (╬│-4 step 230) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1277) |
| `magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS_complement_eq_neg` | **Negation relation**: `magEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS (┬υA) N) = έΙΤmagEigenvalueS (neelConfigOfS A N)`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 224 + ╬│-4 step 230 + `ring`. The complement N├σel sits at the opposite `┼ε_tot^(3)` eigenvalue (╬│-4 step 231) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1278) |
| `magnetization_neelConfigOf_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` negation relation**: `magnetization ╬δ (neelConfigOf (┬υA)) = έΙΤmagnetization ╬δ (neelConfigOf A)`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 231; direct from existing `magnetization_neelConfigOf{,_complement}` + `ring` (╬│-4 step 232) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1279) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├σel**: `<╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)\|(┼ε_tot)┬▓\|╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)> = <╬ο_N├σel(A)\|(┼ε_tot)┬▓\|╬ο_N├σel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 227 (Casimir = `(M/2)┬▓+\|╬δ\|/2`) + ╬│-4 step 232 (`M(┬υA) = έΙΤM(A)`): `(M)┬▓` is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 233) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1280) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSSquared_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` Casimir is sublattice-swap invariant on N├σel**: `<╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)\|(┼ε_tot)┬▓\|╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)> = <╬ο_N├σel(A)\|(┼ε_tot)┬▓\|╬ο_N├σel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 218 + 221 + 231 + `ring`: `magEigenvalueS┬▓` is sign-flip invariant; `╬μ_x m_x┬▓` evaluates to `\|V\|┬╖(N/2)┬▓` for both A and ┬υA. Spin-`S` mirror of ╬│-4 step 233 (╬│-4 step 234) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1281) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` `(┼ε_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├σel**: `<╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)\|(┼ε_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)> = <╬ο_N├σel(A)\|(┼ε_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ο_N├σel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 205 + 231 + `ring`: squared eigenvalue is sign-flip invariant (╬│-4 step 235) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1282) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` `(┼ε_tot^(3))┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├σel**: `<╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)\|(┼ε_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)> = <╬ο_N├σel(A)\|(┼ε_tot^(3))┬▓\|╬ο_N├σel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 204 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 235 (╬│-4 step 236) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1283) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`S` transverse Casimir `(┼ε^1)┬▓+(┼ε^2)┬▓` is sublattice-swap invariant on N├σel**: equal expectations on `╬ο_N├σel(A)` and `╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)`. Both reduce to `\|V\|┬╖N/2` via ╬│-4 step 220 + 221 (╬│-4 step 237) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1284) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp12_sq_complement_eq` | **Spin-`1/2` transverse Casimir invariance on N├σel**: both expectations equal `\|╬δ\|/2`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 219 (╧Δ-independent constant). Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 237 (╬│-4 step 238) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1285) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`S` linear `┼ε_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├σel**: `<╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)\|┼ε_tot^(3)\|╬ο_N├σel(┬υA)> = έΙΤ<╬ο_N├σel(A)\|┼ε_tot^(3)\|╬ο_N├σel(A)>`. Direct from ╬│-4 step 206 + 231 (╬│-4 step 239) | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1286) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp3_complement_eq_neg` | **Spin-`1/2` linear `┼ε_tot^(3)` negates under sublattice swap on N├σel**. Direct from ╬│-4 step 207 + 232 + `ring`. Spin-`1/2` mirror of ╬│-4 step 239 (╬│-4 step 240) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeelGeneral.lean` (moved in refactor #41, PR #2879) (PR #1287) |
| `neelStateOfS_totalSpinSOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`S` N├σel transverse linear expectations vanish**: `<╬ο_N├σel\|┼ε_tot^(1,2)\|╬ο_N├σel> = 0`. Trivial corollary of ╬│-4 step 214 applied to `╧Δ = neelConfigOfS A N` (╬│-4 step 241). Together with ╬│-4 step 91 (`<┼ε_tot^(3)> = magEigenvalueS_neelConfigOfS`), gives the complete per-axis profile `(0, 0, (\|A\|έΙΤ\|┬υA\|)┬╖N/2)` | `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1288) |
| `neelStateOf_totalSpinHalfOp1/2_expectation` | **Spin-`1/2` N├σel transverse linear expectations vanish**: same as ╬│-4 step 241 for spin-`1/2`, derived from ╬│-4 step 215 (╬│-4 step 242) | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeCasimirNeel.lean` (PR #1289) |

The single-cluster Problem 2.5.a modules below are imported from the
build root again after the 2026-05-30 orphan-module sweep had removed
the earlier implementation.  The restored concrete tip is
`Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianConcreteClusters.lean`, and the
minimum-eigenvalue bridge in `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianMin.lean`
keeps the abstract Hamiltonian, energy hygiene, concrete
dimer/trimer/quartet/pentamer eigenvalue formulas, and variational
upper/lower-bound consumers live in the default Lean build
(PR #4032; PR #4033; PR #4034; PR #4035; PR #4036).

| `singleClusterHamiltonianS` | **Single-cluster (star-graph) Heisenberg Hamiltonian** (Tasaki Problem 2.5.a, p. 38): `H = ╬μ_{j=1}^z ┼ε_0 ┬╖ ┼ε_j` on `Fin (z + 1)` with central vertex `0` and `z` leaves. Ground-state energy `έΙΤS(1 + zS)` (╬│-5 step 243) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1290) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_isHermitian` | **Hermiticity** of the single-cluster Heisenberg Hamiltonian. Sum of Hermitian `spinSDot 0 j N` over `j έΙΙ univ.erase 0` (╬│-5 step 244) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1291) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_zero_z` | **Edge case `z = 0`**: `singleClusterHamiltonianS 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = έΙΖ` in `Fin 1`. Tasaki's formula `έΙΤS(1 + zS)` is intended for `z έΚξ 1` (╬│-5 step 245) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1292) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<╬ο_έΛν\|H\|╬ο_έΛν> = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian. Far above Tasaki's GS energy `έΙΤS(1+zS)` since all-up is in the maximum-`s_tot` Casimir sector, not the minimum (╬│-5 step 246) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1293) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allAligned_expectation` | **AllAligned-c expectation**: `<╬ο_aligned(c)\|H\|╬ο_aligned(c)> = z┬╖(N/2 έΙΤ c.val)┬▓`. Generalises ╬│-5 step 246 (the `c = 0` case). For `c = Fin.last N` (all-down) gives same `z┬╖(N/2)┬▓` (╬│-5 step 247) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1294) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_allDown_expectation` | **All-down expectation**: `<╬ο_έΛξ\|H\|╬ο_έΛξ> = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓`. Direct specialisation of ╬│-5 step 247 at `c = Fin.last N` (╬│-5 step 248) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1295) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Leaf-spin operators on `Fin (z+1)`**: `┼ε_R^(╬▒) = ╬μ_{j έΚι 0} onSiteS j ┼ε^(╬▒)` for axes `╬▒ = 1, 2, 3`. Sum over leaves of single-site `┼ε^(╬▒)` (excludes central vertex `0`). Building blocks for the upcoming Casimir decomposition `H = (┼ε_tot┬▓ έΙΤ ┼ε_0┬▓ έΙΤ ┼ε_R┬▓)/2` (╬│-5 step 249) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1296) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eq_dot_leaves` | **`┼ε_0 ┬╖ ┼ε_R` decomposition**: `H = ╬μ_╬▒ onSiteS 0 (┼ε^(╬▒)) ┬╖ leafSpinSOp_╬▒`. Direct distribution of left multiplication over the sum `╬μ_j (A * B_j) = A * (╬μ_j B_j)` via `Finset.mul_sum` (╬│-5 step 250) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1297) |
| `totalSpinSOp1/2/3_eq_onSite_zero_add_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Total = central + leaves** (per axis): `totalSpinSOp_╬▒ (Fin (z+1)) N = onSiteS 0 (┼ε^(╬▒)) + leafSpinSOp_╬▒ z N`. Direct from `Finset.sum_erase_add` + `add_comm` (╬│-5 step 251) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1298) |
| `leafSpinSSquared` | **Leaf-spin Casimir**: `┼ε_R┬▓ := (┼ε_R^(1))┬▓ + (┼ε_R^(2))┬▓ + (┼ε_R^(3))┬▓`, total-spin-squared restricted to leaves of `Fin (z+1)` (╬│-5 step 252) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1299) |
| `onSiteS_zero_commute_leafSpinSOp1/2/3` | **Center-leaf commutativity**: `Commute (onSiteS 0 ┼ε^(╬▒)) (leafSpinSOp_╬▒ z N)`. Direct from `Commute.sum_right` + `onSiteS_commute_of_ne` (disjoint sites) (╬│-5 step 253) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1300) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_eq_casimir_diff` | **Casimir decomposition**: `2┬╖H = (┼ε_tot)┬▓ έΙΤ ┼ε_0┬▓ έΙΤ ┼ε_R┬▓` for the single-cluster Hamiltonian, where `┼ε_0┬▓ = spinSDot 0 0 N` and `┼ε_R┬▓ = leafSpinSSquared z N`. Proof: expand `╬μ_╬▒ totalSpinSOp_╬▒┬▓ = ╬μ_╬▒ (onSite 0 + leaf_╬▒)┬▓` via ╬│-5 step 251 + `(a+b)┬▓ = a┬▓ + 2ab + b┬▓` (using ╬│-5 step 253 commutativity) + ╬│-5 step 250 (cross term identification) + `noncomm_ring` (╬│-5 step 254) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1301) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_smul_eq_casimir_diff` | **έΕΓ-smul Casimir form**: `(2 : έΕΓ) έΑλ H = (┼ε_tot)┬▓ έΙΤ ┼ε_0┬▓ έΙΤ ┼ε_R┬▓`. Direct corollary of ╬│-5 step 254 via `two_mul + two_smul` (╬│-5 step 255) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1302) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation` | **Casimir decomposition expectation form**: `2 ┬╖ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot┬▓\|v> έΙΤ <v\|S0┬▓\|v> έΙΤ <v\|SR┬▓\|v>` for any `v`. Direct corollary of ╬│-5 step 255 + linearity of `dotProduct + mulVec` over `smul` and subtraction (╬│-5 step 256) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1303) |
| `spinSDot_self_expectation_general` | **Single-site Casimir expectation**: `<v\|spinSDot x x N\|v> = (N(N+2)/4) ┬╖ <v\|v>` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix) + linearity. Used to evaluate the `S0┬▓` term in the Casimir decomposition (╬│-5 step 257) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1304) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_two_mul_expectation_simplified` | **Simplified Casimir expectation**: `2 ┬╖ <v\|H\|v> = <v\|Stot┬▓\|v> έΙΤ (N(N+2)/4)┬╖<v\|v> έΙΤ <v\|SR┬▓\|v>`. Combines ╬│-5 step 256 + 257; the `S0┬▓` term is now an explicit scalar multiple of the norm-squared (╬│-5 step 258) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonianCore.lean` (PR #1305) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_eigenvalue_of_joint_casimir_eigenvec` | **H eigenvalue from joint Casimir eigenvalues**: if `Stot┬▓┬╖v = ╬▒┬╖v` and `SR┬▓┬╖v = ╬▓┬╖v`, then `H┬╖v = (╬▒ έΙΤ N(N+2)/4 έΙΤ ╬▓)/2 ┬╖ v`. Reduces the GS-energy problem to enumerating admissible `(╬▒, ╬▓)` joint eigenvalues (╬│-5 step 259) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1306) |
| `spinSDot_self_mulVec_eq_smul` | **Single-site Casimir as scalar action**: `spinSDot x x N ┬╖ v = (N(N+2)/4) έΑλ v` for any `v`. Direct from `spinSDot_self` (= scalar matrix). Standalone helper extracted from ╬│-5 step 259 (╬│-5 step 260) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1307) |
| `leafSpinSOp1/2/3_zero_z` and `leafSpinSSquared_zero_z` | **Edge case `z=0`** (single vertex, no leaves): `leafSpinSOp_╬▒ 0 N = 0` and `leafSpinSSquared 0 N = 0` since `univ.erase 0 = έΙΖ` in `Fin 1` (╬│-5 step 261) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1308) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` | **`leafSpinSSquared` as double sum**: `leafSpinSSquared z N = ╬μ_{j,k έΙΙ univ.erase 0} spinSDot j k N`. Mirrors `totalSpinSSquared_eq_sum_spinSDot` for the leaf-only Casimir (╬│-5 step 262) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1309) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_allUp_expectation` | **All-up expectation**: `<╬ο_έΛν\|leafSpinSSquared z N\|╬ο_έΛν> = (zN/2)┬╖(zN/2 + 1) = s_R(s_R+1)` where `s_R = z┬╖(N/2)` is max leaf spin. Computed by rearranging ╬│-5 step 254 (Casimir decomposition) and applying closed forms for `<Stot┬▓>`, `<S0┬▓>`, `<H>` (╬│-5 step 263) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1310) |
| `singleClusterHamiltonianS_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form on allUp**: `H ┬╖ \|╬ο_έΛνέθσ = z┬╖(N/2)┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ο_έΛνέθσ`. Proof: each leaf-pair `spinSDot 0 j` at `j έΚι 0` gives `(N┬▓/4)┬╖\|╬ο_έΛνέθσ` (via `spinSDot_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero_of_ne`); sum over `z` leaves (╬│-5 step 264) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1311) |
| `leafSpinSSquared_mulVec_allAlignedStateS_zero` | **Eigenvector form** of `┼ε_R┬▓` on allUp: `┼ε_R┬▓ ┬╖ \|╬ο_έΛνέθσ = (zN/2)┬╖(zN/2+1) ┬╖ \|╬ο_έΛνέθσ`. Witnesses that `\|╬ο_έΛνέθσ` is in the maximum-leaf-spin Casimir sector `s_R = zN/2`. Combined with ╬│-5 step 264 + existing `Stot┬▓` eigenvector identity, confirms `\|╬ο_έΛνέθσ` is a joint eigenstate of `H, Stot┬▓, ┼ε_0┬▓, ┼ε_R┬▓`. Proof: rearrange ╬│-5 step 255 + apply (╬│-5 step 265) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterHamiltonian.lean` (PR #1312) |

| `exists_eigenvector_in_invariant_submodule` | **Invariant-submodule eigenvector bridge**: over an algebraically closed field, any non-zero finite-dimensional submodule invariant under an endomorphism contains a non-zero eigenvector of that endomorphism. The proof first extracts an eigenvector for the first endomorphism, then applies the one-operator bridge to the non-zero intersection of the original submodule with that eigenspace, using commutativity to prove invariance under the second endomorphism. This packages the next linear-algebra step for extracting total/leaf Casimir sector data inside the single-cluster magnetization/Hamiltonian component (╬│-5 step 333) | `Math/InvariantSubmoduleEigenvector.lean` (PR #4042) |
| `singleCluster_totalSpinSSquared_commute_leafSpinSSquared` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_commute_totalSpinSSquared` / `singleClusterHamiltonianS_commute_leafSpinSSquared` / `singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule` / `singleCluster_totalSpinSSquared_mulVecLin_commute_leafSpinSSquared_mulVecLin` / `singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule_totalSpinSSquared_invariant` / `singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule_leafSpinSSquared_invariant` / `exists_joint_casimir_eigenvector_in_singleClusterHamiltonianMagEigenspaceSubmodule` | **Single-cluster Casimir invariance bridge**: the Casimir decomposition `(2 : έΕΓ) έΑλ H = (┼ε_tot)┬▓ - ┼ε_0┬▓ - ┼ε_R┬▓`, the scalar central-site Casimir, and the total/leaf sublattice-Casimir commutation theorem show that the single-cluster Hamiltonian commutes with both the total and leaf Casimirs. Consequently the Hamiltonian eigenspace intersected with a fixed magnetization subspace is invariant under both Casimirs, and any non-zero such submodule contains a non-zero joint total/leaf Casimir eigenvector when the caller supplies the explicit algebraically closed field hypothesis `[IsAlgClosed έΕΓ]` (╬│-5 step 334) | `Quantum/SpinS/SingleClusterCasimirInvariance.lean` (PR #4043) |

The local-cluster wrappers specialize this to the Problem 2.5.a star-cluster energies, yielding the abstract lower bound `E_GS έΚξ -╬μ_{xέΙΙA} S(1 + degree(x) S)` with `S = N/2` once a graph Hamiltonian is decomposed into centered local cluster terms (╬│-6 step 339). Together with cross-commute, gives `Commute (┼ε_A)┬▓ (┼ε_tot^(╬▒))`, hence `Commute (┼ε_A)┬▓ (┼ε_tot)┬▓` | `Quantum/MarshallLiebMattis/SublatticeSpinCore.lean` |

| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `J : ╬δ έΗΤ ╬δ έΗΤ έΕΓ` and `M : έΕΓ`, `v έΙΙ H_M` implies `H ┬╖ v έΙΙ H_M` έΑΦ the operator-level statement that any Heisenberg Hamiltonian block-diagonalises against Tasaki ┬π2.2 (2.2.10), p. 22 magnetisation-sector decomposition (consequence of SU(2) invariance `[H, ┼εtot^(3)] = 0`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : έΕΓ`, `v έΙΙ H_M` implies `┼εtot^- ┬╖ v έΙΙ H_{M-1}` έΑΦ the standard SU(2) lowering ladder action via the Cartan relation `[┼εtot^(3), ┼εtot^-] = -┼εtot^-` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace_of_mem` | for any `M : έΕΓ`, `v έΙΙ H_M` implies `┼εtot^+ ┬╖ v έΙΙ H_{M+1}` έΑΦ the SU(2) raising ladder action via `[┼εtot^(3), ┼εtot^+] = +┼εtot^+` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspace.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_two_site` | for `╬δ = Fin 2` and any `╬╕`, the global rotation factors as `onSite 0 (├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕) * onSite 1 (├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕)` (general-╬╕ extension of Problem 2.2.b) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` | `onSite x (exp A) = exp (onSite x A)` έΑΦ bridge between single-site and many-body matrix exponential. Local Frobenius normed structure + `LinearMap.continuous_of_finiteDimensional` + `NormedSpace.map_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_eq_exp` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.11): `├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot = exp(-i╬╕ ┼ε_tot^(╬▒))`. Composes `spinHalfRot{╬▒}_eq_exp` (single site), `onSite_exp_eq_exp_onSite` (per-site bridge), and `Matrix.exp_sum_of_commute` (commuting-summand exp = noncommProd of exps) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_commute_of_commute` | Tasaki ┬π2.2 (2.2.12) έΗΤ (2.2.13): `Commute A (┼ε_tot^(╬▒)) έΗΤ Commute A (├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot)`. Generic operator version, follows from `Commute.exp_right` after rewriting `├δ` via `_eq_exp` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}_exp_commute_of_commute` | ladder version: `Commute A (┼ε^┬▒_tot) έΗΤ Commute A (exp(c έΑλ ┼ε^┬▒_tot))` for any `c : έΕΓ` (useful for U(1) symmetry) | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conjTranspose_mul_self` | `(├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot)ά┤┤ * ├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot = 1` (unitarity). Derived from `exp_mem_unitary_of_mem_skewAdjoint` after recognizing `-i╬╕ ┼ε_tot^(╬▒)` as skew-adjoint | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_conj_eq_self_of_commute` | Tasaki eq. (2.2.13) finite form: `Commute A (┼ε_tot^(╬▒)) έΗΤ (├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot)ά┤┤ * A * ├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕_tot = A`. Combines `_commute_of_commute` with unitarity | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |
| `IsInMagnetizationSubspace` | predicate for the magnetization-`M` eigenspace `H_M` (Tasaki eq. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace M` | the magnetization-`M` eigenspace as a `Submodule έΕΓ ((╬δ έΗΤ Fin 2) έΗΤ έΕΓ)` | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `basisVec_mem_magnetizationSubspace` | `|╧Δέθσ έΙΙ H_{|╧Δ|/2}` έΑΦ basis states lie in their magnetization subspace | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_disjoint` | distinct sectors `H_M έΛΥ H_{M'} = έΛξ` (`M έΚι M'`) έΑΦ eigenvalue uniqueness | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `iSup_magnetizationSubspace_eq_top` | `έρΗ_M H_M = έΛν` έΑΦ every vector decomposes as a sum across sectors | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_eq_eigenspace` | bridge `H_M = (┼ε_tot^(3) as End).eigenspace M` (used to inherit `iSupIndep`) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_iSupIndep` | `iSupIndep`: each sector is disjoint from the supremum of all others | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |
| `magnetizationSubspace_isInternal` | `DirectSum.IsInternal`: full direct-sum decomposition `H = έΛΧ_M H_M` (Tasaki eqs. (2.2.9)/(2.2.10)) | `Quantum/MagnetizationSubspaceCore.lean` |

### Two-site spin inner product (Tasaki ┬π2.2 eq. (2.2.16))

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬π2.2 eq. (2.2.16), p. 24.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `spinHalfDot x y` | `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y := ╬μ_{╬▒} onSite x ┼ε^(╬▒) ┬╖ onSite y ┼ε^(╬▒)` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_eq_plus_minus` | `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y = (1/2)(┼ε_x^+ ┼ε_y^- + ┼ε_x^- ┼ε_y^+) + ┼ε_x^(3) ┼ε_y^(3)` (Tasaki (2.2.16)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_comm` | `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y = ┼ε_y ┬╖ ┼ε_x` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_self` | `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_x = (3/4) ┬╖ 1` (the S(S+1) = 3/4 identity for S = 1/2) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_isHermitian` | `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_eq_sum_dot` | `(┼ε_tot)┬▓ = ╬μ_{x,y} ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfPairSpinSq` / `spinHalfPairSpinSq_eq` | `(┼ε_x + ┼ε_y)┬▓ = 2┬╖(┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y) + ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_x + ┼ε_y ┬╖ ┼ε_y` (Tasaki (2.2.18)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y, ┼ε_tot^(╬▒)] = 0` for ╬▒ έΙΙ {1, 2, 3} (SU(2) invariance, Tasaki (2.2.17)) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_commutator_totalSpinHalfOpPlus/Minus` | `[┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y, ┼ε^┬▒_tot] = 0` (ladder-operator version of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_parallel` | `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y |╧Δέθσ = (1/4) |╧Δέθσ` when `╧Δ x = ╧Δ y` and `x έΚι y` (Tasaki (2.2.19) parallel case) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_both_up/down` | `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y |έΗΣέΗΣέθσ = (1/4) |έΗΣέΗΣέθσ`, `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y |έΗΥέΗΥέθσ = (1/4) |έΗΥέΗΥέθσ` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = ┬▒1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `basisSwap` / `basisSwap_involutive` / `basisSwap_antiparallel` | site-swap of `╧Δ` at `x, y`, involutive and preserves anti-parallelism | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_basisVec_antiparallel` | `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y |╧Δέθσ = (1/2) |swap ╧Δέθσ - (1/4) |╧Δέθσ` when `╧Δ x έΚι ╧Δ y` (anti-parallel case) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_singlet` | singlet eigenvalue `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y (|╧Δέθσ - |swap ╧Δέθσ) = -(3/4) (|╧Δέθσ - |swap ╧Δέθσ)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) singlet `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfDot_mulVec_triplet_anti` | triplet `m = 0` eigenvalue `┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y (|╧Δέθσ + |swap ╧Δέθσ) = (1/4) (|╧Δέθσ + |swap ╧Δέθσ)` (Tasaki (2.2.19) triplet `m = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian` | `H = ╬μ_{x,y} J(x,y) ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y` (general Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` | `[H, ┼ε_tot^(╬▒)] = 0` for all axes (Tasaki (2.2.13) SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{Plus,Minus}` | `[H, ┼ε^┬▒_tot] = 0` (ladder form of SU(2) invariance) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | `Commute H ┼εtot┬▓` έΑΦ the Casimir operator-level form of SU(2) invariance (consequence of `[H, ┼εtot^╬▒] = 0` for each ╬▒, via `Commute.mul_right` and `.add_right`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_preserves_totalSpinHalfSquared_eigenvalue` | if `┼εtot┬▓ ┬╖ v = S ┬╖ v` then `┼εtot┬▓ ┬╖ (H ┬╖ v) = S ┬╖ (H ┬╖ v)` έΑΦ operator-level simultaneous diagonalisation of `H` and the SU(2) Casimir | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `spinHalfOpPlus_mul_pauliZ` / `pauliZ_mul_spinHalfOpPlus` | `╧Δ^+ ┬╖ ╧Δ^z = -╧Δ^+` and `╧Δ^z ┬╖ ╧Δ^+ = ╧Δ^+` έΑΦ the (anti)commutation at the single-site Pauli algebra level, used for the Jordan-Wigner cross-site CAR | `Quantum/SpinHalfBasis.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `┼ε_tot┬▓ |s s έΑο sέθσ = (N(N+2)/4) |s s έΑο sέθσ` for any constant `s : Fin 2` (Casimir eigenvalue at maximum total spin `S = N/2`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specializations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) and `s = 1` (all-down) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOp{Minus,Plus}_pow_basisVec_const` | for any `s : Fin 2` and `k : έΕΧ`, `┼εtot┬▓ ┬╖ (┼εtot^έΙΥ)^k ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ = (|╬δ|┬╖(|╬δ|+2)/4) ┬╖ (┼εtot^έΙΥ)^k ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ` έΑΦ the iterated ladder iterates remain in the maximum-total-spin SU(2) representation `S = Smax = |╬δ|/2` (Casimir invariance, Tasaki ┬π2.4) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_const` | `H |sέΑοsέθσ = (╬μ_{x,y} J(x,y)┬╖(if x=y then 3/4 else 1/4)) ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ` έΑΦ every Heisenberg-type Hamiltonian acts on a uniformly-aligned basis state as a scalar; bilinear-sum lift of Tasaki ┬π2.4 eq. (2.4.5), p. 32 (`-┼ε_x┬╖┼ε_y |╬οέΗΣέθσ = -S┬▓ |╬οέΗΣέθσ` for `S = 1/2`, `x έΚι y`), with the diagonal `S(S+1) = 3/4` contribution recorded explicitly | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_basisVec_all_up/down` | specialisations of the above to `s = 0` (all-up) / `s = 1` (all-down) έΑΦ the eigenvector property of the fully-polarised states; ground-state status (Tasaki's `E_GS = -|B|┬╖S┬▓`) requires extra ferromagnetic structure on `J` and is not asserted here | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_basisVec_const` | `H ┬╖ (┼ε_tot^+ ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ) = c_J ┬╖ (┼ε_tot^+ ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ)` έΑΦ `┼ε_tot^+` preserves the H-eigenvalue on a constant-spin basis state (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ┬π2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_basisVec_const` | same with `┼ε_tot^-` έΑΦ the canonical lowering ladder Tasaki uses to enumerate the ferromagnetic ground states `|╬ο_Mέθσ` (eq. (2.4.9), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpMinus_pow_basisVec_const` | iterated form: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : έΕΧ`, `H ┬╖ ((┼ε_tot^-)^k ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ) = c_J ┬╖ ((┼ε_tot^-)^k ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ)`; specialised at `s = 0` this gives the unnormalised Tasaki ┬π2.4 (2.4.9), p. 33 έΑΦ every iterate `(┼ε_tot^-)^k ┬╖ |╬οέΗΣέθσ` lies in the same H-eigenspace as `|╬οέΗΣέθσ` | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfOpPlus_pow_basisVec_const` | companion iterated form for `┼ε_tot^+`: for any constant `s : Fin 2` and any `k : έΕΧ`, `H ┬╖ ((┼ε_tot^+)^k ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ) = c_J ┬╖ ((┼ε_tot^+)^k ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ)` (corollary of SU(2) invariance, Tasaki ┬π2.4 (2.4.7), iterated) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_commute_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}` | for any `J` and `╬╕ : έΕζ`, `H` commutes with the global rotation `├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕ = exp(-i╬╕ ┼ε_tot^╬▒)` (composes `heisenbergHamiltonian_commutator_totalSpinHalfOp{╬▒}` with `totalSpinHalfRot{╬▒}_commute_of_commute`; the operator-level form of Tasaki ┬π2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot{1,2,3}_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `╬╕`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ┬╖ (├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ) = c_J ┬╖ (├δ^(╬▒)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ)` έΑΦ the rotated (single-axis) constant-spin state shares the H-eigenvalue (Tasaki ┬π2.4 (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_mulVec_totalSpinHalfRot32_basisVec_const` | for any `J`, `╬╕`, `╧Χ`, and constant `s : Fin 2`, `H ┬╖ (├δ^(3)_╧Χ ┬╖ ├δ^(2)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ) = c_J ┬╖ (├δ^(3)_╧Χ ┬╖ ├δ^(2)_╬╕ ┬╖ |sέΑοsέθσ)` έΑΦ the two-step spin-coherent state of Tasaki eq. (2.4.6) (`|╬η_╬╕,╧Χέθσ` for `s = 0`) is an H-eigenvector with the same eigenvalue as the constant configuration (Tasaki eq. (2.4.7), p. 33) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | `┼ε_tot┬▓ (|έΗΣέΗΥέθσ - |έΗΥέΗΣέθσ) = 0` for `╬δ = Fin 2` (singlet, `S = 0`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfSquared_mulVec_two_site_triplet_zero` | `┼ε_tot┬▓ (|έΗΣέΗΥέθσ + |έΗΥέΗΣέθσ) = 2(|έΗΣέΗΥέθσ + |έΗΥέΗΣέθσ)` for `╬δ = Fin 2` (triplet `m = 0`, `S = 1`) | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `totalSpinHalfOp3_mulVec_two_site_singlet` | the two-site singlet has zero `┼ε_tot^(3)` magnetization | `Quantum/SpinDot.lean` |
| `onSite_commutator_totalOnSite` | `[onSite x S╬▒, ╬μ_z onSite z S╬▓] = onSite x [S╬▒, S╬▓]` | `Quantum/TotalSpin.lean` |

### One-dimensional open-chain quantum Ising

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬π3.3 eq. (3.3.1), p. 55 (transverse-field Ising on an open
chain). Our formalization uses the Pauli convention `╧Δ = 2┬╖S` and an
explicit bond coupling `J`.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h` | `H = -J ╬μ ╧Δ^z_i ╧Δ^z_{i+1} - h ╬μ ╧Δ^x_i` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_isHermitian` | `H` is Hermitian for real `J`, `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot x y` | the Ising bond operator `╧Δ^z_x ┬╖ ╧Δ^z_y` (generic in `╬δ`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `spinZDot_isHermitian` | each bond operator is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric (J : ╬δ έΗΤ ╬δ έΗΤ έΕΓ) (h : έΕΓ)` | **graph-friendly** Ising Hamiltonian `╬μ J(x,y) ╧Δ^z_x ╧Δ^z_y έΙΤ h ╬μ ╧Δ^x_x` for any finite `╬δ` and pairwise coupling `J`; specialises to chain / graph / lattice via the choice of `J` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianGeneric_isHermitian` | Hermitian for entry-wise real `J` and real `h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph G J h` | graph wrapper: `isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf G J) h`; double-sum convention matches Heisenberg-on-graph | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` | Hermitian for real `J, h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph G ╬▓ J h` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `isingGibbsStateOnGraph_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity (real `J, h`) and commute with the Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.sum_pathGraph_forward` / `sum_pathGraph_backward` / `sum_pathGraph_adj` | sum-decomposition helpers for `pathGraph (N+1)` adjacency: `╬μ_{x,y}` over ordered adjacent pairs = `╬μ_{i:Fin N} (f i.cs i.s + f i.s i.cs)` | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `pathGraphParityColoring` / `pathGraph_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of `pathGraph (N + 1)` (`i έΗο i.val % 2`) and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Underpins the N├σel state (`Quantum/NeelState.lean`) and the Marshall-Lieb-Mattis theorem (Tasaki ┬π2.5) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `cycleGraphEvenParityColoring` / `cycleGraph_even_isBipartite` | parity-based 2-colouring of the even cycle `cycleGraph (2 * K + 2)` and the corresponding `IsBipartite` proof. Wrap-around case `(2K+1) + 1 έΚκ 0` still flips parity because the cycle length is even (odd cycles are not bipartite) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `quantumIsingHamiltonian_eq_isingHamiltonianGeneric` | **generic-N bridge**: `quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h = isingHamiltonianGeneric (couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J/2)) h`. The proof itself is the robust regression test | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Testing infrastructure

| Lean name / location | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem/Tests/Sanity.lean` | sanity-check `example` blocks for concrete small-N instances (Pauli arithmetic, spin-1/2 ladder actions, Heisenberg / Ising Hermiticity on small chains, graph-centric bridge identities) |
| decide-based property tests (in `Tests/Sanity.lean`) | universally-quantified properties verified by `decide` on small finite types (graph adjacency symmetry / irreflexivity / connectivity on `pathGraph n` and `cycleGraph n` for small `n`); real proofs, no `sorry` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState ╬▓ J h N` | `gibbsState ╬▓ (quantumIsingHamiltonian N J h)` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_isHermitian` | the Ising-chain Gibbs state `╧Β_╬▓` is Hermitian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[╧Β_╬▓, H_Ising] = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_zero` | infinite-temperature closed form `έθρAέθσ_0 = (1/dim) ┬╖ Tr A` (independent of `J, h`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `O`, `(έθρOέθσ_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | conservation `έθρ[H_Ising, A]έθσ_╬▓ = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(έθρH_Isingέθσ_╬▓).im = 0` (energy expectation is real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_im` | for Hermitian `O`, `(έθρH_Ising ┬╖ Oέθσ_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_sq_im` | `(έθρH_Ising^2έθσ_╬▓).im = 0` (energy-squared expectation real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_pow_im` | `(έθρH_Ising^nέθσ_╬▓).im = 0` for any `n : έΕΧ` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_anticommutator_im` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(έθρA┬╖B + B┬╖Aέθσ_╬▓).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_commutator_re` | for Hermitian `A, B`, `(έθρA┬╖B έΙΤ B┬╖Aέθσ_╬▓).re = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsHamiltonianVariance_im` | `(Var_╬▓(H_Ising)).im = 0` (energy variance real) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsing_partitionFn_im` | `(partitionFn ╬▓ H_Ising).im = 0` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq` | for Hermitian `O`, `((έθρOέθσ_╬▓).re : έΕΓ) = έθρOέθσ_╬▓` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(╧Β_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for the Ising Hamiltonian | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation_self_eq` | `έθρH_Isingέθσ_╬▓ = -J ┬╖ έΙΣ έθρ╧Δ^z ╧Δ^zέθσ_╬▓ + (-h) ┬╖ έΙΣ έθρ╧Δ^xέθσ_╬▓` (energy as bond + transverse-field decomposition) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |

### Gibbs state (Tasaki ┬π3.3)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬π3.3.

All theorems in this module are fully proved with **zero `sorry`**.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `gibbsExp ╬▓ H` | `exp(-╬▓H) := Matrix.exp (-╬▓ έΑλ H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isHermitian` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is Hermitian (when `H` is Hermitian) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_zero` | `exp(-0┬╖H) = 1` (Tasaki ┬π3.3, pp. 75έΑΥ78) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add` | `exp(-(╬▓έΓΒ+╬▓έΓΓ)H) = exp(-╬▓έΓΒH) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓έΓΓH)` (one-parameter group) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_add_of_commute_hamiltonians` | `exp(-╬▓(HέΓΒ+HέΓΓ)) = exp(-╬▓HέΓΒ) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓HέΓΓ)` for commuting `HέΓΒ, HέΓΓ` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_neg_mul_self` | `exp(╬▓H) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_self_mul_neg` | `exp(-╬▓H) ┬╖ exp(╬▓H) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_isUnit` | `exp(-╬▓H)` is invertible | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_ne_zero` | `exp(-╬▓H) έΚι 0` (corollary of `gibbsExp_isUnit`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_ne_zero` | `╧Β_╬▓ έΚι 0` when `Z(╬▓) έΚι 0` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_inv` | `(╧Β_╬▓)έΒ╗┬╣ = Z(╬▓) ┬╖ e^{╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) έΚι 0` (general matrix inverse, generalises `gibbsState_zero_inv`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_smul_gibbsState_eq_gibbsExp` | `Z(╬▓) ┬╖ ╧Β_╬▓ = e^{-╬▓H}` when `Z(╬▓) έΚι 0` (canonical rescaled identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_mul_gibbsExpectation_eq` | `Z(╬▓) ┬╖ έθρAέθσ_╬▓ = Tr(e^{-╬▓H} ┬╖ A)` when `Z(╬▓) έΚι 0` (canonical unnormalised expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_natCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬╖╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : έΕΧ` (exact discrete semigroup identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_two_mul` | `exp(-(2╬▓)H) = exp(-╬▓H) ┬╖ exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_inv` | `(exp(-╬▓H))έΒ╗┬╣ = exp(╬▓H)` (matrix inverse made explicit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_intCast_mul` | `exp(-(n┬╖╬▓)H) = (exp(-╬▓H))^n` for `n : έΕν` (integer-power extension) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn ╬▓ H` | `Z := Matrix.trace (exp(-╬▓H))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero` | `Z(0) = Fintype.card (╬δ έΗΤ Fin 2)` (dimension of the Hilbert space) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `partitionFn_zero_ne_zero` | `Z(0) έΚι 0` (concrete sorry-free proof that the partition function is nonzero at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.IsHermitian.trace_im` | for any Hermitian `A : Matrix n n έΕΓ`, `A.trace.im = 0` (generic helper; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `partitionFn_im_of_isHermitian` | for Hermitian `H`, `(partitionFn ╬▓ H).im = 0` (Z is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_mul_self_trace` | `Tr(╧Β_╬▓┬▓) = Z(2╬▓) / Z(╬▓)┬▓` (purity / R├σnyi-2 entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_pow_trace` | `Tr(╧Β_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` for any `n : έΕΧ` (R├σnyi-n entropy precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero` | `╧Β_0 = (1/dim) ┬╖ I` (maximally mixed state at infinite temperature) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_zero_inv` | `╧Β_0έΒ╗┬╣ = dim ┬╖ I` (matrix inverse at ╬▓ = 0) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_zero` | `έθρAέθσ_0 = (1/dim) ┬╖ Tr A` (high-temperature limit) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState ╬▓ H` | `╧Β := (1/Z) έΑλ exp(-╬▓H)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_trace` | `Tr(╧Β) = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_isHermitian` | `╧Β` is Hermitian | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation ╬▓ H O` | `έθρOέθσ := Matrix.trace (╧Β * O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_one` | `έθρ1έθσ = 1` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_add` | `έθρOέΓΒ + OέΓΓέθσ = έθρOέΓΒέθσ + έθρOέΓΓέθσ` (linearity in observable) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_smul` | `έθρc ┬╖ Oέθσ = c ┬╖ έθρOέθσ` (scalar linearity, `c : έΕΓ`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_neg` | `έθρ-Oέθσ = -έθρOέθσ` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sub` | `έθρA - Bέθσ = έθρAέθσ - έθρBέθσ` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sum` | `έθρέΙΣ i έΙΙ s, f iέθσ = έΙΣ i έΙΙ s, έθρf iέθσ` (finite-sum linearity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExp_commute_hamiltonian` | `[exp(-╬▓H), H] = 0` (Tasaki ┬π3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | `[╧Β_╬▓, H] = 0`, i.e. `╧Β_╬▓` is stationary under the dynamics generated by `H` (Tasaki ┬π3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_star_of_isHermitian` | `star (Tr(A ┬╖ B)) = Tr(A ┬╖ B)` for Hermitian `A, B : Matrix n n έΕΓ` (algebraic core, Gibbs-independent; relocated to the matrix-analysis layer in PR #4344) | `Math/MatrixAnalysis/HermitianTrace.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_star_of_isHermitian` | `star έθρOέθσ_╬▓ = έθρOέθσ_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_im_of_isHermitian` | `(έθρOέθσ_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H`, `O` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_ofReal_re_eq_of_isHermitian` | `((έθρOέθσ_╬▓).re : έΕΓ) = έθρOέθσ_╬▓` for Hermitian `H`, `O` (real-cast equality) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_hamiltonian_comm` | `έθρH ┬╖ Aέθσ_╬▓ = έθρA ┬╖ Hέθσ_╬▓` for any `A` (Tasaki ┬π3.3, p. 80) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_mul_comm_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A` (`[A, H] = 0`), `έθρA ┬╖ Oέθσ_╬▓ = έθρO ┬╖ Aέθσ_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_eq_zero_of_commute_hamiltonian` | for any conserved `A`, `έθρA ┬╖ O έΙΤ O ┬╖ Aέθσ_╬▓ = 0` (selection rule) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_commutator_hamiltonian` | `έθρ[H, A]έθσ_╬▓ = 0` (conservation law) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_hamiltonian_im` | `(έθρHέθσ_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H` (real energy expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_sq_im_of_isHermitian` | `(έθρO ┬╖ Oέθσ_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (second-moment realness, variance precursor) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsExpectation_pow_im_of_isHermitian` | `(έθρO^nέθσ_╬▓).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O`, any `n : έΕΧ` (all natural-power moments real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance ╬▓ H O` | `Var_╬▓(O) := έθρO ┬╖ Oέθσ_╬▓ έΙΤ έθρOέθσ_╬▓┬▓` (canonical-ensemble variance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsVariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Var_╬▓(O)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, O` (variance is real) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_zero` | at ╬▓ = 0, `Var_0(O) = (1/dim) ┬╖ Tr(O┬▓) έΙΤ ((1/dim) ┬╖ Tr O)┬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_eq_centered_sq` | `Var_╬▓(O) = έθρ(O έΙΤ έθρOέθσ_╬▓ ┬╖ 1) ┬╖ (O έΙΤ έθρOέθσ_╬▓ ┬╖ 1)έθσ_╬▓` (centered-square form, `Z έΚι 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) := έθρA ┬╖ Bέθσ_╬▓ έΙΤ έθρAέθσ_╬▓ ┬╖ έθρBέθσ_╬▓` (canonical-ensemble complex covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq` | unfolding lemma for `gibbsCovariance` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_self_eq_variance` | `Cov_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_sub_swap_eq_commutator` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) έΙΤ Cov_╬▓(B, A) = έθρA ┬╖ B έΙΤ B ┬╖ Aέθσ_╬▓` (antisymmetric part = commutator expectation) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_left` | `Cov_╬▓(AέΓΒ + AέΓΓ, B) = Cov_╬▓(AέΓΒ, B) + Cov_╬▓(AέΓΓ, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_add_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, BέΓΒ + BέΓΓ) = Cov_╬▓(A, BέΓΒ) + Cov_╬▓(A, BέΓΓ)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_left` | `Cov_╬▓(c έΑλ A, B) = c ┬╖ Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_smul_right` | `Cov_╬▓(A, c έΑλ B) = c ┬╖ Cov_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov_╬▓(c έΑλ 1, B) = 0` and `Cov_╬▓(A, c έΑλ 1) = 0` (when `Z έΚι 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_const_smul_one_left/right_eq_zero` | `Cov^s_╬▓(c έΑλ 1, B) = 0` and `Cov^s_╬▓(A, c έΑλ 1) = 0` (when `Z έΚι 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm ╬▓ H A B` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) := (1/2) ┬╖ έθρA ┬╖ B + B ┬╖ Aέθσ_╬▓ έΙΤ έθρAέθσ_╬▓ ┬╖ έθρBέθσ_╬▓` (symmetric / real-valued covariance) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_self_eq_variance` | `Cov^s_╬▓(O, O) = Var_╬▓(O)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_im_of_isHermitian` | `(Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)).im = 0` for Hermitian `H, A, B` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_comm` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(B, A)` (symmetric in observables) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_add_left/right` | additivity of `Cov^s_╬▓` in each argument | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_smul_left/right` | scalar pull-out from each argument of `Cov^s_╬▓` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add` | `Var_╬▓(A + B) = Var_╬▓(A) + Var_╬▓(B) + 2 ┬╖ Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)` (sum-of-observables variance identity) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_one` | `Var_╬▓(1) = 0` (when `Z έΚι 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul` | `Var_╬▓(c έΑλ A) = c┬▓ ┬╖ Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(c έΑλ 1) = 0` (when `Z έΚι 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_neg` | `Var_╬▓(έΙΤA) = Var_╬▓(A)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsVariance_add_const_smul_one` | `Var_╬▓(A + c έΑλ 1) = Var_╬▓(A)` (when `Z έΚι 0`) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_add_half_commutator` | `Cov_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) + (1/2) ┬╖ έθρA ┬╖ B έΙΤ B ┬╖ Aέθσ_╬▓` (symmetric / antisymmetric decomposition) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovarianceSymm_eq_half_add_swap` | `Cov^s_╬▓(A, B) = (1/2) ┬╖ (Cov_╬▓(A, B) + Cov_╬▓(B, A))` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `gibbsCovariance_eq_symm_of_commute` | for commuting `A, B`, `Cov_╬▓(A, B) = Cov^s_╬▓(A, B)` | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |
| `Matrix.trace_mul_conjTranspose_swap_of_isHermitian` | `star Tr(╧Β ┬╖ X) = Tr(╧Β ┬╖ Xά┤┤)` for Hermitian `╧Β` (generic helper) | `Quantum/GibbsState.lean` |

### Heisenberg chain (Tasaki ┬π3.5)

Primary reference: Tasaki, *Physics and Mathematics of Quantum Many-Body
Systems*, ┬π3.5, p. 89.

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf G J` | the canonical pairwise coupling `╬δ έΗΤ ╬δ έΗΤ έΕΓ` of a `SimpleGraph G` with uniform edge weight `J`: returns `J` on edges of `G`, zero otherwise (graph-centric bridge) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.couplingOf_symm` / `_self` / `_real` | symmetry (from `G.Adj` symmetry), vanishing on the diagonal (from irreflexivity), and reality (for real edge weight) | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `LatticeSystem.Lattice.pathGraph_adj_iff` / `cycleGraph_adj_iff` | path / cycle graph adjacency in the explicit `x.val + 1 = y.val έΙρ ...` form used elsewhere in the codebase | `Lattice/Graph.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+1) έΗΤ Fin (N+1) έΗΤ έΕΓ`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds, zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling N J` | coupling `Fin (N+2) έΗΤ Fin (N+2) έΗΤ έΕΓ`: returns `-J` on nearest-neighbour bonds (mod N+2), zero otherwise | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the open-chain coupling is `couplingOf (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `periodicChainCoupling_eq_couplingOf` | the periodic-chain coupling is `couplingOf (cycleGraph (N+2)) (-J)` | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_isHermitian_of_real_symm` | for any real symmetric coupling `J` the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `H = ╬μ_{x,y} J(x,y) ┼ε_x ┬╖ ┼ε_y` is Hermitian | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonian_couplingOf_isHermitian` | **graph-centric** Hermiticity: for any `SimpleGraph G` and real edge weight `J : έΕΓ`, the Heisenberg Hamiltonian `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` is Hermitian. The chain instances are corollaries via the bridge theorems | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph G J` | named wrapper = `heisenbergHamiltonian (couplingOf G J)` (parallel to `isingHamiltonianOnGraph`) | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `heisenbergHamiltonianOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfOp{1,2,3}` / `_commute_totalSpinHalfSquared` | corollaries re-exposed under the named wrapper | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `openChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` / `periodicChainHeisenbergGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridges: chain Gibbs = graph Gibbs on pathGraph/cycleGraph | `Quantum/HeisenbergChain.lean` |
| `quantumIsingGibbsState_eq_isingGibbsStateOnGraph` | chain Ising Gibbs = `isingGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) ╬▓ (-J/2) h` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsState_commute_hamiltonian` | the periodic Ising Gibbs state commutes with the periodic Ising Hamiltonian (free corollary of `gibbsState_commute_hamiltonian`) | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `isingCycleGibbsExpectation_zero` / `_im_of_isHermitian` / `_commutator_hamiltonian` / `_hamiltonian_im` / `_hamiltonian_pow_im` / `isingCycle_partitionFn_im` / `_ofReal_re_eq` / `isingCycleGibbsState_pow_trace` | periodic-Ising expectation companions of the open-chain `quantumIsingGibbsExpectation*` family: ╬▓ = 0 closed form, real-valuedness for Hermitian observables, conservation `έθρ[H, A]έθσ = 0`, energy / energy-power expectations real, partition-function real, real-cast `((έθρOέθσ_╬▓).re : έΕΓ) = έθρOέθσ_╬▓`, R├σnyi-n trace `Tr(╧Β_╬▓^n) = Z(n╬▓) / Z(╬▓)^n` | `Quantum/IsingChain.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph N ╬▓ G J U` | Gibbs state of the graph-built Hubbard Hamiltonian | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph_isHermitian` / `_commute_hamiltonian` | Hermiticity / commute corollaries | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `hubbardChainGibbsState_eq_onGraph` | rfl bridge: `hubbardChainGibbsState = hubbardGibbsStateOnGraph (pathGraph (N+1)) (-J) U` | `Fermion/JordanWigner.lean` |
| `jwStringAbstract i` | Jordan-Wigner string for any `[Fintype ╬δ] [LinearOrder ╬δ]` έΑΦ product of `╧Δ^z_j` over `j < i`; generalises the Fin-specific `jwString` | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `jwStringAbstract_isHermitian` / `jwStringAbstract_sq` / `jwStringAbstract_commute_onSite` | basic structural identities | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract i` / `fermionCreationAbstract i` / `fermionNumberAbstract i` | abstract-╬δ annihilation / creation / number; rfl-bridges to the Fin-specific versions | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |
| `fermionAnnihilationAbstract_conjTranspose` / `fermionCreationAbstract_conjTranspose` / `fermionNumberAbstract_isHermitian` | adjoint relations and Hermiticity in the abstract form | `Fermion/JWAbstract.lean` |

### Perron-Frobenius theorem (`Math/PerronFrobenius.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean`, `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean`, `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean`)

Perron-Frobenius theorem for nonneg irreducible/primitive matrices (Issue #405, closed).
The sorry in `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` is eliminated via the Collatz-Wielandt port (PRs AέΑΥC).

| Lean name | Statement | File |
|---|---|---|
| `Matrix.IsPrimitive.of_irreducible_pos_diagonal` | irreducible nonneg + positive diagonal έΗΤ primitive (Seneta ┬π1.1, Prop. 1.3, p. 17) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusPrimitive.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.collatzWielandtFn` | CW function `min_{i\|x_i>0} (Ax)_i/x_i` (Seneta ┬π1.2, p. 27) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_any_ratio` | `CW(x) έΚν (Ax)_i/x_i` for `x_i > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.le_mulVec` | fundamental inequality `CW(x)┬╖x έΚν Ax` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.smul_eq` | scale invariance `CW(cx) = CW(x)` for `c > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.upperSemicontinuousOn` | CW is upper-semicontinuous on stdSimplex | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.exists_maximizer` | CW attains its max on stdSimplex (EVT for USC, Seneta ┬π1.2, p. 28) | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.eq_eigenvalue` | `CW(v) = r` when `Av = r┬╖v`, `v > 0` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `CollatzWielandt.lt_of_all_ratios_gt` | all ratios `> c` έθ╣ `CW(x) > c` | `Math/CollatzWielandt.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.pos_of_nonneg_eigenvec` | irreducible nonneg + `Av = ╬╝v`, `v έΚξ 0`, `v έΚι 0` έθ╣ `v > 0` (standard propagation argument) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_primitive` | primitive nonneg έθ╣ έΙΔ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬π1.2, pp. 27έΑΥ28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `PerronFrobeniusMain.exists_positive_eigenvector_of_irreducible` | irreducible nonneg έθ╣ έΙΔ `r > 0`, `v > 0` with `Av = rv` (Seneta ┬π1.2, pp. 27έΑΥ28) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusMain.lean` |
| `exists_pos_eigenvec_max` | (**sorry-free**) irreducible nonneg Hermitian έθ╣ max eigenvalue has strictly positive eigenvector | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `pos_eigenvec_unique` | strictly positive eigenvector unique up to positive scalar | `Math/PerronFrobenius.lean` |
| `PerronFrobenius.eigenvec_proportional_of_pos_eigenvec` | **geometric simplicity of the Perron eigenvalue**: irreducible nonneg with a strictly positive eigenvector `v` at `╬╝` έθ╣ every real eigenvector at `╬╝` is `s έΑλ v` for some scalar `s` (the `╬╝`-eigenspace is 1-dimensional). Perturbation trick: `v + t έΑλ u > 0` for small `t > 0` is an eigenvector at `╬╝`, hence `έΙζ v` by `pos_eigenvec_unique`, forcing `u έΙζ v`. Used to show a Perron ground state is a joint eigenvector of any commuting operator (e.g. the total Casimir) | `Math/PerronFrobeniusSimple.lean` |

References: E. Seneta, *Non-negative Matrices and Markov Chains* (3rd ed.), Springer 2006, ┬π1.2 (pp. 27έΑΥ28);
or4nge19/MCMC: `MCMC/PF/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/PerronFrobenius/`.

### Spin-`S` MarshallέΑΥLiebέΑΥMattis on the magnetization sector (Tasaki ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.2 generic S, sector form)

> **Route correction (2026-05-26).** The earlier "saturated-ladder-iterate"
> route toward `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` was found **unsound**: it reduced the
> theorem to a leaf hypothesis (lowerable attach-sum dominance) that is **false**
> έΑΦ the ferromagnetic ladder iterate `(┼εέΒ╗_tot)^M|all-upέθσ` is *not* the
> Marshall-positive antiferromagnetic ground state (concrete counterexample:
> spin-1/2, `V={a,b}`, `A={a}`, `B={b}`, `M=0έΗΤ1`). The 40 conditional-wrapper
> modules of that route were removed; their detailed rows below were deleted with
> them. The **sound** route keeps the per-sector PerronέΑΥFrobenius ground state
> (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`, sorry-free) and chains sectors by
> SU(2) invariance `[─ν, ┼εέΒ╗_tot] = 0`; some rows below still reference helper
> lemmas from the removed route pending re-documentation. See
> `.self-local/docs/tasaki-2-5-pf-route-design.md` and Issue #3542.

Generic-spin (`N = 2S`) version of Tasaki ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.2 applied to the
**magnetization-`M` sector** of the un-dressed antiferromagnetic
Heisenberg Hamiltonian on a bipartite graph. The sector subtype
`magConfigS V N M := { ╧Δ : V έΗΤ Fin (N + 1) // magSumS ╧Δ = M }` is the
natural index type since the dressed Heisenberg matrix is irreducible
on each sector. The core sector-matrix API lives in
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSector.lean`; the trailing
eigenvalue-uniqueness + complex-form MLM Theorem 2.2 statements
were moved to the companion
`Quantum/SpinS/DressedMatrixOnMagSectorEigenvalueUnique.lean` in
refactor #30 (PR #2829). The two bundled sector-level MLM Theorem 2.2
ground-state results (`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_groundState`,
`marshallLiebMattis_spinS_heisenbergSector_complexGroundState_full`) were
subsequently relocated to
`Quantum/SpinS/MarshallLiebMattisSectorBundled.lean` (#4569, re-proved via the
canonical `*Structural*` twins). Tracked in Issue #412.

| Lean name | Statement |
|---|---|
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (lowering)**: if a magnetization-`M` sector vector `magSectorEmbedding ╬ο` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at energy `╬╝` and a total-Casimir eigenvector at `╬│` away from the sector's lowering-kernel value `(card┬╖N/2έΙΤM)(card┬╖N/2έΙΤMέΙΤ1)`, then `┼εέΒ╗_tot ┬╖ magSectorEmbedding ╬ο` is a Heisenberg eigenvector at the **same** `╬╝` (via `[─ν, ┼εέΒ╗_tot]=0`), is non-zero (total-Casimir non-vanishing criterion), and lies in the next magnetization sector. This is the energy-preserving step that chains the per-sector PerronέΑΥFrobenius ground states (`tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_sector_existence`) across the admissible range; it uses **no** Marshall positivity of the lowered vector (the false hypothesis of the removed ferromagnetic-ladder route). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred` / `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_pred_of_mem_predictedGS` | **Sound PF adjacent-sector ladder link (raising)** and its predicted-GS specialisation: the raising companions of `tasaki23_pf_ladder_link_succ(_of_mem_predictedGS)`. `┼εέΒ║_tot` maps a sector-`K` Heisenberg eigenvector (total-Casimir away from the raising-kernel value `(card┬╖N/2έΙΤK)(card┬╖N/2έΙΤK+1)`) to a non-zero same-energy eigenvector in the previous sector; predicted-GS membership in sector `M+1` strictly above the left endpoint discharges the Casimir hypotheses via `tasaki23_predictedCasimirValue_ne_raising_kernel_value_of_mem_of_left_lt`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23PFLadderLink.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagSubspaceS` / `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS` (with `mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_iff`, `basisVecS_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`, `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_disjoint`) | **Sublattice magnetization grading**: the `┼ε_A^(3)`-eigenspaces `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` and the diagonal eigenvalue `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧Δ = έΙΣ_{xέΙΙA}(N/2έΙΤ╧Δ_x)`. Submodule API (zero/add/smul/neg/sub/finite-sum membership), disjointness of distinct eigenvalues, and basis-state membership. Sublattice analogue of `magSubspaceS`/`magEigenvalueS`; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 5) for the sublattice highest-weight argument bounding `(┼ε_A)┬▓`. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagnetization.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_sublattice_casimir_re_le` | **Sublattice Casimir bounds for the toy ground state**: the per-sector Marshall-positive toy GS is a joint Casimir eigenvector (#3657) whose `(┼ε_A)┬▓`/`(┼ε_┬υA)┬▓` eigenvalues `╬│_A, ╬│_B` obey `╬│_A.re έΚν s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B.re έΚν s_B(s_B+1)` (apply the spectral max bound #3672 to the non-zero Marshall-dressed embedding). Discharges two hypotheses of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 3). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23StructuralPFJointCasimir.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_sector_magnetization_re_eq_predicted` | **Extremal sector realizes the predicted total spin**: for the left-endpoint sector `M = min(|A|,|┬υA|)┬╖N`, the magnetization eigenvalue `|V|┬╖N/2 έΙΤ M` has real part `= tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin A N = |s_A έΙΤ s_B|`. Supplies the extremal-sector hypothesis `hM` of the toy total-Casimir pin #3676 (Issue #3674, PR 4). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalSector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_groundState_casimir_eq_predicted_of_energy_le` | **Toy ground state is the predicted-Casimir witness (modulo the energy bound)**: in the extremal sector `M = min(|A|,|┬υA|)┬╖N`, the Marshall-positive toy GS with toy energy `╬╝ έΚν predicted έΙΤ s_A(s_A+1) έΙΤ s_B(s_B+1)` is a `(┼ε_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N`. Capstone assembling the joint eigenvector (#3657), sublattice bounds (#3677), toy energy formula (#3673), extremal sector (#3678), the two-sided pin (#3676), and Hermitian eigenvalue realness έΑΦ exactly the witness form required by #3656. The single remaining obligation is the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (Issue #3674, PR 5). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyWitness.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_toy_sector_groundEnergy_le_of_witness` | **Toy ground energy is minimal in its sector**: the Marshall-positive toy ground state (energy `╬╝`) has `╬╝ έΚν E` for every sector eigenvector `╧Η έΚι 0` of the toy Heisenberg sector matrix at real energy `E`. Specialises `heisenbergHamiltonianSReMatrixOnMagSector_eigenvalue_ge_of_marshallPositive` to `J = bipartiteCoupling A`; reduces the reference energy bound `h╬╝` (and hence the whole route, via #3679) to exhibiting one sector eigenvector at the predicted ground energy `predicted έΙΤ s_A(s_A+1) έΙΤ s_B(s_B+1)` (Issue #3674, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyGroundEnergyBound.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_total_casimir_re_eq_predicted_of_bounds` (with `tasaki23PredictedTotalSpin_nonneg`) | **Toy total-Casimir pin (extremal sector)**: in the extremal magnetization sector `M.re = |s_AέΙΤs_B|`, a non-zero `(┼ε_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot` with sublattice Casimir max bounds `╬│_A έΚν s_A(s_A+1)`, `╬│_B έΚν s_B(s_B+1)` and reference toy energy bound `(╬│_totέΙΤ╬│_AέΙΤ╬│_B).re έΚν predicted έΙΤ s_A(s_A+1) έΙΤ s_B(s_B+1)` has `╬│_tot.re = predicted`. A two-sided squeeze (magnetization lower bound #3675 + sublattice upper bounds #3672 + energy formula #3673), isolating the extremal-sector and reference-energy obligations as hypotheses; no ClebschέΑΥGordan (Issue #3674, PR 2). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ToyCasimirPin.lean`) |
| `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_ge_of_mem_magSubspaceS` (with `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector_ge`) | **Total-Casimir magnetization lower bound** `(┼ε_tot)┬▓ έΚξ m(m+1)`: a non-zero `(┼ε_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `magSubspaceS V N M` with `0 έΚν M.re` has `M.re┬╖(M.re+1) έΚν ╬│.re`. The total spin dominates its `z`-projection: raising with `┼εέΒ║_tot` only increases the magnetization, so the highest weight `M'` reached has `M' έΚξ M`, giving `╬│ = M'(M'+1) έΚξ M(M+1)`. Lower-bound companion of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; in the extremal sector `m = s_AέΙΤs_B` it pins the toy GS total Casimir to predicted, bypassing the ClebschέΑΥGordan triangle inequality (Issue #3674, PR 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/CasimirSpectralLowerBound.lean`) |
| `heisenbergToyHamiltonianS_mulVec_of_joint_casimir_eigenvector` | **Toy energy on a joint Casimir eigenvector**: a simultaneous eigenvector of `(┼ε_tot)┬▓`, `(┼ε_A)┬▓`, `(┼ε_┬υA)┬▓` at `(╬│_tot, ╬│_A, ╬│_B)` is a `─ν_toy`-eigenvector at `╬│_tot έΙΤ ╬│_A έΙΤ ╬│_B` (from `─ν_toy = (┼ε_tot)┬▓έΙΤ(┼ε_A)┬▓έΙΤ(┼ε_┬υA)┬▓`). The energy formula underlying the variational identification of the toy ground state's predicted total Casimir (Issue #3658, PR 4 step 1). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/ToyHamiltonianJointEnergy.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvalue_re_le_sA` | **Sublattice Casimir spectral max bound** `(┼ε_A)┬▓ έΚν s_A(s_A+1)`: every eigenvalue `╬│` of `(┼ε_A)┬▓` (with a non-zero eigenvector) has `╬│.re έΚν s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬╖N/2`. Assembles the sublattice weight-component extraction (#3670), highest-weight existence (#3671), and the highest-weight bound (#3668). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvalue_re_le_sMax`; the sublattice analytic ingredient of the Issue #3658 witness construction (Issue #3658, PR 12). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirSpectralBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeExists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_ne_sMax_add_one`) | **Sublattice highest-weight existence**: from a non-zero `(┼ε_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A (s_AέΙΤk)`, repeatedly raising with `┼ε_A^+` (which terminates once the sublattice magnetization exceeds `s_A`, since the subspace at `s_A+1` is `έΛξ`) produces a non-zero highest-weight `(┼ε_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at the same `╬│`. Sublattice analogue of `exists_highestWeight_eigenvector` (Issue #3658, PR 11). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeightExistence.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (with `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice weight-component extraction**: since `(┼ε_A)┬▓` commutes with `┼ε_A^(3)` it preserves each sublattice magnetization subspace and commutes with the projector; hence a non-zero `(┼ε_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` has a non-zero sublattice-weight component `sublatticeMagProjFn A (s_AέΙΤk) v` that is again a `(┼ε_A)┬▓`-eigenvector at `╬│` in a single sublattice magnetization subspace. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_eigenvec_exists_weight_component` (Issue #3658, PR 10). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagWeightComponent.lean`) |
| `sublatticeMagProjFn` / `sum_sublatticeMagProjFn_eq` (with `sublatticeMagSumS`, `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_eq_sub`, `sublatticeMagProjFn_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS`) | **Sublattice magnetization projection & decomposition**: the pointwise projector `sublatticeMagProjFn A M v` (keeps `v ╧Δ` where `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧Δ = M`) lands in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M`, is linear, and the projectors over the finite spectrum `s_A έΙΤ k` (`k=0..|A|┬╖N`) sum to the identity (`sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧Δ = s_A έΙΤ sublatticeMagSumS A ╧Δ`, `sublatticeMagSumS A ╧Δ = έΙΣ_{xέΙΙA}╧Δ_x έΚν |A|┬╖N`). Sublattice analogue of `magProjFn`/`sum_magProjFn_eq` (Issue #3658, PR 9). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagProjection.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (with `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS_im_zero`) | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir bound**: a non-zero highest-weight `(┼ε_A)┬▓`-eigenvector (`┼ε_A^+ w = 0`) at `╬│` in `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M` satisfies `╬│.re έΚν s_A(s_A+1)`, `s_A = |A|┬╖N/2`. Its Casimir value is `M(M+1)` (#3663), `M` is an attained basis eigenvalue (#3667 έΛξ-lemma), so `|M| έΚν s_A` (#3660). Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSSquared_highestWeight_eigenvalue_re_le` (Issue #3658, PR 8). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeCasimirHighestWeightBound.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_diag` / `sublatticeSpinSOp3_apply_off_diag` / `sublatticeMagSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum` | **Sublattice magnetization spectrum**: `┼ε_A^(3)` is diagonal with entry `sublatticeMagEigenvalueS A ╧Δ` on the diagonal and zero off it; hence a value `M` outside the diagonal spectrum gives `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M = έΛξ`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOp3_apply_diag/off_diag` and `magSubspaceS_eq_bot_of_not_in_spectrum`; lets the highest-weight argument conclude an attained sublattice magnetization is an actual basis eigenvalue (so `|M| έΚν s_A`) (Issue #3658, PR 7). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagSpectrum.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_of_mem` / `sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_mem_sublatticeMagSubspaceS_of_mem` | **Sublattice ladder shifts the sublattice grading**: `┼ε_A^+` maps `sublatticeMagSubspaceS A M έΗΤ A (M+1)` and `┼ε_A^-` maps it to `A (MέΙΤ1)`, from the grading commutators `[┼ε_A^(3), ┼ε_A^┬▒] = ┬▒┼ε_A^┬▒`. Sublattice analogue of `totalSpinSOpPlus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`; drives the termination of the highest-weight raising for `(┼ε_A)┬▓` (Issue #3658, PR 6). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeMagShift.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpPlus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice highest-weight Casimir relation**: if `┼ε_A^(3) w = m_A┬╖w` and `┼ε_A^+ w = 0`, then `(┼ε_A)┬▓ w = m_A(m_A+1)┬╖w`. From the sublattice Cartan identity `┼ε_A^- ┼ε_A^+ = (┼ε_A^1)┬▓+(┼ε_A^2)┬▓έΙΤ┼ε_A^(3)` (so `(┼ε_A)┬▓ = ┼ε_A^-┼ε_A^+ + ┼ε_A^(3) + (┼ε_A^(3))┬▓`); on a highest-weight `┼ε_A^(3)`-eigenvector the `┼ε_A^-┼ε_A^+` term vanishes. Sublattice analogue of the total highest-weight relation; scaffold (Issue #3658, PR 4) for the sublattice Casimir spectral max bound. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeHighestWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_mulVec_of_sublatticeSpinSOpMinus_eq_zero` | **Sublattice lowest-weight Casimir relation**: if `┼ε_A^(3) w = m_A┬╖w` and `┼ε_A^- w = 0`, then `(┼ε_A)┬▓ w = m_A(m_AέΙΤ1)┬╖w`. From the other sublattice Cartan identity `┼ε_A^+ ┼ε_A^- = (┼ε_A^1)┬▓+(┼ε_A^2)┬▓+┼ε_A^(3)` (so `(┼ε_A)┬▓ = ┼ε_A^+┼ε_A^- έΙΤ ┼ε_A^(3) + (┼ε_A^(3))┬▓`); on a lowest-weight vector the `┼ε_A^+┼ε_A^-` term vanishes. At `m_A = έΙΤs_A` this gives `(┼ε_A)┬▓ = s_A(s_A+1)` (max), so the all-down sublattice state realizes the maximal `(┼ε_A)┬▓` έΑΦ a building block of the minimal-total-spin state (Issue #3674). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/SublatticeLowestWeight.lean`) |
| `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_antiAligned_eq_max` / `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_complement_antiAligned_eq_max` (with `antiAlignedConfigS`) | **Anti-aligned state realizes the maximal sublattice Casimirs**: the basis config `antiAlignedConfigS A N` (all `A`-sites up `╧Δ=0`, all `┬υA`-sites down `╧Δ=N`) satisfies `(┼ε_A)┬▓ |╧ΔέΓΑέθσ = s_A(s_A+1) |╧ΔέΓΑέθσ` and `(┼ε_┬υA)┬▓ |╧ΔέΓΑέθσ = s_B(s_B+1) |╧ΔέΓΑέθσ` έΑΦ the `A`-block is the sublattice highest weight (#3663), the `┬υA`-block the sublattice lowest weight (#3681). Total magnetization `s_A έΙΤ s_B` (the extremal sector); projecting onto `(┼ε_tot)┬▓=predicted` yields the minimal-total-spin state (Issue #3674, PR 8). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23AntialignedJointEigenvector.lean`) |
| `tasaki23_extremal_highestWeight_totalCasimir_eq_predicted` | **Extremal-sector highest weight has the predicted total Casimir**: a vector in the extremal magnetization sector `M = min(|A|,|┬υA|)┬╖N` annihilated by `┼εέΒ║_tot` is a `(┼ε_tot)┬▓`-eigenvector at exactly `tasaki23PredictedCasimirValue A N` (the total highest-weight relation gives `m(m+1)` with `m = |s_AέΙΤs_B|`, via #3678). With the maximal sublattice Casimirs, such a highest-weight vector is the minimal-total-spin joint eigenstate; its existence (ClebschέΑΥGordan) is the last obligation (Issue #3674, PR 9). Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3, p. 42 (file `Quantum/SpinS/Theorem23ExtremalHighestWeight.lean`) |

Since `┼εέΒ╗_tot` commutes with all three Casimir operators (`totalSpinSSquared_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus`, `sublatticeSpinSquaredS_commute_totalSpinSOpMinus`) the eigenvalues are preserved, and lowering is non-vanishing because the predicted value differs from the sector's lowering-kernel value. The base sector `M = min(|A|,|┬υA|)┬╖N` re-derives #3709 in embedded form; the general sector is reached by induction on the distance from the base, lowering with `tasaki23_jointPredicted_lowering_succ` (#3714) and re-embedding via `totalSpinSOpMinus_mulVec_mem_magSubspaceS_of_mem`. | `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` / `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` | **Tasaki ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.3 truly-unconditional capstone for the bipartite toy coupling** (#3887 extension chain, Thm23-#3887.12έΑΥ.21, PR #3891): the canonical `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3` API is now the structural statement, dropping the vacuous-at-`N=1` `h_intermediate` hypothesis (`έΙΑ ╧Ε, έΙΑ x, έΙΔ z, A z έΚι A x έΙπ (╧Ε z).val < N`; unsatisfiable when `╧Ε έΚκ Fin.last 1`). The toy capstone `tasaki_2_5_theorem_2_3_bipartiteToy` proves the conclusion from `(1 έΚν N, 1 έΚν |A|, 1 έΚν |┬υA|)`, the genuine physical hypotheses, by chaining structural variants of the entire MLM / sector existence / Casimir / lower-bound stack: complex sector Marshall eigenvector (#3887.12), full-Hilbert lift (#3887.13), four-level Marshall-positive uniqueness chain (#3887.14), Theorem 2.2 bundled full (#3887.15), Theorem 2.3 sector existence wrapper (#3887.16), toy sector energy lower bound (#3887.17), per-sector toy ground state at predicted (#3887.19), final capstone (#3887.21). Proof: ╬ο, ┼ε┬│_tot ╬ο, ╬α ╬ο all lie in the GS eigenspace (`H` commutes with `┼ε┬│_tot` (#3741) and with the reflection `╬α` (#3745)). From `finrank έΚν 1` + ╬ο έΚι 0 and `Module.finrank_le_one_iff`, `┼ε┬│_tot ╬ο = ╬│ ╬ο` and `╬α ╬ο = ╬┤ ╬ο` for some scalars. `╬α┬▓ = 1` έθ╣ `╬┤┬▓ = 1` έθ╣ `╬┤ έΚι 0`. Apply the anticommutation `╬α ┼ε┬│_tot = -┼ε┬│_tot ╬α` (#3744) to ╬ο: get `╬┤╬│ έΑλ ╬ο = -╬│╬┤ έΑλ ╬ο` έθ╣ `2╬┤╬│ έΑλ ╬ο = 0` έθ╣ `╬┤╬│ = 0` έθ╣ `╬│ = 0` (since ╬┤ έΚι 0). Ladder-form decomposition: the matching transverse ladder product (`┼εέΒ╗_x ┼εέΒ║_y` or `┼εέΒ║_x ┼εέΒ╗_y` depending on direction) gives a strict positive real factor at both sites (`spinSOpPlus_apply_re_pos_of_raise`, `spinSOpMinus_apply_re_pos_of_lower`); the off-direction transverse, both parity hops, and `┼ε┬│┼ε┬│` vanish by Fin-index mismatch / off-diagonality; coefficient `(1 + ╬╗.re)/4 > 0` strict (from `╬╗.re > έΙΤ1`). | `dressedAxisSwapped_bond_re_neg_bipartite_x_of_raiseLower_witness`, `..._y_...` | **Strict negativity of the Marshall-dressed axis-swapped bond on a `RaiseLowerStepS` witness** (Tasaki ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3791): for a bipartite bond `{x, y}` (A-site at either `x` or `y`) under case (i) strict (`έΙΤ1 < ╬╗.re έΚν 1` real), the dressed bond entry `marshallSignS(╧Δ) ┬╖ marshallSignS(╧Δ') ┬╖ spinSDotXXZSwap x y ╬╗ ╧Δ' ╧Δ` has strict negative real part. The Marshall sign product equals `έΙΤ1` on any `┬▒1` move on the `A`-site (#3760), flipping the strict-positive bond entry (#3790) to strict-negative. The per-bond ingredient of the full dressed-`─ν'` strict negativity on a transverse step. Tasaki, Springer 2020, ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.4, p. 43 (file `Quantum/SpinS/DressedAxisSwapBondReStrictNeg.lean`) |

Strictness comes from the matching `(x, y)` bond by #3791; all other per-bond contributions (#3770's `_bipartite_x/y`) and the dressed single-ion (#3770's `dressed_singleIonAnisotropyS2_re_nonpos`) are non-positive. The double sum is closed via a `Finset.sum_neg_of_forall_nonpos_of_exists_neg` helper, applied nested over the outer and inner sums. This is the `hB_step` of `Matrix.isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos` for the transverse `RaiseLowerStepS` move in PR5 (parity-block PerronέΑΥFrobenius irreducibility). Parallels the transverse step (#3792) with the parity ladder product (`┼εέΒ╗┼εέΒ╗` for both-raised, `┼εέΒ║┼εέΒ║` for both-lowered) replacing the transverse ladder; coefficient `(1 έΙΤ ╬╗.re)/4 > 0` strict from `╬╗.re < 1`; the same nested `Finset.sum_neg_of_forall_nonpos_of_exists_neg` assembly. Bond contributions all vanish: either `x έΙΚ {a, b}` έθ╣ NOT agree-off-`{a, b}`; or `x έΙΙ {a, b}` έθ╣ no single ladder operator (`┼εέΒ║`, `┼εέΒ╗`, `┼ε┬│`) connects `┬▒2`-shifted Fin indices. Single-ion contribution: only `y = x` survives (off-`x` `╬┤`-product vanishes); `(┼ε┬▓)┬▓(i, j).re = έΙΤ(1/4) ┬╖ ((┼εέΒ║)┬▓ + (┼εέΒ╗)┬▓)(i, j).re < 0` strict (one of the two parity-square ladder products strict positive). Marshall sign at same-site even (`┬▒2`) shift `= +1` (no flip). The `hB_step` of `Matrix.isIrreducible_iff_exists_pow_pos` for the single-ion `┬▒2`-move. Hence `finrank έΕΓ (eig M ╬╜ έΛΥ ker(P=(-1)^p)) έΚν finrank έΕΓ (eig M.submatrix ╬╜)` for `p < 2`. The bridge for lifting the per-block PF bound (#3831) to the full eigenspace έΛΥ parity ker. | `matrix_similar_eigenspace_inter_finrank_eq` | **(g.3) similarity-invariant intersection eigenspace finrank** (Tasaki ┬π2.5 Theorem 2.4, Issue #3739, PR #3835): generalises `matrix_similar_eigenspace_finrank_eq` (#3746 family) to intersections with a second operator's eigenspace, provided the similarity `U` commutes with that operator `Q`. Given `U * Uinv = 1`, `Uinv * U = 1`, `H' = Uinv * H * U`, AND `Q * U = U * Q`, then `finrank έΕΓ (eig H' ╬╝ έΛΥ ker(Q=c)) = finrank έΕΓ (eig H ╬╝ έΛΥ ker(Q=c))`. The core technical step for transferring per-parity-block PF bounds from the dressed `─ν'_dressed` to the bare `─ν'`, since the Marshall sign diagonal `╬α_A` commutes with the diagonal `magParityDiagS P`. Key observation: the Marshall sign diagonal `╬α_A` and the magnetization parity diagonal `P = magParityDiagS` are both diagonal and hence commute, so the intersection `eig ─ν' ╬╝ έΛΥ ker(P=c)` is preserved by the Marshall similarity dressed έΗΤ bare. Result for `p < 2`: `έΙΔ ╬╜ : έΕζ, finrank έΕΓ (eig (axisSwappedAnisotropicHeisenbergS J lam D N) (╬╜ : έΕΓ) έΛΥ ker(P=(-1)^p)) έΚν 1`.
